The activity is

The activity is aimed at children from 4-6 years (dedicated zone), teens, families and groups (birthdays, stag/hen, seminars, schools outside school hours). Wide choice of packages: single session, multi-session passes, birthday deals with private room for cake and drinks. The ultimate bad-weather indoor activity in the valley, complementing the Grand Massif and XQV0Z resorts. Just-Jump non-slip socks are mandatory (sold on site, reusable).

The activity is exclusively winter and depends on snow (typically December to March). Ride prices ~€50-80/person, kennel visits ~€10-15/person, longer outings on quote. Booking essential, several weeks ahead in high season (Christmas, February). Wear very warm clothes (thick coat, gloves, hat, waterproof warm boots). Cancellation possible in case of insufficient snow or extreme weather.
The activity is free
The activity is generally accessible from age 13-15, with parental authorisation required for minors. ID is requested at sign-up.

The activity is open from age 14 and is a hit with groups of friends, stag/hen parties, teen and adult birthdays, corporate team-building events or couples looking for an original night out. The centre offers coached sessions of about 1h-1h30, lane privatisation and full birthday packages with cake and drinks. The venue also features a full games bar — billiards, foosball, electronic darts, board games — and a selection of local craft beers to extend the evening after your session.
The activity is open from age 14 at XQV0Z. Minors must be accompanied or have parental authorisation per the centre's conditions.

The activity is open to anyone in good health, from around 13-15 years (parental authorisation required for minors), weighing between ~45 and 110 kg depending on the operator. No previous experience is needed: a full briefing is given on site before the jump, equipment checked in front of you, then guided up onto the viaduct by the team. The jump itself lasts a few seconds, but the full experience (briefing, checks, climb, jump, descent, debrief, souvenir photo) takes around 1h30 to 2 hours.

The activity is seasonal (usually May to October depending on snow and weather) and very weather-dependent: wind, rain or storms may cause postponement. Advance booking is essential, especially during school holidays and summer weekends. Bring sportswear, closed shoes, and an ID card (minors: signed parental authorization). On site, plan 1-2 hours (briefing, kit, wait, jump, debriefing). Official site and 2026 prices to confirm via the XQV0Z tourist office or the operator.

The activity is suited to families with children (from around 8-10 depending on the cave), groups of friends, stag/hen parties, school groups or corporate team-building. No prior experience is needed for discovery outings. Trips run 2 to 4 hours including the approach walk, safety briefing, underground progression (crawling, easy down-climbs, sometimes short rope pitches) and return. For seasoned cavers, the bureau also offers introductions to vertical caving on rope.
The activity is suspended in strong wind, storms or rain. If closed, tickets are refunded or rescheduled. Check the day before with the resort.
The activity is weather-dependent.

The activity runs from mid-December to mid-March depending on snow. January and February are the most reliable months for fresh snow. In December and March, availability depends on the snow cover. Conditions are confirmed the day before.

The actual summit is a ten-minute walk north of this platform, on a limestone ridge that is exposed but secured by cables and barriers at exposed passages. From there, the view spans 180°: Lake XQV0Z below, Semnoz to the southwest, summits of the Bauges massif (Tournette, Dents de Lanfon, Arcalod) to the southeast, the Aravis range and Mont Blanc to the east (visible via Col des Aravis), and the Jura to the north. The ridge continues towards Mont Veyrier for those wishing to extend the hike.
The adjacent

The admission price for the discovery course at XQV0Z is €14 for an adult and €13 for a child. The main course costs €20 for an adult and €18 for a child. Group rate (from 8 people): -€2 per entry. Verified on 14 May 2026.

The adult admission price (15+) for the guided tour of the XQV0Z is €12. Child (5–14): €6. Free for children under 5. Gardens and terraces only ticket: €3. Group rate (10+ people): €10 per person. Verified 14 May 2026.

The adult entrance fee for XQV0Z (from 16 years) is €9. Child (4–15 years): €6. Free under 4 years. Reduced rate: groups (15+ people on reservation), persons with disabilities, hikers who walked or cycled from the valley. 15% of ticket price funds 2027 operations (closure for rehabilitation). Verified 14 May 2026.

The adult price for the XQV0Z is €15. Children 6–18 years: €8.50. Free for children under 6. Family Pass (2 adults + 2 children): €38.50. Student (under 26): €11. Season pass (unlimited entry April–October): €25. Verified 6 June 2026.
The adult rate for XQV0Z is €7. Child 6–16 years: €3.50. Adult group (min. 20 persons): €5.60. Free for under 6 and with Multi Pass Portes du Soleil or Pass XQV1Z France. Verified May 14, 2026.

The adult rate for the Advanced Course at XQV0Z is €27. Junior Course €22. Children's Course €17. Acro'Ninja, Hop Up Challenge and Acro'Filet €17 each. Sensory Forest €5 (free under 3). Treasure Hunt €10. Orienteering €6. Multi-activity pass available. Group rates on request. Verified 5 June 2026.
The adult rate for the main course at XQV0Z is €26. Youth under 16: €20. Children's course: €12 (1 round) or €17 (2 rounds). Group rates from 9 people: €22/adult, €17/youth. Verified May 14, 2026.

The adult wellness path starts at around €55-90 depending on day, duration and time slot. Aperitivo and snack are included. Treatments (massages etc.) are extra. Exact 2026 prices to confirm at qcterme.com.
The alpine-pasture trail at XQV0Z has 8 stations with educational panels, each covering an aspect of alpine-pasture life: cheese, herd, wildlife, flora.
The alpine-pasture trail at XQV0Z is a family walk of 6.5 km and 2 h 30 — accessible to regular walkers and to children from age 7–8.
The alpine-pasture trail at XQV0Z is about 6.5 km as a loop, with 330 m of climb, i.e. 2 h 30 to 3 h of walking at a relaxed pace.
The alpine-pasture trail at XQV0Z is freely accessible from May to October depending on snow cover. Mid-mountain trail — avoid in snow or thunderstorms. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The alpine-pasture trail at XQV0Z offers panoramas over the Vallée Verte, a pastoral valley typical of the Pre-Alps of Lake Geneva.
The alpine-pasture trail at XQV0Z starts behind the church, letting you discover the village and its heritage before or after the walk.
The alpine-pasture trail is a 6.5 km discovery loop at XQV0Z (Vallée Verte), inspired by Guy Chatiliez's book 'Alpage de mon enfance'. 8 stations with panels and QR codes on alpine-pasture life.
The alpine-pasture trail itself is entirely free. Guy Chatiliez's book 'Alpage de mon enfance' (sold at the town hall and TOs) is extra. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The alpine-pasture trail pays tribute to the book 'Alpage de mon enfance' (1977) by Guy Chatiliez, who recalls his childhood memories of the Vallée Verte alpine pastures.
The alpine-pasture trail starts behind the church at XQV0Z (74420). A 10-minute walk from the village centre along a gently descending lane.
The animals are in semi-freedom: it's important to respect the enclosures, not shout, and not feed the animals (feeding strictly controlled by ONF).
The animals are staged in reconstructed natural habitats — forests, alpine pastures, scree, mountain lakes — that bring them to life.
The annual fee and playing rates are set by the association and revised each season. For exact 2026 prices, contact the club directly.
The aquatic area is open to all (accompanied children). The spa area (sauna/hammam/jacuzzi) is generally reserved for adults from age 16.

The arboretum of the Jardins de l'Europe brings together around 250 trees and more than 1,000 shrubs: ginkgo biloba, Virginia tulip tree, Corsican pines, giant sequoias and many exotic species planted from 1863 to the designs of Henri Porreaux.
The architecture is typical of the late 13th century: a
The area is home to ibex, marmots, and chamois. Early morning offers the best observation chances. Maintain distance from wild animals.
The atmospheric Le Gallery Bar inside the complex is open to casino guests and non-players alike.
The base hosts training sessions of the club's competition section and several regional regattas during the season.

The base is generally open from May to September, peaking in July-August when swimming and catering run at full capacity. Site entry is modestly priced and access is easy by car from the A410 (XQV0Z exit), with a large free car park. For a great day, arrive early on a sunny weekend, bring sunscreen, a parasol and water shoes, and check the lifeguard times before leaving. Out of season, the site remains open for walks.
The base station is 5 minutes on foot from Chamonix SNCF station. Local bus shuttles run in season from main accommodation zones.

The beach combines a shaded grassy area with a sandy swimming zone supervised in July and August by lifeguards. An amphibious bathing chair (tiralo) is available from the lifeguards to facilitate access for people with reduced mobility. The beach has a dedicated swimming area for dogs (right side facing the lake), one of the rare Lake Geneva beaches to allow this.

The beach comprises a grass area shaded by century-old chestnut trees, popular for picnics and relaxation, and lake access with a pebble and gravel bottom. Swimming is supervised from July 1 to August 31, 10:30 am to 7 pm, with a rescue post and lifeguards on duty. In summer, several thousand walkers and swimmers visit this very family-friendly site daily.
The beach faces an exceptional panorama: the
The beach features a designated

The beach has a wild, preserved feel and is one of the quietest spots in the Bas-Chablais, far less busy than nearby Anthy, Corzent or XQV0Z. The restaurants of Port de Séchex are within walking distance, as is the Bowling de XQV1Z in the village. Bathing is free and not supervised.
The beach has been

The beach has two swimming zones: a shallow paddling pool for children with games, and a deeper zone open to the lake. The bottom is made of small pebbles. The concrete installations facilitate water access and evoke natural swimming pools by the seaside. The beach is equipped with fixed and floating jetties, diving boards, several modern sanitary facilities with showers and changing rooms, as well as a large snack bar with indoor and outdoor seating offering fine views over the lake.
The beach holds the

The beach is accessible year-round and free. The best period for swimming is June to September, with water reaching 20–24°C in July–August. During high season (27 June–31 August), the beach is paid and supervised from 10am to 7pm, with heavy crowds in sunny afternoons — prefer early morning or evening. Off-season (April–May, September–October), access is free, the atmosphere more peaceful, but water cooler (15–18°C). In winter and spring, the beach remains open for lakeside walks, but without supervision or swimming possible.

The beach is accessible year-round, but the ideal bathing season runs from June to September, with water reaching 22 to 25°C in July–August. Supervised swimming operates from 1 July to 30 August. Spring and autumn offer a quieter atmosphere, ideal for walks on the sand and observing dune flora. In winter, the beach becomes a popular birdwatching site, with the calm waters of the Vion stream hosting diverse lakeside wildlife. Summer events (concerts, sand festival, artisan market) are in full swing in July and August. Avoid busy weekends during hot periods in peak season for a more pleasant experience.

The beach is accessible year-round, but the ideal period runs May to September for pleasant temperatures and water activities. July and August offer supervised swimming and 22–24 °C water, but are also peak season: favour mornings and evenings to avoid crowds. In June and September, water is cooler but the beach is noticeably quieter. Out of season, the site remains a popular walking destination, with beautiful light on Lake XQV0Z and the Alps in the background.

The beach is accessible year-round. High tourism season runs June to September, with supervised swimming only in July and August. The site is very busy on summer weekends and during nautical events (Nautical Festival in June). In winter, waterfront saunas operate even in cold weather. Spring (April–May) and autumn (September–October) offer a quieter atmosphere with clear views of Lake Geneva and the Alps.

The beach is distinguished by its very gentle slope extending several tens of metres, allowing visitors to walk up to a hundred metres with water at waist height. Summer water temperatures reach around 25°C from June to September, making it a preferred bathing location for families. The setting offers panoramic views of Dent d'Oche and the Swiss coast, with pine forest in the background. The beach has held Blue Flag status since 2018, a label guaranteeing water quality and environmental site management.

The beach is equipped with a 3-level diving board (5 metres high) accessible by swimming from pontoons, a paddling pool and play area for young children, a beach-volleyball court and petanque pitch. A restaurant and snack bar are on site. Immediately nearby: XQV0Z marina, the Reed Path trail, and the Lake XQV1Z cycle route (9 km to XQV2Z).
The beach is officially supervised by lifeguards in July and August, with a swimming area marked out by buoys. The local water-sports club next door offers paddle, kayak and catamaran hire, and the
The beach is open year-round but visits vary by season.

The beach is pleasant year-round. In summer, visit early morning for clear water and quiet, or after 5 p.m. when crowds decrease. Off-season, XQV0Z is ideal for picnicking, walking, or sports by the lake.

The beach is shaded by numerous trees, making it particularly popular with families with young children in summer. It is supervised by lifeguards from the start of summer school holidays until August 31, from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m. Outside the season, the beach remains open for walks, picnics, sports, or simply enjoying the view of the lake and surrounding mountains.

The beach offers a wide sand-and-lawn area with a floating pontoon in summer. The Club Nautique XQV0Z, next door, hires out paddleboards, kayaks and sailing boats. The beach is also the western terminus of the Voie verte du XQV1Z, a great cycling route along the lake.
The beach was awarded the

The best period for XQV0Z extends from June to October. July and August offer the most stable conditions. September and early October are ideal for solitude and light. Late May and early June: persistent snowfields. In winter: ski touring or snowshoeing only.

The best period for XQV0Z is mid-July to late August. The lake is frozen or snow-covered for approximately 9 months per year. In early season (June-early July), snow patches may remain on the trail. Check conditions with La Chamoniarde before departing.

The best period for swimming is June through September, with water temperatures between 68 and 75°F. July–August: maximum crowds, arrive before 11 a.m. to find a spot on the lawn. Active lifeguard supervision 11 a.m.–7 p.m. May and September: reasonable attendance, water still cool but pleasant on sunny days. Off-season (October–April): the beach becomes a favorite jogging and walking destination with clear mountain views. Avoid sunny weekends in July–August if seeking peace and quiet.

The best period to hike XQV0Z extends from June to October, when trails are snow-free and passable. July and August offer the most stable conditions, but also the highest visitor numbers. Late May and early June still have persistent snowfields on high slopes. September and early October offer exceptional light and fewer crowds, but days shorten and mountain refuges close. In winter, XQV1Z is accessible by ski touring or snowshoes, but requires good terrain knowledge and understanding of snow conditions. The summit is very exposed to lightning during storms: descend immediately if weather deteriorates.

The best period to visit the XQV0Z is April to October, with preference for May, June and September offering pleasant weather and moderate crowds. Summer provides the best conditions for panoramic views over Lake XQV1Z.
The best period to visit the XQV0Z is June to September. In May-June, the waterfall is particularly powerful due to snow melt. July-August offer the best weather conditions for hiking.

The best periods for XQV0Z are spring (April–June) and autumn (September–October). Visitor numbers are calmer than in summer, temperatures are pleasant and the lake view is often clear. Avoid winter (November–March) without suitable equipment: the ridge can be iced.

The best season depends on your interests: winter (November–March) for wintering waterfowl; spring (April–May) for wader and heron migration; autumn (September–October) for peace and colours. Summer is busier.
The best season for XQV0Z is May to October. Summer: strong flow, busy site. Spring/autumn: fewer crowds, soft light. Winter: access depends on snow cover.

The best season for XQV0Z is spring (April–May) for maximal flow with snowmelt, or summer (June–September) for cool forest cover and canyoning. In winter, the cascade can be frozen, spectacular but access is slippery.
The best season for the XQV0Z is May to October, when the trail is clear. Flow is maximum in spring and early summer thanks to snowmelt.

The best starting point to discover the Thiou is the Pont des Amours, at the outflow of Lake XQV0Z on the Pâquier side: from there you can head up the canal du Vassé and the Port arm through the old town.
The best time is mid-July to mid-August, when the park is open continuously every day and conditions are optimal. The shaded forest setting provides ideal comfort even on hot days.
The best time to see the waterfalls at XQV0Z is mid-May to end June, during snowmelt: over 30 waterfalls then cascade down the cliffs with spectacular flow.

The best time to swim at XQV0Z is June to September, with 25°C water and supervised swimming in July–August. Spring and autumn are ideal for enjoying calm surroundings and observing the unique dune flora.

The best time to visit XQV0Z runs from April to November, with full opening in July and August (daily 11am–7pm). The park is also open on weekends and Wednesdays in April and November, as well as daily during Easter and All Saints holidays. In bad weather (heavy rain, strong wind), the park may close temporarily for safety reasons — it is advisable to contact the establishment before you travel. Summer mornings are quieter and offer ideal conditions to enjoy the coolness of the forest.
The birdwatching observatory at Grande Corne meadow allows observation of nesting and migratory birds (tufted ducks, herons, egrets, sandpipers) while ensuring their tranquility.
The board-game bar and quiz room are accessible. The escape rooms include a few steps — call +33 4 50 05 49 63 to check your specific case.
The book 'Alpage de mon enfance' by Guy Chatiliez is on sale at the XQV0Z town hall and the XQV1Z and XQV2Z Tourist Offices — useful to prepare or extend the alpine-pasture trail.

The booklet for the Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z trail game is available free of charge at the Chamonix Valley Tourist Offices: XQV1Z, Chamonix, Argentière, XQV2Z, XQV3Z. It can also be downloaded on chamonix.com.

The booklet for the Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z trail game is available free of charge at the Chamonix Valley Tourist Offices: XQV1Z, Chamonix, XQV2Z, Argentière, XQV3Z. It can also be downloaded on chamonix.com.

The bouldering wall covers all profiles — slabs, vertical, overhangs — with a hold variety suited to every level, from beginners discovering the sport to advanced climbers working on movement and reading. The

The boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is a stone dated 1579, set at the foot of the steps near the church of XQV0Z. It is today the only tangible remnant of the seigneurial Château du Vuache, which dominated the village in the Middle Ages.
The boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is accessible 24/7, year-round, with free access. Daytime is preferable to read the 1579 date clearly. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is in the heart of the village of XQV0Z (74520), at the foot of the steps immediately next to the church. GPS: 46.101° N · 5.927° E.
The building stands on the Boissy hillside at
The building, sober and harmonious in its classical Grand Siècle style, is today a cultural hub for the Chablais. It houses both the
The cable car is closed from 1 November to 31 March. The trails remain open on foot or with snowshoes depending on snow cover — beware of ice on the rocks.

The cascade is accessible by hiking trail in approximately 30 minutes from the Burzier parking area. It can also be approached through a Via Corda organized by the XQV0Z Guides Bureau (a paid and supervised activity, distinct from free access to the natural site). The cascade cliff is also a renowned climbing site in XQV1Z, with routes ranging from 5c to 8c on gray and orange limestone, facing east.

The cascade is accessible in 5 to 10 minutes' walk from the Parc Biolley parking area in Chedde, via a maintained trail leading to an observation platform. A second unmarked trail allows visitors to approach even closer to the waterfall. The flow is particularly spectacular after heavy rain or in spring during snowmelt.

The cascade is accessible year-round. Spring (April–June) is the most spectacular period due to snowmelt, which swells the Ugine torrent flow and makes the heart shape particularly visible. Summer (July–August) offers pleasant walking conditions, but flow may be less impressive late in the dry season. After heavy autumn rains (September–October), the waters regain their power. In winter, the trail remains passable but can be slippery; partially frozen waters create ice sculptures. The site is less crowded early morning and weekdays, ideal times for photographing rainbows in the mist.

The cascade is visible year-round from the Arve valley. To approach it on foot, the recommended period runs from May 1 to October 15, when the trail is clear of snow. Cascade flow is maximum in spring (snowmelt) and early summer, offering a more impressive sight. Autumn is also pleasant, with broadleaf forest in warm colors and quieter crowds. In winter, the trail may be snow-covered or icy, making access difficult without proper equipment. Climbing on the cliff is practicable from April to October; some routes are sheltered and climbable in light rain.

The casino is part of the Évian Resort group (formerly Royal Resort), a key heritage destination on the French side of Lake Geneva. The slot floor and table games occupy elegant Belle-Époque rooms that double as venues for events and entertainment throughout the year.
The castle comprised a main body of around 30 m by 13 m and a southern tower about 14 m high, built in the mountain fortification tradition:
The castle had a remarkable historical function: it controlled a
The castle had an eventful history:
The castle had an eventful history: converted into the
The castle hosts weddings, private receptions, corporate seminars, and family events by reservation year-round. No regular public events. Contact the establishment directly for personalized quotes.
The castle houses private collections related to Saint Francis of Sales and Camille di Cavour, visible during private events held on site.
The castle is available for weddings, family receptions, and celebrations. Historic halls, 5-hectare park, and bespoke services for unforgettable events.
The castle is now home to the offices of the

The castle is open from April to early November, with peak season in July–August (daily, theatrical tours on weekends). Spring (April–June) and autumn (September–October) offer fewer crowds and picturesque settings, with blooming gardens in spring and autumn colours in October. School holidays (Easter, All Saints') benefit from exceptional daily openings. In summer, the view of the lake from the terraces is sublime in the late afternoon (last entry 6:30 pm). The castle is closed mid-November to end March, except for the "Christmas Castle" event in December.
The castle now houses the

The castle of XQV0Z was built in the 15th century by the marquises of XQV1Z in Gothic style, enlarged until the 17th century then acquired by the Gerbais de Sonnaz family in 1696. It was burnt down by the SS on the night of 25 to 26 December 1943, during the Christmas 1943 massacre that cost the lives of 32 people.
The castle offers luxurious rooms and suites arranged in historic spaces, with period furnishings and modern comfort. High-end accommodation for a unique stay.
The castle overlooks the Thiou and offers a complementary reading of XQV0Z's heritage.
The castle preserves architectural elements from the Middle Ages (keep, towers from the 12th and 13th centuries), the Baroque period, and the 19th century. It was the residence of
The castle was
The castle was built by the lords of Langin as a fortified hunting residence, during the expansion phase that peaked in the 15th–16th centuries. In
The castle, built in the early
The catalogue includes iconic circuits (Nürburgring, Spa, Monaco) and a wide car range (GT, F1, rally). Staff advice on site.
The cave displays a rich set of calcite formations — stalactites, stalagmites, draperies, rimstones — typical of Alpine karst. Reading the underground landscape with the guide.

The cave has been open to the public since 1906, making it the village's first tourist attraction. It represents the ancient underground course of the torrent and collects water that infiltrates the karst network in the Bauges mountains. The galleries display the circular profile characteristic of pressure conduits, illustrating the geological concept of the "keyhole cave". The guided tour lasts approximately 40 minutes at a constant temperature of 10°C, allowing visitors to discover the spectacular effects of erosion on limestone.
The cave is unsited. Independent visiting is only realistic for self-sufficient confirmed cavers, properly equipped and accompanied. Flood risk must be monitored.
The centre combines a swimming pool, kids' pool, massage jets, plus a wellness area with sauna, hammam, jacuzzi and relaxation zone.

The centre covers all main disciplines: sailing (Optimist, dinghy), towed wakeboard, water-skiing, stand-up paddle (SUP) and kayak. The flat water of the early morning makes the spot particularly suited to technical disciplines and beginner courses, while the southern end of the lake gives easy onshore access via the Voie verte.

The centre covers all watersports: sailing (Optimist, Laser, catamaran), rowing, kayak and stand-up paddle (SUP). Children's, teenage and adult courses run through the summer season, and equipment is also available to rent. The Marquisats beach is open to the public and offers direct access to Lake XQV0Z from the city centre.
The centre has a 25m six-lane sports pool, a fun pool with current river and massage jets, a paddling pool for toddlers and water slides.
The centre is in XQV0Z (74100), right next to XQV1Z, 15 minutes from Geneva via the Mon-Idée or Moillesulaz border crossings, and 45 minutes from XQV2Z on the A41.
The centre is open to children

The centre is open year-round with different time slots depending on day and period (schools, clubs, public). The weekly schedule is published on the XQV0Z town-hall website. An annual maintenance closure usually takes place in summer or autumn.

The centre is open year-round, with extended hours during school holidays. Public prices are around €5-8 for adults and €3-5 for children; 10-entry cards, monthly/annual passes and reduced rates are available. Swim caps compulsory, soapy shower before swimming. Free parking, bus access from Évian and Thonon. Perfect for an indoor activity alongside a day by the lake.

The centre is open year-round. Outside Zone A school holidays: Mon-Tue-Thu-Fri 8:30 am-8 pm (25 m pool closed to the public 1:45-4:15 pm for schools), Wed 10:30 am-8 pm, Sat 9 am-12 pm & 2 pm-6 pm, Sun 9 am-12:15 pm. Extended hours during holidays. Last admission 45 min before closing. Check current hours on the official site.
The centre is served by CCPEVA bus lines from Évian and Thonon. The Lake Geneva cycle greenway runs nearby — ideal by bike from Évian-Amphion.
The centre lets
The centre offers aquabike, aquagym, aquafitness, baby swimming, aquatic garden, swimming lessons for children and adults, and aquaphobia sessions. Swimming schools 1st semester 25/26.

The centre offers three complementary water zones: a children's area (heated paddling pool at 32 °C, water tree, slides, suspended water games), a relaxation pool (gentle lazy river bordered by tropical trees at 30 °C, 1.5 m swimming pool, aquatic climbing wall), and a thrills zone (Slidewinder, a 10 m high tube slide half-pipe style). An outdoor hot tub heated to 33 °C offers breathtaking views of the Portes du Soleil peaks.

The centre suits beginners (group intro lessons, racquet rental) as well as experienced players (free booking, tournaments, intermediate/advanced levels). Private lessons with a coach, children's/teen camps during school holidays, corporate events and birthdays are also available. Padel is one of the fastest-growing sports, accessible and fun, ideal for friend groups.

The centre targets the French-Swiss border area: families (usually from age 8), groups of friends, stag/hen parties, business seminars and schools outside school hours. Packages range from a single game to bundles of 2 or 3 consecutive games at reduced per-game rates. Children's birthday parties are a specialty: private room subject to availability, cake and drinks possible, personalised invitations and souvenir photo. The experience is intense but contactless (IR technology) — closed shoes and comfortable clothing recommended.

The centre welcomes families (usually from age 7-8 depending on the child's size), friends, stag/hen parties, business seminars and school groups outside school hours. Packages range from a single game to bundles of 2 or 3 consecutive games at reduced per-game rates. Children's birthday parties are the specialty: private reception room subject to availability, cake and drinks possible, personalised invitations. The experience is intense but contactless (IR technology) — closed shoes and comfortable clothing recommended.

The centre welcomes serious swimmers (lanes reserved early morning and at certain times), families with children (fun area, paddling pool, slides), seniors (aquagym, dedicated slots) and school groups. Programme of coached activities: aquagym, aquabike, swimming school, parent-and-baby classes, water garden. The wellness area (sauna, hammam, sensory showers) is available with an extra fee and open to over-16s. Lifeguards are present at all times around the pools.
The centrepiece is a
The château is private property, not open to the public, but is easily visible from the surrounding paths around XQV0Z. Listed as a Monument Historique in 1944.
The château is privately owned and not open to the public, but is clearly visible from the road through XQV0Z.
The château preserves several remarkable decorative features:
The cinema is in central XQV0Z: plan for a nearby public car park (Bonlieu, Hôtel de Ville, Courier). Public transport (Sibra) or the cycle greenway is also a good option.

The cinema is located in XQV0Z (74130), sub-prefecture of the XQV1Z, 25 km from XQV2Z and 35 km from Geneva. Tickets are available on site and online (official site or partner platforms such as AlloCiné) for quick booking. Parking is usually available nearby and screens are wheelchair accessible. Concession stand on site.

The cinema is located in XQV0Z (74700), at the foot of the XQV1Z massif and very close to the resorts of XQV2Z, Cordon, Saint-Gervais and XQV3Z. Tickets are available on site and online (official site or partner platforms such as AlloCiné) for quick booking. Parking is usually available nearby and screens are wheelchair accessible. Concession stand on site.

The cinema joins national operations (Fête du Cinéma in late June/early July, Printemps du Cinéma in March, Rentrée du Cinéma in September) and hosts occasional previews, talks with directors and pop-up cine-clubs. Programming changes every Wednesday — see the official site.

The cinema joins national operations (Fête du Cinéma in late June/early July, Printemps du Cinéma in March, Rentrée du Cinéma in September) and hosts occasional previews, talks with directors and relays. Programming changes every Wednesday — see the official site.

The cinema takes part in national operations: Fête du Cinéma (June/July), Printemps du Cinéma (March), Rentrée du Cinéma (September). Previews, talkback screenings, family sessions and occasional film clubs throughout the year.

The cinema takes part in national operations: Fête du Cinéma (June/July), Printemps du Cinéma (March), Rentrée du Cinéma (September). Previews, talks with directors, family screenings, opera/concert relays.

The cirque is spectacular year-round, with very different atmospheres depending on the season. May–June is the peak period: over 30 waterfalls fed by snowmelt cascade down the cliffs with impressive flow, set among colourful vegetation. July–August: high visitor numbers, heat, fewer waterfalls (about a dozen persist). September–October: low crowds, autumn colours, snow-free peaks, ideal for wildlife watching. December–March: cross-country domain and snowshoeing, frozen waterfalls, monochrome landscape. Check the webcam before you go to verify conditions (haut-giffre.fr).

The cirque's rocks are Jurassic limestone, heavily fractured. High rainfall intensifies erosion: a major rockfall occurred in 1602 (burying several hamlets, commemorated by a chapel), then in 2003 (300,000 m³) and September 2025 (35,000 m³ at Pas Noir), leading to temporary closure of the Fond de la Combe sector by municipal order.
The classic package is 3 hours, ideal to enjoy the full circuit: indoor and outdoor pools, saunas, hammams, herbal tea room and sensory experiences. Allow time to relax fully.

The classic route departs from Bois Brûlé parking (Villaz) via the Grand Montoir (1 h 45, equipped with cables) and descends via the Petit Montoir (easier). For a short access, depart from L'Anglettaz chalet (Aviernoz): 1 h 15 ascent, 382 m elevation gain.
The classic start is at

The climb to the XQV0Z from Morsulaz is short but steep (significant average gradient). The route remains accessible to families used to mountain walking. The gentler Bété trail is currently closed by municipal order.

The closest car park to the Jardins de l'Europe is the Hôtel de Ville (underground, paid), 200 m away. The Bonlieu car park is 250 m away. XQV0Z city centre is also accessible by train (SNCF station 700 m away) and SIBRA bus.
The club also offers

The club covers both disciplines: karting on its own circuit and motocross on dedicated land. It welcomes members for training and stages, and is a niche option in the XQV0Z area for those who want a club-based motorsport experience rather than a one-off paying activity.
The club generally lends starter gear for first lessons and trials. Ask at reception.
The club has French billiards (carom) and American (pool) tables. Depending on the slots, other disciplines like snooker may also be played.
The club has a
The club has a 'coastal rowing' section dedicated to more stable boats, ideal for long-distance trips on Lake Geneva.
The club is an association: access is mostly reserved for members. Discovery slots or occasional rentals may be offered depending on availability. Contact the club before travelling.

The club is open to adults and teenagers wishing to practise billiards regularly, as well as occasional players interested in a discovery or hourly table rental (limited slots, subject to club availability). Several tables allow small groups to play in parallel. Coaches and reference members run discovery sessions in French billiards for newcomers, and the team also supervises in-house tournaments and regional inter-club meetings.
The club mainly opens in the evening (weekdays and weekends) for training and matches. Exact hours vary by sports season and are available on the club's site.

The club offers several formats: intro discovery sessions for visitors (1-2h, gear included), regular classes for members (weekly training), holiday camps for young people, internal competitions and FFTA competition participation for competitors. Open from around age 8 depending on strength and height.
The col is also a

The col is generally open from early May to mid-November. In 2026, it opened on 6 May at 2 pm and will close around 18 November depending on snow conditions. Check inforoute74 for the current road state.
The col itself is accessible by car without difficulty. The panoramic terrace and restaurant are accessible. The hiking trails are not adapted for wheelchairs.

The collection brings together a variety of aircraft, helicopters, gliders and aviation pieces under restoration or on display, including a recently acquired Dassault Mirage IIIE n°571. Volunteers — retired pilots, mechanics, engineers — restore the machines.
The collection covers the 1900s to today:
The collection holds more than

The collection is eclectic and regularly refreshed: vintage pinball machines from 1980s-1990s (Bally, Williams) and modern (Stern Pinball, Jersey Jack Pinball, Spooky Pinball). Up-to-date catalogue on artofpinball.fr.
The collection route presents
The collection traces the evolution of
The collection was
The complete loop around XQV0Z takes 45 minutes to 1 hour of easy walking (2.7 km). The trail is flat and well-marked, suitable for families.
The complex is located at
The complex offers

The complex offers direct access to the Lake Geneva shoreline, so swimmers can switch between the pool and open-water bathing in the lake itself, with a view across to the Vaud Alps on the Swiss side.
The complex offers padel (the fast-growing sport since 2020), tennis (indoor and outdoor depending on season) and squash. The three most popular racket sports are available on one site.
The complex welcomes

The concept suits families with children (selection from age 5-6) as well as friend groups, stag/hen, birthdays (children, teens, adults), after-work drinks and team-building. Access to the game library is a small flat fee (~€3-5/person) giving unlimited play all evening. Drinks (beer, wine, softs, snacks, boards) are extra.
The course combines sport and walking — good shoes, water, cap recommended.
The course is in XQV0Z resort. Accessible by car (resort parking), by Lihsa bus from XQV1Z, or by bike via the XQV2Z-XQV3Z cycle route.
The course is open all day during summer. No ticket required.
The courses are graded for every age and size:

The covered rink is used year-round for hockey, figure skating, public sessions, lessons and training camps. The outdoor rink, opened in the winter season (typically December to March), offers a unique open-air atmosphere facing the Aiguilles de Chamonix.
The deck of the XQV0Z is secured but the sense of emptiness is strong, including through the wooden planks. The panoramic walkways are not advised in case of vertigo.
The delta is 10 min by car from central Thonon: pair Château de Ripaille, lakeside and delta in one day.

The discovery course at XQV0Z is accessible from age 5 with a minimum height of 1.10 m. The main course requires a minimum height of 1.40 m. Children under 18 must be accompanied by an adult on the courses.

The domain is accessible year-round. Spring (April–May) offers abundant meadow flowering and the song of migratory birds. Summer (June–September) is ideal for family walks with free guided tours and access to the lake shores. Autumn (October–November) reveals the colours of the chestnut trees and golden light over Lake XQV0Z. Winter is quieter, conducive to nature observation and solitary walks. Trails may be muddy after rain; bring sturdy shoes at all times.
The du Mont via ferrata, within the protected Cirque du Fer-à-Cheval.
The establishment exclusively uses
The estate is home to
The estate now houses the
The estate suits both
The exact meeting point is given at booking confirmation. It's usually on the XQV0Z ski area, accessible by car from the village. Snow equipment mandatory in winter.
The experience is designed as a full day

The experience is designed for families, suitable for children from 3–4 with an adult. The trail is easy and downhill, with no technical difficulty. The route is part of XQV0Z's summer activity package and the "Pass Découverte" combining foot-passenger lifts and several summer activities.
The exterior view of the XQV0Z is freely accessible year-round, 24/7, from the road and the local paths. No scheduled interior visit. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The external view of the XQV0Z is free year-round. The CCBC offices inside the château are open on weekdays, but without tourist visits. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The external view of the XQV0Z is free. There is no regular tourist interior visit (CCBC headquarters). Verified on 10 June 2026.
The facility has
The family loop of the Smugglers' Audio Trail is 4 km with about 200 m of climb, walked in around 2 hrs 30 with listening stops.
The family loop starts at
The famous Pont des Amours, spanning the Vassé canal, starts from the gardens and offers the postcard shot of XQV0Z with a view of the bay.
The farm is home to
The farm is open
The fee is a time-based pass: once inside, you play unlimited on all available machines for the chosen duration (1h, 2h or free evening). No extra tokens. Prices from ~€12-20/person.
The flight is rescheduled if conditions are unsafe (strong wind, rain, storms). The pilot decides the previous evening or the same morning. Flights are usually rescheduled rather than refunded.
The footpath alongside the via ferrata continues to the Pierre à Bérard refuge (2 h from the buvette), in the heart of the Aiguilles Rouges nature reserve.
The format suits families with primary-school children, school outings and nature clubs. The trail is doable in any season but spring and early summer are richest for wildlife observation.

The fortress originally belonged to the Ternier family, an influential lineage that sometimes rivalled the Counts of Geneva. The castle was then ceded to the Counts and became a comital residence and administrative seat — hence the name "Comtal". Not far away stood another fortification,
The fossil sand dunes, created by wind on the lake shores until the 18th century, constitute a rare geological curiosity. Marked trail through the woods.
The foundry has cast
The full Boucles du Coteau network in XQV0Z totals about 5.5 km and 200 m of positive elevation. The terrain is mostly shaded woodland, with no technical difficulty.

The full collection counts over 600 objects, mostly 17th-18th c.; about fifty are permanently shown: polychrome wood sculptures (saints, Virgins, angels), silverware (chalices, ciboria), and liturgical ornaments. Reduced price; opening hours vary with the season.
The game master follows the game via camera and mic and can provide hints on request or if you're stuck for too long. The aim is for you to fully enjoy the experience, not to remain stuck.
The game master follows the game via camera and mic and can provide hints on request or if you're stuck. The aim is for you to fully enjoy the experience.
The game session in the room lasts 60 minutes, plus around 15 minutes of briefing before and debriefing/souvenir photo after. Plan 1h30 on site.
The games bar (pool, foosball, darts, board games) and the craft-beer selection make it easy to extend the evening on site.
The garden displays more than
The garden evolves across the season (April to October): roses in spring/summer, autumn colours at the end of the season.
The garden has no formal ban, but dogs must be leashed to respect the plants and other visitors.

The garden is divided into two sections: the first includes an alpine meadow (a nod to the mountainous environment), a woodland of shade plants, a Renaissance-inspired weaving pattern, and a hedge cloister. The second section, below, houses the chambers of greenery and the five sensory gardens. Each visitor receives an explanatory map at the entrance, and a discovery quiz is offered to children to learn while having fun. Free guided tours are organized during the season (schedule on the official website). XQV0Z village itself is ranked among France's

The garden is open from April through early October, with annual closure from November through mid-April. Spring (April–May) offers apple tree blossoms, tree peonies, akebia, and cornflowers. Summer (June–August) is peak season: high visitor numbers, all flower beds in full bloom, fragrances of roses and aromatic plants at their peak. Autumn (September–early October) is quieter, with changing colors (crape myrtle, autumn foliage, asters). The visit is pleasant throughout the season, but gardeners recommend mornings to enjoy the dew and fragrances. On weekdays outside school holidays, the garden is less crowded.
The garden is open mid-April to mid-October,

The garden occasionally organizes themed guided tours (gardening techniques, medicinal plants, garden history), a spring plant market, and children's workshops in summer. Program and dates on the official website or by contacting the team. The garden regularly hosts television programs (France 5 "Silence, ça pousse") and photographers for features.
The garden offers close views of the lake, with the estate opening onto the water. An exceptional setting for a contemplative walk.
The gardens extend the Pâquier and the Champ de Mars: a several-kilometre running or walking loop along the lake, flat and accessible.
The gondola serves the Portes du XQV0Z ski area in winter. Meg'Bus shuttle from XQV1Z centre. Ski pass not included.
The greenway leads to the
The guided tour lasts about 1h30 to 2 hours, plus boat rides (~10 min each way). Plan around 2h30 on site.
The guided tour of XQV0Z lasts approximately 40 minutes. The exterior waterfall circuit takes 20 to 30 minutes. With the mill exhibition and time on-site, plan 1.5 to 2 hours for the entire site.
The guided tour of the Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm lasts about 1 hr 30, covering the cuzna, the pèle, the cowshed, the hayloft and the outbuildings; bread-oven event included in summer.
The guided tour of the Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z lasts about 1 hour, led by a volunteer of the Muséâm association.
The guided tour of the XQV0Z takes approximately 45 minutes. Allow 1–2 hours in total including exploration of the gardens, terraces and vegetable garden, and a break at the gift shop or food truck.
The gym offers
The gym welcomes climbers of all levels, from the curious first-timer to the experienced climber prepping for outdoor crags.
The heart silhouette created by the division of waters is particularly photogenic in spring and after heavy rain, when flow is high and the shape clearly visible.
The high-stakes room of the Casino of Saint-Julien includes 2 blackjack tables, 3 traditional English roulette wheels, 4 Texas Hold'em poker tables and 15 electronic English roulette wheels.
The highlight of the visit is the
The hike from XQV0Z or from the Ajon alpine pasture crosses

The hike to XQV0Z is moderately difficult. The final cliff-side section is exposed, secured by barriers and chains, but the ground can be slippery. Good hiking boots mandatory. Children must be held. Not recommended during rain.

The historic cinema of XQV0Z nestled in the valley, XQV1Z combines arthouse and repertory. Arthouse films, classics and mountain-themed sessions (Mountain Film Festival) sit alongside mainstream releases.

The historical trail of XQV0Z takes 1 hour to walk. A complete visit (monument, trail, exhibition, Maquis Memory building) takes approximately 2 hours. For sports activities (cross-country skiing, snowshoeing, mountain hiking), allow half a day to a full day.

The history of the pass is closely linked to the tourist development of XQV0Z: the opening of the road in 1902 marked the beginning of the resort's expansion. Today, the XQV1Z is a must for mountain lovers, hikers and touring cyclists. The alpine depression where the chapel stands is framed by the cornices of the Etale and the spectacular opening of the Porte des Aravis to the north.
The iconic summit of the Parmelan plateau, a vast limestone balcony at 1,832 m offering panoramic views over the XQV0Z basin, the Aravis and the XQV1Z massif.

The ideal period runs from May to October. In May, witness the transhumance of XQV0Z cattle, a festive event. Summer offers stable weather and verdant alpine pastures. In winter, access is possible on snowshoes for experienced hikers.

The ideal season for Col des Pitons runs May through October, when the road is fully snow-cleared and accessible by car and bike. In winter (November–March), the road closes roughly 1.5 km before the pass (from Grotte du Diable onward). Cyclists favor June through September for climbs, with peak traffic on sunny weekends. Hikers prefer spring (May–June) and autumn (September–October) to avoid heat and enjoy autumn colors. Note: On July 7, 2026, the road is closed to cars in the morning (9am-12pm) for "XQV0Z Au Sommet" (cyclists only). Check the Department website before traveling that day.
The inaugural exhibition
The inner courtyard is the marvel of the XQV0Z:
The instructor takes photos and videos during the outing, sent digitally afterwards. Perfect souvenir to share.
The interpretation trail around the XQV0Z takes about one hour. Geopark panels along the route tell the story of the lake's formation and the submerged hamlets.
The journey is made in

The jump is open to anyone in good health, weighing between ~45 and 110 kg depending on the operator. Excluded: pregnant women, cardiac issues, serious back problems, recent surgery. If unsure, consult your doctor.

The karting is located at the XQV0Z leisure center, by the lake between XQV1Z and Chamonix. Exceptional natural environment to complete your day: swimming, picnic, hiking. 20 min from Chamonix, 15 min from XQV2Z and Saint-Gervais.

The keep is open from mid-June to end of August, an ideal period to enjoy long days and clear views of the surrounding mountains. Outside this summer season, the keep is accessible during the European Heritage Days (September) and can be visited by request for groups through the Association of Friends of Viuz-Faverges. The climb of 145 steps is more pleasant in dry weather; wear suitable shoes and avoid very hot or stormy days. The castle courtyard offers a shaded setting for a rest before or after the climb.

The keep visit is organised by the Association of Friends of Viuz-Faverges and the Heritage Guides of Savoie Mont Blanc, who accompany visitors and tell the history of the castle, its successive owners since 1112, and the site's strategic role through the centuries. The castle itself, private property since 2017, hosts events and accommodation, while the keep and courtyard remain open to the public for heritage visits.

The kennel operates on the Plaine-Joux plateau, in XQV0Z (Pays du XQV1Z), at around 1,300 m altitude. Access by car via the D43 + route des Plagnes from XQV2Z (10-15 min). Parking at the Plaine-Joux station. Snow equipment mandatory in winter.
The killing blow came around

The lake has a supervised swimming zone in summer (July–August, 1–7 pm), with shallow areas cordoned off for young children. Around the lake, several leisure providers offer paid activities: tree-top course, equestrian centre, Big Air Bag, inflatable water structures (Le Splash), pumptrack, trampolines, kayaking, paddleboarding, and beach volleyball. A free children's play area (slides, swings, wooden trail course) is accessible to all.

The lake is a natural mountain lake formed by an 18th-century landslide that dammed the Dranse de XQV0Z valley. Hemmed in between impressive limestone cliffs and a forest of conifers, it is famous for its emerald waters, earning it the nickname

The lake is accessible year-round. High season runs from June to August: the beach is supervised in July–August (12 noon–6 pm), activity providers open daily, parking becomes paid. May–June and September are quieter periods, ideal for walks and water activities without crowds. In autumn and winter, the site becomes a popular place for peaceful strolls with spectacular views of snow-capped XQV0Z. The fitness trail and lake loop remain passable year-round except during heavy snowfall.
The lake is home to alpine newts, dragonflies and numerous plant species. Regeneration zones dedicated to filtration, separate from swimming area.
The lake is the starting point for an

The lake offers exceptional views of the XQV0Z massif and the Fiz range. Accessible year-round, it provides a relaxing setting in summer and winter alike (walks along the snow-covered lake). A 3-star campsite and snack restaurant are on-site. The site also hosts aquabike courses during the summer season.
The lake offers remarkable reflections of surrounding peaks, notably Aiguille de Charlanon. Morning light is particularly beautiful in July-August.
The lake stands apart from conventional swimming facilities through its

The lakefront was redesigned during winter 2025-2026 (municipal order no. 46/2026), with new lawn, redrawn paths and modernised facilities, jointly funded by the village and the Grand XQV0Z authority.
The largest alpine cirque with 500-700m cliffs and 30+ waterfalls in spring. Marked trails, paid parking, free access to XQV0Z.
The largest developed lake in the XQV0Z region, located at 547 m elevation with sandy beach, supervised swimming zone in summer and panoramic views of the XQV1Z massif.
The largest developed lake in the XQV0Z region: sandy beach, supervised swimming in summer, water and land activities in a green setting facing the massif.
The largest free beach on Lake XQV0Z: 400 m of grass and sand, summer lifeguards, free showers and toilets. Albigny district, XQV1Z.
The largest leisure park in XQV0Z country: 30 hectares of nature at the foot of the Contamines Nature Reserve, with mountain lakes, free swimming beach and over 20 activities for the whole family.
The largest leisure park in XQV0Z country: 30 hectares, 2 lakes, free swimming, 20+ sports and relaxation activities. Free access all season in XQV1Z.
The largest public beach on Lake XQV0Z, featuring 400 meters of grassy shoreline facing the mountains. Lifeguard-supervised in summer, very popular, full amenities.
The last 200 m to the summit include
The legendary Grands Montets ski area (3,275 m) above Argentière is being completely rebuilt after the 2018 fire. The new Renzo Piano 3S cable car is scheduled for December 2026.

The leisure base is accessible year-round. Summer (May to September) is peak season: supervised swimming in July–August, open-air pool open, all activities available, animated terraces. Spring and autumn are quieter, ideal for walks, fishing and land-based activities. In winter, the site is regularly snow-covered and the lakes frozen: the base becomes a starting point for snowshoe walks and cross-country skiing along the Giffre. Playgrounds and sports fields remain accessible when snow-free. Espace Le Bois aux Dames hosts cultural events year-round.

The leisure complex is open daily in July and August, the busiest period with all activities running and permanent swimming supervision. In June, opening is limited to weekends and public holidays. The lake water, naturally temperate, is most pleasant from late June to late August. Outside high season, the complex offers afternoon opening from 1:30pm for unsupervised swimming and adventure course. The restaurant "À la Belle Étoile" remains accessible year-round Thursday to Sunday (except July-August when open daily), allowing you to enjoy the lake setting even outside summer. Autumn and spring offer a quieter setting for hikers wishing to discover the site without the summer crowds.
The limestone massif offers many equipped climbing routes from beginner to advanced. Topos available from guide offices.
The local club and ESF offer lessons. Ask directly at the rink.
The location is convenient for an evening in XQV0Z:

The loop around the Lac des Plagnes is easy and flat (30-45 min). The option that climbs to the Cubourré plateau and the erratic boulder is more demanding (2 to 3 hrs round trip) with elevation through the forest alongside the waterfall.
The loop's main feature is that it is
The main
The main XQV0Z measures 60 meters in height. There are actually two waterfalls at the site: a smaller one the trail passes beneath, and the main 60 m cascade at the canyon's end.
The main gondola of the XQV0Z Bike Park: 5-minute ride from the village to the Chavannes plateau (1,482 m), gateway to the Portes du Soleil ski and MTB network.
The main loop at XQV0Z is 4.5 km along the Dranse. Shorter loops (1 to 2 km) are possible by taking intermediate bridges.

The main season is July and August (daily 10:00–18:00). In June, the park opens weekends and public holidays only. Prefer mornings in July–August to avoid crowds. Beunaz Beach is quieter in June and early July. In very hot weather, combine the treetop course in the morning and swimming in the afternoon. Check the weather before visiting: strong winds or storms may cause the activity to close for safety reasons.

The main season runs December to March, when snow cover on Plaine-Joux allows sledding under the best conditions. Peak season (Christmas and February holidays) is in very high demand — book very early. Outside the snow season (April to November), cani-hiking and wheeled formats take over on the plateau's forest paths. Weather and snow may force a reschedule: keep some flexibility in your trip.

The main season runs December to March-April depending on Portes du Soleil snow cover. Christmas and February holidays are in very high demand — book very early. Shoulder periods (early December, late March) often offer more flexibility and quieter conditions. Off-season, cani-hiking and wheeled formats remain possible, on request. Weather, snow or thaws may force a reschedule: allow some flexibility in your trip.
The marsh hosts numerous waterfowl species: Eurasian reed warblers, grey herons, black kites. A purpose-built observatory allows discreet observation, especially in spring and at dawn.

The marsh offers beautiful photographic opportunities: golden reedbeds in autumn, dragonflies in flight, morning mists and water reflections. Light is particularly beautiful early morning and late afternoon.

The maze is composed of hedgerows and espalier apple trees that define chambers of greenery. Each thematic space showcases a collection of plants chosen for their sensory, medicinal, or edible properties: 25 varieties of mint, 25 of sage, 50 of fragrant pelargoniums, alpine plants, heirloom vegetables, espalier fruit trees, and old roses. Over 1,500 plant varieties are cultivated by a passionate team of gardeners led since 2009 by Matthieu Constans, head gardener. The garden welcomes approximately 40,000 visitors per year.
The minimum age is generally 10, provided you're accompanied by an adult depending on the room. Each room shows its difficulty level and minimum recommended age on the site.
The minimum age is generally 10, with an adult depending on the room. Each room shows its difficulty level and minimum recommended age on the site.
The minimum height for the main course at XQV0Z is 1.35 m. The black loops are optional and reserved for experienced participants.

The most common starting point for XQV0Z is Petit Port in XQV1Z-le-Vieux, accessible by car (10 min from the centre) or by bus line 20. The trail starts on Avenue de Chavoire, just after the bakery, at the Bois Jettaz sign.
The most iconic access is the
The multi-activity park includes several options adapted to all heights and levels:
The municipal beach adjoins the park: direct access on foot to the sunbathing lawns, the solarium and supervised swimming in season.
The municipal museum
The museum also features a
The museum also offers two
The museum has heavily modernised its offer in recent years with a strong family focus:

The museum is aimed at families with children from age 6-7 (curious about history and natural sciences), school groups (by booking), local archaeology enthusiasts and visitors staying in XQV0Z or around the lake. The visit is relatively short (45 min-1h30 depending on interest), ideal alongside a lakeside walk, a meal in a XQV1Z restaurant or a visit to the XQV2Z château and the Maison des oiseaux.
The museum is housed in XQV0Z's former tobacco manufactory, whose industrial history (tobacco, Tefal) is widely covered in the collections.
The museum is in XQV0Z (74140), on the shore of Lake Geneva, 15 minutes from XQV1Z and 40 minutes from Geneva. Exact address to confirm on ville-sciez.fr.
The museum is open
The museum is suited to families with children from age 6-7 (curious about history and natural sciences). For younger kids, the visit can be shorter.

The museum is the life's work of Philippe Piccot, who ran the Cinéma du Chablais in XQV0Z and saved and restored every Buisse-Bottazzi model: projection-cabin machines, lab equipment and accessories. Open every Tuesday in July-August, 2:30-5:30 pm; free guided tours.
The museum sits beneath the striking timber frame of an 1842 farmhouse, an architectural treasure of the Bornes Plateau. A sight in its own right.

The musher occasionally offers discovery evenings, moonlit rides in peak season and birthday packages for children. Watching local sled-dog races (Grande Odyssée Savoie XQV0Z, in the area) is sometimes proposed. Programme and dates posted on the official website and the kennel's social media.
The musher often photographs the ride; souvenirs are shared at the end (depending on package). Capture the moment.
The name 'Bellegarde' comes from Janus Bellegarde, syndic and secretary of the syndic of Thonon in the 16th century, who lived in the tower. The château today houses
The network is organised in three concentric loops marked with the classic hiking colour code:
The new
The new Nid d'Aigle platform and Col de Voza provide access to mountain bike trails. Ride the train up and bike down toward XQV0Z (separate ski lift pass required).
The offer is structured in two formats:
The offering centres on
The offering covers all
The offering varies with season and age. In winter, when snow is sufficient, the musher offers

The office (contact point) is in central XQV0Z. From Geneva (~45 km): A41 exit XQV1Z-Nord — 40 min. Outings usually start at meeting points in the massifs (Bauges, Aravis, XQV2Z) — carpool or personal vehicle depending on outing.
The office is also a valuable information hub

The on-site restaurant is a traditional brasserie, well suited to family evenings combining a meal with a few games. A bar and lounge area completes the offer, providing a space to wait between games or to round off the evening.
The only large natural sand beach on Lake XQV0Z, equipped with shaded lawns, diving board and accessible facilities. Blue Flag certified since 2010.
The only natural fine sand beach on the French shore of Lake Geneva, with a gentle slope extending several tens of metres and shallow waters reaching 25°C in summer.
The only place with aquariums in XQV0Z, at the Port de Rives. 2 Lake Geneva aquariums, fishermen's huts. Adult €3.50, free under 18 and every Sunday.
The only underground gallery open to the public in XQV0Z. A 40-minute guided tour 10° below ground and a spectacular 40-meter-high waterfall.
The operator may occasionally take part in regional balloon gatherings (Chablais local festivals). Private flights for birthdays, proposals, hen/stag. Gift vouchers year-round.
The operator supplies full equipment:
The origin of the name

The original cable cars (Lognan I and II) were destroyed by fire in August 2018. A new 3S cable car designed by Renzo Piano has been under construction since 2024, linking Argentière to the 3,275 m summit. The Plan Joran gondola provides interim winter access.
The outdoor
The outdoor park welcomes kids from around age 5-6 (depending on the workshop), bouldering from age 4 with family. Classes from age 6-7.

The park also hosts facilities managed by operators: the Centre Nautique de l'Espace Bernard Pellarin (heated open-air pool open May to late August), an adventure tree-climbing course (Tactiq Aventure), tennis courts and a fishing lake (permit required). The animal enclosure allows free observation of deer, stags, does and mouflons, making the park a popular family destination in the Genevois region.
The park celebrates its
The park connects with the redesigned town hall square — a landscape programme of 2010-2013 (ADP Dubois) that structured XQV0Z's civic heart.
The park distinguishes itself through its

The park features several emblematic species of local Alpine fauna: red deer, hinds, roe deer, wild boar, chamois, marmots (depending on season), ibex and other Alpine forest animals. Visibility varies by time of day and season.
The park has

The park includes the

The park is aimed at families with children, walkers and hikers, schools and holiday centres. It's an ideal visit to discover Alpine fauna in a few hours, in a natural and soothing setting. Educational panels explain the species, their way of life and their role in the ecosystem. The walk around the enclosures is easy (forest tracks) and suitable for all-terrain pushchairs or young children.
The park is at Beunaz Beach: supervised swimming, paddle, canoe, pedalo, inflatable lake structures, Télébeune (zip line), diving boards.
The park is best enjoyed April to October, with marked seasonal highlights. The
The park is divided into three zones: the
The park is divided into two worlds: a
The park is in the Semnoz forest, above XQV0Z (74000), accessible by the Semnoz road (D241) 10-15 minutes from central XQV1Z by car. Parking nearby.

The park is open April to October, but the best period to enjoy activities fully is June to September, when swimming is open and all providers are active. In July–August, heavy weekend crowds — prefer mornings or weekdays. In June and September, quieter weather, ideal to enjoy nature without crowds. The park closes in winter when the resort focuses on alpine skiing.
The park is open from
The park is open roughly May to October,

The park is open year-round with hours varying by season. High season (April to August) offers the widest time slots with evening openings until 7:30 pm on weekends. In winter, the park operates Wednesdays and weekends with daily opening during school holidays. Spring and autumn are ideal periods to enjoy the forest with less crowding. Allow 1 to 2h30 depending on the course chosen. Avoid heavy rain or strong wind days. Online booking required, possible until the last minute.
The park is operated by
The park is split into two zones: a

The park is the official local recommendation under the 'Indian Forest XQV0Z-Les Aravis XQV1Z' label. The Indian Forest network has no other site in the XQV2Z department, so this XQV3Z park is the reference for the area, in a mid-mountain setting overlooking the Aravis.
The park occasionally hosts community sports events (running races, family activities) organised by XQV0Z Town Hall. Check the Tourist Office calendar for dates.
The park offers fine lawns overlooking XQV0Z: a family picnic with views of the Pointe de Marcelly and the Aravis range.

The park offers four types of courses suited to all sizes and ages: the Grand Course (from 1m35, approximately 2h30) with Tarzan swing and return zip-lines of 200 m; the Junior Course (from 1m20, approximately 2h) with 5 loops including a zip-line course; the Children's Course (from 1 m, approximately 1h) with 4 loops; and the Acro'Net (all ages, 1 to 2h) with giant balls, ball pit, trampolines and slide. All courses are equipped with a continuous safety line system developed by Koala Equipment and designed by Altus, guaranteeing maximum safety while allowing autonomous progression once the briefing is completed.

The park offers two marked circuits: an easy route (approximately 1 hour) suitable for all-terrain strollers, and a "fitness" route to explore every corner. New for 2026: an immersive sensory route, "The Secret Atmospheres of Merlet" (5 minutes). Free activities are offered in July–August (Antlers and Horns, The Mountain and Its Secrets, The Predators), as well as exhibition rooms and video screenings. The park is committed to an eco-responsible approach: local electricity production (solar panels and micro-hydroelectric power station), selective sorting and zero waste.

The park provides harness, absorber lanyard, helmet and Smart Belay carabiners. Bring: flexible clothing, long sleeves, long trousers (harness friction), closed-toe shoes/trainers, hair tie for long hair.
The park sits on the site of the former château of Count Joachim de la Grange (18th c.). Interpretive panels cover the seigneurial history of the XQV0Z.

The park welcomes children from around 4-6 years depending on the zone, with a dedicated little-jumpers area. Teens and adults enjoy free-jump, dodge ball and trampoline basket. Check the minimum age on just-jump.fr.

The park welcomes children from around age 3 (dedicated toddlers' area) through to adults, as families or with friends. Sessions typically last 1 hour, on fixed slots, with non-slip socks compulsory (sold on site). Children's birthday parties are a specialty: all-inclusive package with private room, cake and drinks. The park also offers group packages, stag/hen, business seminars and school groups outside school hours. Systematic briefing by the Jump Coaches.

The park welcomes families, groups of friends, schools and businesses. Access is from age 5 or so, with dedicated slots for the youngest (pre-school) in the morning on some days. Birthdays are a major part of the offer: 1 hour of jumping + private room for cake and drinks subject to availability. The park also hosts stag/hen parties, business seminars (team-building with dodgeball or ninja) and themed sessions (neon nights, music).
The park's main attraction is

The pass has been climbed six times by the Tour de France. Two routes to choose from: via XQV0Z (13 km, 6.5% average, sections at 10-11%) or via XQV1Z (18 km, 4.6% average, more regular). Splendid views of the Aravis range.

The pass is crossed by the D909 departmental road, opened in 1902, today a stage of the Route des Grandes Alpes® linking XQV0Z to Nice. On site: the Sainte-Anne chapel, restaurants, souvenir shops, alpine farms selling Reblochon cheese directly, parking areas, and hiking trailheads. On a clear day, open view of the XQV1Z massif. The pass also welcomes cyclists and bikers who tackle this legendary ascent.

The path along the Dranse at XQV0Z is walkable from around 1 May to 19 November, outside the snow season. In winter the upper section is snowy and used for cross-country skiing or snowshoeing depending on conditions.
The path along the Dranse at XQV0Z runs through the XQV1Z valley (AOP cheese country), follows the Dranse de Sous-le-Pas river, and allows you to reach the Maison du Fromage XQV2Z and XQV3Z Abbey.

The path along the Dranse at XQV0Z starts at the free car park in the Très-les-Pierres hamlet. At the finish, there is a car park in the centre of XQV1Z. Bal'Aulps valley shuttle bus available in season.
The path along the Dranse is around 16 km linear between the Très-les-Pierres hamlet at XQV0Z and the village of XQV1Z. Up to 20 km depending on the variant. Allow 4 hrs on foot, 1 hr 30 by bike.
The path along the banks of the Arve from XQV0Z to Arthaz can be walked year-round. Prefer March to November (outside floods). In summer, the shade of the woods and the river's coolness are welcome.
The path along the banks of the Arve runs through rich riparian forest: grey heron, dipper, kingfisher, coots, sometimes beavers. Pioneer plants and species typical of riverbanks.
The path is officially designated a mountain-bike route by Savoie Mont Blanc Tourisme. Rideable on hybrid and mountain bikes, as an out-and-back or 14.5 km loop.
The path passes near the Jardin des Iris in XQV0Z, a haven of perennials with spring flowering (April-May). Free access.
The path runs through the XQV0Z valley, home of the AOP XQV1Z cheese. Dairy farms and the Maison du Fromage can be visited.
The path stays wide and gently sloped, suiting the whole family;
The permanent collection, built up since 1958, gathers contemporary paintings and sculptures where the
The permanent exhibition

The plateau offers a preserved natural setting, both wild and welcoming, where forest meets pastureland and where deer, hinds and mouflons are regularly spotted in the Grande Jeanne park. XQV0Z regularly features in regional sporting competitions, including the XQV1Z Maxi Race, and welcomes thousands of walkers, athletes and families each year seeking fresh air and exceptional panoramas.

The platform offers one of the most spectacular panoramas in the XQV0Z region: a sweeping view 800 m above the lake, a 180° panorama from the Bauges massif in the west to Salève and the Jura in the north, with Mont Blanc, the Aravis and XQV1Znette visible to the east. The viewpoint sits on the ridgeline between Mont Baron (1,299 m, summit 10 minutes above the platform) and Mont Veyrier (1,291 m, 30 minutes further north via the ridge path). Together they form a continuous balcony overlooking Lake XQV2Z, highly prized by hikers, photographers and bivouac campers. An orientation table is installed on the platform. The site is also renowned for its spectacular sunsets over the lake.

The playground spans one hectare of forest and features around twenty fun workshops: tree courses, zip-lines, net maze, giant trampoline, tree cabins, net sea, serpent swing, and a new aerial course over 1.20 m high (reserved for older children). A Mini-Kids area welcomes 2–3 year-olds with age-appropriate games. An organic eco-snack (crêpes, savoury galettes, ice cream, local drinks) completes the offer on site.

The price for XQV0Z varies by course: Grand Course adult (16+) €26, youth (under 16) €21, Junior Course €20, Children's Course €16, Acro'Net €16, Orienteering course €6. Group rate from 8 people. Verified May 14, 2026.
The price is around €25-35 per person, with a discount from 4 players. Group packages and offers for stag/hen or business are available. Exact 2026 prices to confirm.
The price is around €25-35 per person, with a discount from 4 players. Group packages and offers for stag/hen or business available. Exact 2026 prices to confirm.
The price of the Notre Histoire museum in XQV0Z is €3.70 full price, €3 reduced. FREE for everyone on Wednesdays and free all year for under-22s and students. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The price of the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in XQV0Z is not published on the official sources consulted. Contact Philippe Piccot directly on +33 (0)4 50 94 11 79 for the current price.
The price of the Sacred Art Museum of XQV0Z is not published on official sources. Contact the Tourist Office on +33 (0)4 50 47 76 08 for the current price.
The price of the Savoyard Pottery Museum at XQV0Z is not published on the official sources. Please call +33 (0)4 50 62 01 90.
The programme at XQV0Z is refreshed every week, generally on Wednesday. Check AlloCiné or the local press for the weekly schedule.
The programme at XQV0Z is refreshed every week, generally on Wednesday. Check the official site (XQV1Z or AlloCiné for the weekly schedule.
The programme changes every week — check the official site or AlloCiné for current films and showtimes.
The programme features

The project is run by the Office de tourisme des Alpes du XQV0Z and the Foyer nordique des Moises. It introduces families to mountain life past and present while encouraging respect for nature: alpine flora, streams, summer pastures, plateau landscapes and mixed forest. The trails suit family outings as much as gentle walks for adults, and can be tackled
The pèle of the Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm is the living room heated by the stove — the heart of the traditional Savoyard house, where the family would gather.
The quieter north-facing side of XQV0Z: 5-minute gondola to the Mont-Chéry summit (1,535 m), 360° views of Mont Blanc and Lac XQV1Z, hiking and MTB with a wilder feel.
The recent renovation added
The remains of the Château de XQV0Z are visible 24/7, free access year-round. Daytime is preferable to read the arrow slits and the curtain wall properly. Checked on 10 June 2026.

The remains of the Château de XQV0Z can be visited year-round, but spring and summer remain the most pleasant seasons — the village of XQV1Z (820 m altitude) can be covered in snow in winter. Autumn offers beautiful light on the medieval stones.

The restaurant and the panoramic terrace are a strong point of the venue, with classic brasserie cuisine and a particularly enjoyable view of the XQV0Z range at sunset. The bowling lanes, pool tables and arcade games make the complex a one-stop evening venue, combining game, drink and meal under one roof.
The rink is a key sporting facility of the city, hosting individual sessions, school groups and clubs throughout the year. The Friday

The rink is aimed at families with children from age 4-5, groups of friends, school groups and sports clubs. Beginners can take a lesson with an instructor (ice school or private), while experienced skaters enjoy free skating or join the local hockey club training. Disco-on-ice and themed nights run during winter school holidays, and some evening sessions are open in high season.

The rink welcomes the XQV0Z Hockey Club for home games, as well as figure skating galas and regional championships, with a packed events calendar from December to March. Public sessions alternate with sports activities, making the rink a key family destination of the resort.

The road is closed in winter, usually from mid-November to early May depending on snow cover. In 2026 the col reopened on 6 May at 2pm. Access is free year-round during the open season, and a free shuttle bus runs to the col from XQV0Z from 11 July to 21 August.
The road to the pass winds through forest for most of the route, then opens to panoramic views in the final kilometres:
The rooms at XQV0Z generally host 2 to 6 players per room, with an ideal team of 4 to move easily in the décor. For larger groups, several rooms can be booked in parallel.
The rooms cover varied worlds:
The rooms generally host 2 to 6 players, with an ideal team of 4. For larger groups, several rooms can be booked in parallel.
The route
The route accepts dogs on leads, with no risky livestock over most of the trail.
The route alternates wooded natural stretches, urban sections (XQV0Z, the Jardin des Iris, the Grand Casino) and farmland. Wide views of

The route begins with an
