The route climbs from
The route covers all aspects of local life:
The route finishes along a rocky ridge, a fun and aesthetic section, before descending via a hiking trail.
The route is
The route is waymarked in yellow (PDIPR); a map is available at the tourist information office. Dogs must be kept on a lead because of the grazing herds.
The route leaves the village centre and crosses the
The route lends itself well to snowshoeing in winter: gentle gradient, waymarking maintained (depending on snow cover).
The route measures
The route mostly follows the hillsides above the lake (300–900 m altitude), offering panoramic balcony views over
The route offers three emergency exits allowing you to adapt the itinerary to your level and fitness on the day.

The route via XQV0Z (18 km at 4.6%) is more accessible for intermediate cyclists. The route via XQV1Z is significantly harder (sections at 10% for 3 km after the village), reserved for experienced cyclists.
The route was developed in two phases: the
The routes are passable on snowshoes in winter depending on weather conditions and snow cover (information at the Foyer des Moises).
The ruins of the Château de XQV0Z are open 24/7 year-round. Guided tours by the Heritage Guides are by reservation. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The schedule at XQV0Z changes every week, usually on Wednesday. Check the official site (XQV1Z or AlloCiné for the weekly schedule.
The school covers every boat-towed discipline:
The school offers a wide range of activities:
The season runs April to October,

The season runs April to September depending on Dranse flow. Booking is mandatory, online or by phone, with weather confirmation the day before. Prices range from around €45 for classic descents to €60-65 for sporty formats (2026 prices to confirm). XQV0Z supplies all technical equipment: wetsuit, neoprene socks, helmet, lifejacket, paddle/fins. Bring swimwear, towel and worn-out trainers or river shoes. Swimming is mandatory.

The season runs from April to October. Conditions vary by weather: calm flights in the morning (ideal first time), thermals in late afternoon (sensations). In bad weather flights are postponed or cancelled.
The season runs from April to October. May-June offer iris and water-lily bloom, September-October the beautiful autumn light. Avoid peak summer for a more peaceful visit.

The season runs from April to late October, with peak from June to September. Spring (May-June): dry rock after rains, cool but pleasant temperatures, moderate attendance. Summer (July-August): very early starts recommended (from 8 am) as the wall faces full south — it gets very hot from 11 am. September-October: optimal conditions (mild temperatures, reduced attendance, golden light on XQV0Z). Always avoid storm days and after heavy rain (slippery limestone).

The season runs from June to late September depending on water levels and weather. Prices range from around €55-80 for a half-day to €85-110 for a full day (2026 indicative prices, to confirm on the official site). Booking is via escape-canyon.com or by phone, ideally several days ahead in peak season. The meeting point is usually in XQV0Z or near the chosen canyon, communicated when you book. Plan extra driving time to the canyon itself.

The season runs from late March to mid-November, with peak attendance July-August (daily opening). Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) offer a calmer experience with changing forest colours. Wednesdays, weekends and school holidays are preferable outside July-August. The park is forest-based: wear appropriate clothing (covering clothes, closed shoes). Avoid shorts and short sleeves (harness friction). No running water on site: bring your own water bottle.

The season runs roughly from April to October, with optimal conditions in the morning (calm flights, ideal for first-timers) or late afternoon (thermals for those who love sensations). Booking is essential. Plan 2-3h on site (meeting at take-off, briefing, flight, return transport). Conditions vary, so your slot may be moved or rescheduled; keep some flexibility in your programme.

The season usually runs from June to late September depending on river flow. Prices are around €55-80 for a half-day and €85-110 for a full day (2026 indicative prices, to confirm on the official site). Booking is via neo-canyon.com or by phone; very strongly recommended several days ahead in July and August. The meeting point is usually in XQV0Z or close to the chosen canyon, communicated when you book.
The seasonal
The second half is devoted to
The self-guided farm visit is free, no booking required, year-round Monday to Saturday. Workshops, donkey walks, escape game and stays are paid — rates on lafermedupetitmont.fr.
The sensation is powerful: heights reach up to
The setting is the garden's signature feature: an uninterrupted view of the

The seven trails are graded by level: a children's trail (from age 3), then beginner, intermediate, sporty and expert routes. A safety briefing at the start is compulsory. Spectacular ziplines stretch up to 400 m above the thermal park, with sweeping views of the XQV0Z range, a unique setting for treetop adventures.
The single price for the Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z is €5. Free for children under 6. Verified 10 June 2026.
The single price for the Nature Museum at XQV0Z is €6 per person, free for under-2s. Groups €12/person with activity. Family: 3rd person onwards €10. Verified 10 June 2026.
The single rate at the Old Ski Museum at XQV0Z is €4 per person. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The site also includes a

The site also offers an orienteering course (from age 7, search for 20 markers in the forest) and a treasure hunt (from age 3) for the youngest. XQV0Z welcomes families, school groups, corporate committees, birthday parties, bachelor and bachelorette parties with adapted packages. Sportswear and closed shoes covering heel and toes are mandatory for safety reasons. Online booking is required on the official website, with the possibility to book until the last minute. The park stands out for its excellent value for money and professional on-site supervision.
The site can be privatised for weddings, seminars or receptions, in a unique setting in XQV0Z.

The site can only be visited on a guided tour during the season (usually April to October), reached from Évian harbour by a small shuttle boat that hugs the shore — a very enjoyable approach that is part of the experience. The tour, led by a naturalist guide, lasts about 1h30 to 2 hours and alternates botanical, ecological and historical commentary on the villa and the gardens. Children are welcome: the tone is accessible and the paths suit child-height discovery. Daily capacity is limited, so booking is strongly recommended in July-August.
The site comprises the remains of two castle enclosures, an
The site features
The site features a fine sandy beach with supervised swimming in July–August (depth zones

The site has been developed since 1869, when 250 m of footbridges were fixed into the rock 25 m above the torrent. Access to the footbridges is paid, and the trail is open from 15 March to 15 October. Around the site, the commune of XQV0Z has created the Chemin des Châteaux, a free 2 km walking loop linking the entrance of the Gorges to the Château de Montrottier via the pont des Liasses and the pont de Pontverre.
The site has earth paths and requires boarding a boat. Wheelchair access is partial: we recommend contacting the Évian Tourist Office to prepare your visit.
The site has held the

The site hosts cultural events throughout the year: medieval historical reenactments, live performances, temporary exhibitions (art, photography, heritage). European Heritage Days in September with free activities and guided tours. The site is part of the Chablais geological route inaugurated during Heritage Days.
The site hosts throughout the year historical reenactments, live performances and temporary exhibitions organized by the municipality and local heritage associations.

The site includes supervised swimming with a pool fed by lake water, diving boards of various heights, the famous Télébeune (aquatic zip-line inaugurated in 1959), an Indiana Parc tree-climbing course, water sports activities (canoe, pedalo, stand-up paddle), inflatable structures, trampolines, sports fields, as well as a restaurant and unusual accommodation (tree houses, tipis, yurts) to extend the experience.
The site is a
The site is a major memorial place of the XQV0Z Resistance, linked to the Saxel maquis and the Vallée Verte STO refusers.

The site is accessible (disabled parking, adapted toilets). The Alpine Coaster requires being able to get on/off the sled and operate the hand brake — assistance possible but a minimum level of mobility is required. Call +33 4 50 53 08 97 to discuss your situation.

The site is accessible and flyable year-round. In summer (May to October), flight conditions are optimal: strong thermals in afternoon, high numbers of pilots and spectators, pleasant plateau temperature. In winter (December to April), flights also take place, but the ski station is active and the site quieter. Spring and autumn offer stable conditions with fewer crowds. Best flight hours: afternoon (thermals), inadvisable in morning and in strong westerly winds. The site is heavily visited on weekends from June to September: arrive early or visit on weekdays to avoid crowding at takeoff.
The site is accessible by marked paths from the Ternier hamlet. Walls and earthworks visible; free access.

The site is accessible year-round, but the best period extends from April to October to fully enjoy the panorama and favourable weather conditions. In summer (July-August), the site benefits from ideal light in the late afternoon to admire Lake XQV0Z and the Alps. Possibility of intense heat with no shade on the plateau. Winter and spring offer quieter visits and clear views on fine days, but beware of ice on trails and ruins in winter. The site organizes regular cultural events: medieval reenactments, photo exhibitions, live shows — check the XQV1Z municipality agenda or the Chablais Tourist Office.

The site is accessible year-round, but the experience varies by season. Spring (April–May) offers the most powerful flow thanks to snowmelt, with a very impressive waterfall but glacial water. Summer (June–September) is the ideal period for guided canyoning and swimming in the downstream pools, though the water remains very cold. Autumn (October) offers colorful forest scenery and fewer visitors. In winter (December–March), the waterfall may freeze partially or completely, creating spectacular scenery appreciated by snowshoe hikers and ice climbing enthusiasts. Winter access is practicable but requires appropriate equipment (crampons recommended in freezing conditions). The on-site restaurant closes in low season — check before visiting.

The site is bordered by restaurants, brasseries and water sports schools (sailing, diving, boat rentals). A kiosk and automatic restrooms are on-site. Winter saunas on the waterfront complete the leisure offerings year-round.

The site is equipped for relaxation and outdoor sports: a 23-apparatus fitness trail between the second and third lakes, volleyball and pétanque courts, table tennis tables, equipped picnic areas, outdoor showers near the first aid post, public toilets and seasonal café-restaurant. A bird observatory at the northern end of the third lake enables observation of mallards, coots and wild rabbits.
The site is freely accessible on foot via a trail starting between XQV0Z station and the Fontaine car park; a
The site is frequented by canyoning groups in summer. Supervised activity only, specialist equipment required. Do not venture alone on descent areas.

The site is highly popular with the general public who come to admire the ballet of colorful sails against the exceptional panorama. Flights are practiced in thermals in the afternoon, with landing at Chedde or Marlioz in the Arve valley, 760 meters below. The takeoff zone is equipped with an FFVL (French Free Flight Federation) information panel, a wind sleeve, and a webcam viewable online.
The site is labelled Jardin remarquable by the French Ministry of Culture: official recognition of the landscape, botanical and educational quality.

The site is now laid out as an interpretive trail signposted from the Avoriaz tourist office, passing between the Malinka and Saskia buildings down to the old works. Orientation table; free year-round access.
The site is one of 23 geosites in the Chablais UNESCO Global Geopark. Interpretation panels on the geological history of the hilltop, from the molasse sea to alpine glaciers.

The site is open April to September only. High season (July-August) offers the longest hours (9am–6pm) and more visitors, particularly for zip-lines. April, May, June, and September offer reduced hours (10am–5pm) but fewer visitors, ideal for a quieter cave visit. Underground temperature remains constant at 10°C year-round — bring a sweater or fleece even in mid-summer. The waterfall is most impressive in spring (April-May) when snowmelt generously feeds the Saint-Ruph torrent. In September, flow is lower but the setting remains magnificent. On rainy days, the waterfall gains power and the natural site takes on a mysteriously atmospheric and particularly photogenic quality.

The site is part of XQV0Z's Ville d'art et d'histoire label, with interpretive panels and a direct link to the Jardins de l'Europe. It is a natural stop between the old town, the lake and the avenue d'Albigny — and just a short walk from the Pont des Amours and the canals district.

The site is protected: the shorelines have been classified to preserve their natural character and historical connection to the House of Savoy. Swimming is free and unsupervised. The beaches are connected by maintained trails that run along the shoreline. One beach, marked with signs, is reserved for naturist practice.

The site is rich in history: a marshland island in the Middle Ages, the "Clos Lombard" was given to the Visitation order in 1644 by a Lombard banking family, became national property at the Revolution, then bought back by the town between 1835 and 1843. Free 24/7.
The site is visited only on a guided tour (~1h30-2h) led by a specialist guide. Botanical, ecological and historical commentary accessible to all.

The site occupies the location of a mountain hamlet inhabited year-round until the 1900s, before becoming a seasonal pasture. The park was created in 1967–1968 by Philippe Cachat, a Chamonix native passionate about mountains and an airplane pilot, with Bambi, a tamed chamois who became the mascot. Today managed by the Cachat family (6 employees in season), the park combines observation of alpine wildlife, an exceptional panorama of the XQV0Z massif and discovery of mountain heritage through its old alpine chalets. An altiport (Altiport Philippe-Cachat) occupies the park's center.

The site of the ruins of the Château de XQV0Z is freely accessible. For a guided tour, book with the Guides du Patrimoine Savoie Mont Blanc, who offer tours of the village and ruins year-round by appointment.
The site offers
The site offers a rare combination of facilities: diving boards at

The site offers a wide range of activities: supervised swimming in the lake from early July to early September (12pm–6pm), a children's playground, sports pitches (football, basketball, beach volley, pétanque), fishing, lakeside walks, stand-up paddle and a discovery trail. Paying facilities are also available: an open-air swimming pool with slides and three basins, tennis and padel courts, an adventure course and paragliding.
The site operates on two levels: it's a

The site presents remarkable geological interest with its famous S-shaped rock fold, characteristic of Jurassic limestone series of the subalpine nappes. Classified as a natural site since 1991, it offers complete amenities: free parking, picnic areas, and starting points for hiking and mountain biking trails.

The site regularly hosts summer events: children's shows, open-air concerts, trout-fishing contests. La Covagne bar-restaurant on the lake shore. Public toilets, benches, and picnic tables. Mountain-bike wash area. Located in the Grand Massif domain, Lac Bleu is a landing point for paragliders. XQV0Z is served by the intercommunal Haut-Giffre Tourisme office.

The site stands out for its exceptional biodiversity: it is home to 350 plant species, 87 bird species, 29 butterfly species and 13 bat species, including the pygmy pipistrelle (unique colony in XQV0Z). The domain is not an urban park but a preserved natural space, shaped over centuries by human practices. The Rovorée manor and La Châtaignière residence, two 19th-century manor houses, now host temporary exhibitions and artist residencies.

The site stands out for its varied facilities: more than 1.2 km of zip-lines spread across all courses (including a 40 m zip-line dedicated to 3-6 year-olds), an airbag for free-fall jumps of 3 to 7 meters onto a giant cushion, a unique human catapult in XQV0Z propelling participants over 12 meters, and two outdoor escape games around the lake combining riddles, observation and teamwork. The park welcomes families, school groups, birthday parties and corporate events in a wooded environment on the lake shore.
The site was redeveloped in

The site's main draw is the outdoor balneoform pool (open June-September) — the main reason to come in summer. The Spa is the winter option par excellence. Daily access from €25-32 depending on the offer.
The site's second sensory trail, walked barefoot to experience different materials (straw, water, bark, wood chips).

The ski area is accessible in winter (6 Dec 2025–3 May 2026) via the Plan Joran gondola (skiers only). It is closed from 4 May to 30 November 2026 for the new 3S cable car works. Full reopening is planned for December 2026.
The slot floor has

The slot-machine room is open daily 10am–3am, while the table-game room opens every evening 9pm–3am. The combination of casino, palatial hotel and lakeside setting makes the Impérial Palace one of the landmark addresses of XQV0Z's belle-époque waterfront.
The spa is adults-only; depending on the slot, minimum age is 14 or 16. It is not suitable for families with young children. Details on the official website.
The spa is designed around the view: large windows and panoramic terraces facing the Aiguilles and the glacier.
The spirit is firmly family-oriented:
The standout feature is that the château is
The start is in the centre of the village: you can combine it with a stroll through XQV0Z, its ice rink, shops and tourist office.

The summer park is open April to end of August, with a gradual increase in services: April and May with reduced hours (weekends and public holidays only in May), then peak season from early June to end of August with extended hours and all shows. The best months to visit are

The summit features technical installations (mountain rescue radio relay, weather antennae) and a visitor logbook box. Hikers can reach the summit via two main variants: Épaule du XQV0Z (the most frequented route) or Panse (less-used route). The ascent passes through several remarkable points: XQV1Z refuge (2,002 m), Mont Géroux (2,288 m), and the arrival of the Épaule chairlift (closed in summer). On clear days, visibility extends to the most distant massifs, offering a unique reading of the Alpine relief. XQV2Z is also the culminating point of the Diagonale du Mont-Joly, a vertical kilometer race of 4 km and 1,000 m elevation gain, organized in summer and winter versions.

The summit of the Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges offers a 360° panoramic view over the Massif des Bauges (geopark), the Bornes massif, the town of Faverges and the surrounding basin. The wooden gallery (hourd) allows for detailed landscape interpretation.
The summit offers an exceptional circular view of Lake Geneva, the peaks of the Chablais (Cornettes de Bise, Dent d'Oche, Roc d'Enfer), the Mont Blanc massif in clear weather, and the Swiss Jura.
The summit offers one of the widest views over the XQV0Z basin — ideal late in the day for golden light on the lake and Bauges massif.

The three escape-game scenarios cover different themes — scientific exploration, fantasy, mystery — and are designed for teams of
The tone of the tour is suitable for children from age ~6-7. A great way to introduce children to nature and wetland conservation.

The torrent feeding the cascade originates at Pointe d'Areu (2,468 m), a bastion of the Bornes massif. After a 5.5-kilometer course, it breaks through Jurassic limestone cliffs to form the XQV0Z before reaching the plain. Downstream, the torrent bed tumbles down the slope over 200 meters, scattered with large rocky blocks, creating a succession of small secondary waterfalls difficult to spot. The torrent water is captured upstream of the cascade and treated at a microfilter station to supply part of the municipality of XQV1Z; this installation is underground and has not degraded the classified site.
The traditional gaming room at XQV0Z Grand Casino offers English Roulette tables, open in the evening.
The trail climbs via the Tête des Annes and the Col de l'Oulettaz to the
The trail crosses the main communes of the Pays du XQV0Z:

The trail first leads to the most spectacular part of the waterfall, then descends steeply towards the Chinaillon torrent where you discover a wild gorge. The terrain can be muddy and slippery — good hiking boots are essential. This easy and refreshing walk, ideal for families, also allows you to discover old Chinaillon with its typical alpine chalets and panoramic views over the Aravis range.
The trail follows the Chéran river, an emblematic spot for fly-fishers, and offers a green setting right in the heart of the village.

The trail is accessible from April to October without particular difficulty. Spring (April–May) and autumn (September–October) offer the best conditions: the waterfall has strong flow after rainfall, and the potholes are clearly visible. In summer, the shaded trail remains pleasant even in hot weather, but water flow may be lower during dry periods. In winter, access can be difficult depending on snowfall. Avoid periods of heavy rain: the trail becomes very slippery and stream crossings can be risky.
The trail is one of the few in XQV0Z to take in

The trail is passable from April to November, except in snowy conditions. In spring (April–May), after rain or snowmelt, the waterfall reveals itself in all its power with spectacular flow. In summer, prefer a visit early morning or late afternoon to enjoy the peace and coolness in the shade of the beech forest, especially during peak season at the resort. In autumn (October), the flamboyant colours of the forest add different charm to the walk. Avoid snowy periods (December to March) when the trail may be impassable. Check with the Tourist Office before departure.

The trail is passable from April to October. Summer (June–September) is the busiest period, with peak crowds on sunny weekends — the viewpoint is one of the most popular in the XQV0Z basin. Spring (April–May) and autumn (September–October) offer lower visitor numbers and soft photographic light. In winter, the terrain becomes slippery and icy; weathered rocks can be dangerous in damp conditions. Sunsets in late afternoon (17:00–20:00 depending on season) attract many bivouac campers. The view is clear year-round, but fog may obscure the lake during winter thermal inversions.
The trail leads to the
The trail leads to the pink marble quarry of XQV0Z, a relic of local quarrying that tells the geological history of the Chablais.
The trail lies within the Chablais UNESCO Global Geopark and offers an introduction to the geology of the Northern Alps.
The trail runs alongside a mountain river; keep an eye on children but enjoy the sound of the water.

The trail runs through the commune of XQV0Z (74350), in the Monts du Genevois, halfway between XQV1Z and XQV2Z. It is short and flat, accessible to children and occasional walkers alike, in shaded woodland ideal in summer. At the heart of the trail, a stop is offered by the
The trail starts at the Virage du Tenne, at the end of the Route du Petit-XQV0Z (2 km from the village). It crosses a

The trail to XQV0Z is rated difficult (400 m elevation gain from Planpraz, scree passages, mountain trail). It is suitable for children experienced in hiking (from approximately 8-10 years old) and in good physical condition. Not accessible with a stroller.

The treetop course at XQV0Z is accessible from age 2 with the Little Kid course (up to 105 cm), under adult supervision. From 105 cm, children access the Kid courses independently. From 135 cm, access to Indiana Park courses (blue, red, black).

The usual starting point of the Heritage Trail is at the Charly chapel (Saint-Jacques chapel, 15th century), in the heart of the village of Charly in XQV0Z. You can also join it from Jussy or Saint-Symphorien.
The valley with its meadows offers an ideal setting for a packed lunch. Respect nature: take your waste with you, no fire outside authorized areas.

The venue includes a traditional restaurant open at lunch and dinner, ideal for family or group evenings combining a meal with several games of bowling. The cuisine is classic brasserie style, designed for everyday dining and group bookings.

The venue is fully accessible (wheelchair-friendly, audio description on some shows). Plan at least 1h30 on site, up to half a day with planetarium and workshops. Tickets are around €5-7, free under age 6, with a family pass and discounts for Grand XQV0Z residents. Booking advised for the planetarium and holiday workshops. The Passerelles district also offers riverside walks along the Thiou — perfect before or after the visit.
The venue offers

The venue offers outdoor games on forested fields with obstacles, plus possibly urban-style scenarios. Usual scenarios include capture the flag, elimination, zone defence, protect the president, base attack/defence. A typical game lasts 10-15 minutes followed by a debrief, with several games totalling 1h30 to 2h of actual play. Pricing is generally degressive depending on number of paintballs (packages of 100, 200, 300 balls) and players.

The very gentle profile — inherited from the railway — makes it one of the most

The via ferrata hosts no organised events: it is a free-access route integrated into the Aiguilles Rouges nature reserve. Chamonix and XQV0Z guide offices occasionally run collective introduction sessions (summer school holidays) or discovery days combining Bérard with another XQV1Z via. The Reserve Visitor Centre in XQV2Z also offers educational nature activities in July-August.
The via is graded AD+ (Fairly Difficult +): fun yet sporty. It requires good fitness for the steep 20-min approach and proper exposure management. Suitable for accompanied beginners without vertigo.

The via is graded AD+ (Fairly Difficult +): not very physically demanding, but the starting wall and aerial passages require managing exposure. Suitable for accompanied beginners with prior introduction to verticality.
The village also preserves a rural church and chapels typical of Savoyard architecture.

The village and ruins of the Château de XQV0Z (74270) are the remains of a former châtellenie of the Counts of Geneva. The castle was razed around 1630 by Louis XIII's troops. The site is freely accessible and can be visited on a guided tour.
The village car park is nearby and free. The garden is 2 minutes' walk behind the church.
The village centre is the start of several hiking trails through the Brevon and col de Tré-l'Aigle — the garden often serves as a starting or finishing point.

The village of XQV0Z has two beaches: the main 'Sous les Prés' (400 m of lawn) and the smaller, more confidential 'La Pointe'. A snack-bar opens in season at Sous les Prés, along with paddle and pedalo hire. Swimming is not supervised, so parental vigilance is recommended for children.

The village of XQV0Z is pedestrian-only. Paid parking is available approximately 300 meters from the XQV1Z. During peak season (July–August), it is recommended to arrive early in the morning to find parking easily.
The village of XQV0Z, its abbey and the Maison du Fromage are 10 min away by car to extend the day.
The visit

The visit circuit includes the remains of the abbatial church, former cellars, gatehouse, medicinal herb garden, medieval vegetable garden and vegetated cloister. An augmented reality application allows visitors to discover the abbey reconstructed in 3D as it was between the 16th and 18th centuries, using a virtual reality headset or digital tablet.
The visit combines well with a lakeside walk, XQV0Z beach, XQV1Z château, the Maison des oiseaux (raptor demonstrations) or a meal at a lakeside restaurant.

The visit highlights the patient gestures of breeders, cheesemakers and master ripeners of the XQV0Z Valley through images, sounds, hands-on stations and reconstructions. Visitors discover the local XQV1Z cow (brown-ringed eyes), her rich milk and the cheese's signature.

The visit lasts about 45 minutes to 1h30 depending on your interest and the possible presence of a guide. The museum is modest in size, making it an ideal stop alongside other activities.

The walking route along the banks of the Arve from XQV0Z to Arthaz is 8.9 km one way, around 2 hours of walking. A 14.5 km loop is possible by taking the other bank on the return.
The walkway runs from the ticket office downstream up to the

The water park features a 25 m swimming pool, a 500 m² balneoform pool (indoor and outdoor by season) heated to 33°, a current river, a fun paddling pool and 9 slides. Climbing is the main feature on the sport side, with one of the largest indoor walls in the Geneva area: 1,500 m² of climbing surface.

The water sports centre at Espace Bernard Pellarin is open from 18 May to 30 August 2026. Public opening hours (outside school group periods in May-June): Monday to Friday 11:15–13:30 and 16:30–19:00, Wednesday and weekends extended hours. Verified on the CCPC website on 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
The waterfall and its potholes offer excellent photographic opportunities, especially in spring and autumn when water flow is high. The site is particularly photogenic after rainfall.

The waterfall can be visited year-round. Its flow is most impressive in spring (April–May) during snowmelt. In summer, the flow decreases but the site becomes a popular swimming destination with its natural pool and shaded forest areas. Autumn offers a peaceful atmosphere with seasonal colors. The site can be crowded on weekends and during school holidays.

The waterfall consists of several water falls that flow into potholes known as the "Chartreux potholes", naturally carved into the rock. At the base of the waterfall, a Chartreux cross is engraved in the stone, marking the former boundary of Carthusian monk territory. The trail follows the Diomaz stream through the forest, with several stream crossings via footbridges or at fords.

The waterfall is fed by the Arpenaz torrent, also called Nant d'Arpenaz or La Laiteuse, which descends from the Platé massif from an altitude of 1,850 m and travels 4 km before joining the Arve. The name "Arpenaz" comes from the Celtic prefix "Ar" (flowing water) and the suffix "pen" (rocky head).
The waterfall transforms into an ice climbing site in winter. Practice reserved for experienced and guided climbers. Winter access is difficult due to snow cover.
The waymarking for the alpine-pasture trail uses a dedicated logo, 'l'Alpagiste', painted on the signposts — easy to follow for walkers.
The wellness & spa area is accessible from age 16. Those aged 16-18 must be accompanied by an adult. The aquatic area (included) welcomes all ages with a paddling pool for little ones.

Throughout the route, spectacular views of the Étale massif, the XQV0Z valley and the Aravis range.

To the south, the Philibert d'Orlye promenade runs along the lake shore to Menthon harbour. Views extend to the Semnoz mountain range stretching across the water, Leschaux pass and Entrevernes mountain. During high season, the beach is supervised by lifeguards. Immediately nearby are the Cercle Nautique du Lanfonnet (sailing club) and Delphis diving school.

Today only
Today only a few wall sections of the Ruins of the castle of XQV0Z remain, along with a memorial monument erected in memory of the young victims of the Christmas 1943 massacre.

Today only a single fragment of the enclosure wall survives, leaning against the back of the presbytery in the village centre. Pierced with arrow slits and built of rough-cut stone, this short stretch of wall is the only physical reminder of the medieval fortress of XQV0Z and of the dramatic episode in XQV1Z–Savoy relations that preceded the final union of the region under Savoy.
Today only the base of the south tower and a wall section pierced by four arrow slits remain of the Saint-Michel castle, lost in the forest above the Le Lac hamlet in XQV0Z.
Today the

Today, the Glières Plateau is a natural area classified Natura 2000 and a Sensitive Natural Area, appreciated year-round: hiking and alpine pastures in summer, cross-country skiing (36 km of trails) and snowshoeing in winter. Accessible via two mountain roads (from XQV0Z/XQV1Z to the west or Le Petit-Bornand-les-Glières to the east), the site offers an escape 45 minutes from XQV2Z. Six mountain restaurants serve Savoyard specialties and alpine cheeses, some accessible on skis in winter.

Together with Albigny Beach, XQV0Z is one of only two entirely free beaches in XQV1Z. It benefits from a prime location near the water sports center, tennis courts, the Brise Glace concert hall, and the Marquisats site, which houses the superior art and design school (ESAAA). To the north lies XQV2Z's water sports base where canoes, kayaks, sailboats, and divers operate — the launch zone is off-limits for swimming.

Trail with free access year-round. Walkable in summer and winter (snowshoes), conditions permitting.
Trail with free access. Passable from snowmelt (June) to the first snowfall (October). Check snow cover.

Trails with free access year-round. Summer (June-September) recommended for the summer version; winter possible on snowshoes depending on snow cover.

Tunnel creusé chaque hiver dans le glacier, accessible depuis la télécabine. Salles thématiques sur la vie d'alpiniste et l'évolution du glacier.
Two 10-metre outdoor walls in the courtyard for sun climbing from May to September.
Two Kids courses with 8 workshops each (nets to cross, obstacles, mini-descents), accessible from age 3, less than one metre from the ground or protected by nets.
Two bars-restaurants on the base: L'Entre 2 Lacs and the Espace Le Bois aux Dames bar. Terraces with lake views.

Two bridges side by side above the Usses gorges: the Pont Charles-Albert, suspended, inaugurated in 1839, now pedestrian-only and listed as a Historic Monument since 1966; and the Pont Caquot, a concrete arch from 1928, which carries the RD1201. Owned by the XQV0Z Departmental Council.
Two complementary worlds: a VR room (HTC/Oculus latest-gen headsets, solo and multiplayer games up to 6) and a trampoline park (lanes, foam pit, dodgeball).
Two courses are offered: a
Two descents in a row, or rafting + hydrospeed with picnic on site. Around €75-95 depending on format.
Two firings: bisque then glaze
Two independent courses 2–3 m high. Continuous safety lines: children clip in at the start and can only unclip at the finish. Maximum safety.
Two intermediate stops with mountain restaurants and panoramic terraces over the massif. Ideal for a family half-day with a meal on the terrace.
Two large 150-meter zip-lines succeed one another above the waterfall at 40 meters height. Guaranteed thrills with views of the site and the Bauges Massif.

Two main routes: (1) From Les Praz de Chamonix, Flégère cable car to 1,877 m, then 2 hours' walking to XQV0Z. (2) From Tré-le-Champ car park at Col des Montets, 3 hours' hiking with technical passages (ladders). The Flégère option is more accessible.
Two medieval castles facing each other atop a hilltop, residence of Saint Francis of Sales. Panoramic view over Lake XQV0Z. Free access year-round in XQV1Z.
Two outdoor escape games lasting 1.5 to 2 hours around XQV0Z lake: riddles, observation and team problem-solving in a natural setting. From age 10.
Two possible exits along the way (Pin Sec and before the final overhang) to adapt the route to your level.
Two rival medieval fortresses facing each other at the summit of a hilltop overlooking Thonon and Lake XQV0Z, with a listed Romanesque chapel and frescoes from the 11th century.
Two roped areas with 6 autobelays for solo climbing, grades 4B-7B. Great for first-timers or for solo training.
Two separate play areas: one for toddlers and a larger one for children up to 10-12, with swings and climbing structures.
Two towers 3 and 7 meters high for free-fall jumps onto a giant air-filled cushion. Landing like on a cloud, sensations guaranteed.
Two tree cabins connected by suspended nets and a large final slide. Reserved for children over 1.20 m tall for thrilling sensations.
Two versions of the Nature Detective booklet are available for the XQV0Z walking loop: one for ages 8-10, the other for ages 10 and up. Children play accompanied by their parents.
Two-cable giant zip-line in XQV0Z: 1,200 m + 1,325 m, up to 240 m high and nearly 100 km/h. €43/person, open June to September.
Two-paddler inflatable mini-raft, more engaging than team rafting. More direct sensations with water contact, from age 14.

Urban Kart-In welcomes families, groups of friends, stag/hen parties, corporate seminars and birthdays. Visite de la chartreuse de Ripaille fondée par Amédée VIII en 1410, bâtiments actuels XVIIe-XVIIIe. Cellules des chartreux, cloître, église conventuelle. Vie monastique recluse jusqu'à la Révolution.

Visite des 4 logis du Château d'XQV0Z retraçant l'évolution résidentielle princière : Logis Vieux (XIIIe-XIVe), Logis Perrière (fin XIVe), Logis Nemours (~1535), Logis Neuf (XVIe-XVIIe). Architecture médiévale et Renaissance.

Visite des appartements du XQV0Z : résidence du duc Amédée VIII de Savoie (devenu pape Félix V) qui s'y retira en 1434 avec 5 chevaliers de l'Ordre de Saint-Maurice. Salle des chevaliers, chapelle, mobilier Belle Époque Engel-Gros.
Visite des cellules et salle de torture

Visite du XQV0Z : maison forte XIIIe-XIVe siècle fortifiée par le comte de Savoie, inscrite Monument Historique 1974 (POP PA00118354). Architecture défensive savoyarde typique conservée et restaurée.
Visite du XQV0Z : maison forte XIVe siècle, l'un des très rares bâtiments médiévaux conservés à XQV1Z. Épargnée par la Révolution française ET par l'incendie de 1840 qui détruisit la vieille ville.

Visite du cabinet de curiosités du XQV0Z : collection de 1500+ pièces rassemblées par Léon Marès - armures samouraï japonaises XVIIe, faïences, armes anciennes africaines et asiatiques, bronzes, manuscrits, ivoires, objets ethnographiques.

Water sports schools and rental shops nearby: sailing, paddle, pedalo, windsurfing, boats with or without licence, wakeboard. Adjacent water sports facility.
Water-powered sawing demonstration: the wheel is started, gears turn and a log is sawn before your eyes. Duration 45 min - 1 h.
Waterfall with natural potholes and Chartreux cross, 30 min walk from Vallon Lake. Free access year-round in XQV0Z, XQV1Z.
Waterfalls and contrasted light: a favourite spot for photographers (tripod recommended).
Watersports centre at the southern port of Lake XQV0Z at XQV1Z: sailing, wakeboard and paddle hosted with Skiwake74 and other operators, reached via the Voie verte.

Waymarked orienteering course in the forest around the ruins, ideal for getting children involved in the outing.

Well rooted in XQV0Z local life, the cinema acts as a cultural hub for the Pays du XQV1Z: mainstream programming, holiday animations and partnerships with schools (École et cinéma, Collège au cinéma). The cinema welcomes families, couples, students and film buffs in a friendly setting where comfortable seats, quality sound and digital HD projection (with laser projection where available) ensure a modern experience. Ticketing typically includes full price, reduced rate (students, under 14/18, jobseekers), the French youth Pass Culture (ages 15-20), morning shows and a season pass (5- or 10-ticket booklet) to lower the per-seat cost.

Well supervised, paintball is safe: compulsory mask, calibrated marker, initial briefing, referees on field. An impact can leave a small temporary bruise — that is expected. The junior / low-impact format strongly reduces intensity for children.
Well-developed lakeside cycle network (XQV0Z). XQV1Z route possible from Évian or Anthy. Bike parking at the club.
Well-maintained and restored alpine pasture on the path to Grand Piton. Former estate of the Pomier Charterhouse. Splendid views of the Geneva basin and Jura. Access from Beaumont or the pass.

What distinguishes Plaine-Joux from other free flight sites in XQV0Z is its unique combination of accessibility (road to takeoff), stable aerological conditions thanks to its south-southeast orientation, and one of the most beautiful alpine views from the launch area. Several professional paragliding schools are located directly on-site, ensuring optimal supervision for beginners and experienced pilots seeking to improve cross-country skills.

What remains in XQV0Z is a curtain wall set against the parish rectory, on which arrow slits can still be made out. It is the last visible vestige of the 92 m-long fortress built in 1304 by Hugh of XQV1Z, dismantled after the 1564 fire to be used as a stone quarry.

What sets Indiana'Ventures apart from many Chablais treetop parks is its unique setting beside a natural temperate mountain lake at 900 m altitude. Unlike purely forest parks, families here can alternate morning climbing with afternoon swimming without changing location. The continuous safety line system, on-site professional height safety staff, and graduated offering (from Little Kid to black course) welcome the whole family, from 2-year-old toddlers to adults seeking thrills. The address '1000 route de la plage' reflects the site's multi-activity vocation: lake, beach, forest and leisure coexist harmoniously.

XQV0Z can be visited year-round. June to September offers active pastures and clear trails. December to March is ideal for skiing. October delights with autumn colours. Avoid April-May transition period unless you enjoy late snow.

XQV0Z can be visited year-round. Winter (December to March) is the high season for the cross-country domain: 29 km of skiing trails, snowshoeing, sled dogs, sledging. Snow reserves allow early-season opening and late closing. Nordic nights (Thursday evenings, 10 km lit 6 pm–8:30 pm, €5 per person) attract experienced skiers. Spring (April–May) is a transition period: the historical trail reopens early April with the Maquis Memory building, but snow often persists through May on higher ground. Summer (June–September) offers the best conditions for hiking, mountain biking, wildlife watching and discovering the historical heritage. Alpine pastures are in flower, high-altitude restaurants open, views are clear. Autumn (October–November) is quieter: the Maquis Memory building closes end September, but the plateau remains accessible. Autumn colours of coniferous forests and low-angle light appeal to photographers. The plateau is frequented year-round by locals for endurance sports (trail, cycling) and remains a place of reflection and remembrance in all seasons.

XQV0Z can be visited year-round. Winter (December–March) for cross-country skiing and snowshoeing; summer (June–September) for hiking, mountain biking, discovering the historical heritage and observing alpine fauna within the Natura 2000 site.

XQV0Z de Bellecombe se visite en accès libre et gratuit toute l'année. Praticabilité optimale mai-octobre : sentier dégagé, vue maximale, conditions de photo idéales. Pré-saison (avril-mai) et arrière-saison (septembre-octobre) : floraison printanière, couleurs automnales, fréquentation très faible. Hiver : conditions variables (neige, verglas), sentier praticable par beau temps avec équipement adapté. DONJON CARRÉ XIIe-XIIIe SIÈCLE DE 25 M de hauteur actuelle (originellement ~30 m), DERNIER VESTIGE D'UN CHÂTEAU MÉDIÉVAL aujourd'hui disparu. ARGUMENT HISTORIQUE UNIQUE : la tour contrôlait un PÉAGE SUR L'ARVE - les marchands traversant la rivière entre la Savoie et Genève devaient s'acquitter d'une redevance. ARCHITECTURE MILITAIRE SAVOYARDE XIIe-XIIIe (plan carré, base 6 × 6 m, murs épais) comparable au donjon de XQV1Z. ENTRÉE 100% GRATUITE toute l'année. PATRIMOINE CONFIDENTIEL à 15 km d'XQV2Z, rarement signalé dans les guides traditionnels - argument distinctif pour les visiteurs curieux et les locaux du Grand Genève cherchant des découvertes patrimoniales hors des circuits classiques. Idéal pour passionnés d'histoire médiévale, d'architecture défensive savoyarde, et famille avec enfants à partir de 5-6 ans. XQV0Z des Langues se visite aux abords extérieurs en accès libre et gratuit toute l'année. Position au Port de Rives, idéale pour intégration à une balade au bord du lac XQV1Z. Été (juin-septembre) : Port de Rives animé, débarcadère CGN actif (croisières lac XQV2Z), terrasses, baignade au lido voisin. Hiver : ambiance feutrée, vue dégagée sur les Alpes vaudoises et la Suisse de l'autre côté du XQV3Z. VESTIGE EXCEPTIONNEL d'une MAISON FORTE DE LA MESTRALIE DE THONON créée en 1283 par Philippe Iᵉʳ de Savoie. ORIGINE PITTORESQUE DU NOM 'LANGUES' : redevance féodale unique en France où les bouchers de Thonon devaient livrer LES LANGUES DE BŒUF de toutes les bêtes abattues - REDEVANCE EN VIGUEUR DE 1283 À 1773 (490 ANS), abolie 5 ans avant la Révolution française. Anecdote toponymique qui marque les esprits et fait sourire les visiteurs. POSITION AU PORT DE RIVES en bord de lac XQV4Z, à 200 m du débarcadère CGN (Compagnie Générale de Navigation suisse-française). VISIBLE depuis le Funiculaire de Thonon, la Voie verte du Chablais, et les bateaux CGN. ENTRÉE 100% GRATUITE. ACCESSIBLE PMR aux abords (Port de Rives plat et goudronné). Bus Star't direct + Funiculaire à 200 m + Voie verte = idéal sans voiture. Combinable avec le Château de Bellegarde, le Musée du Chablais - Château de Sonnaz, le Château de Ripaille, ou une croisière CGN sur le lac XQV5Z.

XQV0Z des Langues tient son nom d'une redevance féodale unique : les bouchers de Thonon devaient livrer annuellement à la mestralie LES LANGUES DE BŒUF de toutes les bêtes abattues. Redevance en vigueur de 1283 à 1773 (490 ans).

XQV0Z fonctionne de mi-avril à la Toussaint, avec une amplitude maximale en juillet-août (tous les jours 10h–19h). Mai-juin et septembre offrent les conditions idéales : températures douces, fréquentation modérée, forêt verdoyante. Les week-ends et vacances scolaires zone A concentrent la fréquentation — réserver à l'avance. Le parc dépend totalement de la météo : vent fort, pluie soutenue ou orage entraînent une fermeture sans préavis annoncée sur le site officiel et la page Facebook. Consulter la météo suisse (mentionnée comme plus fiable par l'exploitant) avant de venir.

XQV0Z fonctionne toute l'année et constitue l'une des meilleures options de la rive sud-est de Genève pour les jours de pluie ou les week-ends d'hiver. La fréquentation se concentre sur les mercredis après-midi, samedis, dimanches et vacances scolaires zone A (Genève). Les matins d'ouverture (10h-12h) sont les créneaux les plus calmes, idéaux pour les tout-petits qui profitent pleinement de l'espace 0-3 ans. Les vacances d'été (1 juillet – 31 août) ouvrent du Mardi au Dimanche 10h-18h30 avec horaires variables selon l'affluence. Le parc est climatisé l'été et chauffé l'hiver, indépendant de la météo.

XQV0Z is a challenging and exposed hike, reserved for experienced hikers accustomed to mountain terrain. The final section, after the refuge, is particularly technical with scree and steep poorly-marked passages. Beginners can hike to the XQV1Z refuge (2,002 m) to enjoy the panorama without continuing to the summit.

XQV0Z is a succession of small natural pebble beaches, unmanaged and unsupervised, offering a wild atmosphere. Thonon Municipal Beach is a managed beach with pools, lifeguards, and facilities.

XQV0Z is accessible June to October depending on snow cover. In June and early July, the upper lake may remain partially iced, creating a distinctive spectacle. July and August see the heaviest traffic (averaging 1,000–1,500 visitors daily), with possible cable car queues. September is often considered the best period: stable conditions, exceptional light, lower crowds, autumn colours. Avoid weekends and public holidays in high season. Starting early (before 8 am) helps avoid crowds and allows wildlife observation in the tranquillity of dawn. In winter, access is restricted to very experienced hikers equipped with snowshoes.

XQV0Z is accessible and pleasant year-round. In summer (May to September), the shade of the forest trails offers welcome coolness during hot spells, and the pool is open. Spring and autumn are ideal for nature walks with fewer visitors. In winter, the park remains open for peaceful forest walks, the animal park and play areas. Evening activities at the water sports centre take place during summer evenings. Weekends and school holidays are the busiest periods.
XQV0Z is accessible by car from XQV1Z (30 min) or Geneva (1h30). From the village of Sixt, follow the D907A for 6 km to the terminal parking at the cirque's foot.

XQV0Z is accessible from 1 m tall (approximately age 3) for the Children's Course and Acro'Net. The Junior Course is accessible from 1m20 (approximately age 6) and the Grand Course from 1m35 (approximately ages 9–10). Adult required depending on age and height.
XQV0Z is accessible in 30 to 40 minutes on foot from Les Lindarets village (trail starts to the right of the Le Chaudron restaurant). Marked easy trail, 4.2 km round trip.

XQV0Z is accessible only during the summer season, typically from early July to mid-September. The lake is frozen or snow-covered for the rest of the year (approximately 9 months). The best period is between mid-July and late August, when residual snow has melted and the trail is well clear. In early season (June-early July), snow patches may remain on the trail, making progress more difficult. Morning light (departing early from Planpraz) offers spectacular reflections on the lake. Note: the Planpraz cable car operates on a summer schedule (typically late June to late September) — verify hours before departing and plan to descend before closing (last departure often around 5–6 p.m.).

XQV0Z is accessible to beginner hikers in good physical condition. The ascent from Petit Port is sustained (850 m elevation gain) but without major technical difficulty. The Sentier des Crêtes has secured exposed passages (cables, railings) not recommended for people prone to vertigo.

XQV0Z is accessible via several hiking routes, with the main starting points at Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce (Plateau de la Croix), Bettex via Les Communailles, Planay hamlet from XQV1Z, or Baptieu hamlet from XQV2Z. The climb is challenging and sustained, crossing alpine meadows and forests before reaching the mineral ridges of the summit. Hikers walk among summer grazing herds (Tarine and XQV3Z cows, goats and sheep) whose milk is used to make Beaufort cheese. The final section, particularly exposed from the XQV4Z refuge onward, becomes more technical with steep and rocky passages with sparse markings, requiring great caution.

XQV0Z is accessible via several routes. The departure from Plateau de la Croix (Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce) is the most direct: 4 km, 1,000 m elevation gain, approximately 3 hours ascent. From Bettex (Les Communailles): 5 to 6 hours round trip. From XQV1Z (Planay hamlet): 6 to 8 hours round trip.

XQV0Z is accessible year-round and offers different experiences by season. Summer (July–August) is ideal for supervised swimming, water activities and beach time — the site is then very busy, especially on sunny weekends. Spring (April–June) and autumn (September–October) offer a more peaceful setting for walks, cycling, fishing and picnics, with beautiful light on XQV1Z. In winter, the snow-covered lake circuit becomes a contemplative walk facing the white peaks; the lake can even partially freeze in extreme cold, offering a magical sight (however, ice is off-limits for safety reasons).

XQV0Z is accessible year-round subject to weather conditions, but May to October is the most pleasant period. In summer (July–August), the site is very busy and the waterfall has strong flow — the water is cold but the natural pools allow for refreshing swims. Spring and autumn offer fewer crowds, soft light, and changing forest colours. In winter, access depends on snow cover; the trail may be icy or impassable.

XQV0Z is accessible year-round, but the best conditions are found from April to October. In summer (July–August), the ascent is very busy, especially at weekends: prefer an early morning start (before 8 a.m.) or late afternoon to avoid crowds and heat. The forest ascent remains shaded, but the Sentier des Crêtes is exposed to the sun. In autumn (September–October), visitor numbers decrease, forest colours are magnificent and the lake view is often clear in fine weather. In winter and spring (November to March), the trail may be snowed and iced, making exposed passages dangerous without crampons. Several accidents occur each year on the ridge in winter conditions. In rainy weather, trails become very slippery (clayey ground in lower sections). The sunset from Mont Baron viewpoint is particularly stunning: many hikers camp on the old cable car platform.

XQV0Z is accessible year-round. High season runs from June to September, with supervised swimming in July–August (1–7 pm) and summer events (concerts, children's shows). In May–June and September, the site is quieter, ideal for picnics and lakeside walks. Paid activities (tree-top course, horse-riding, inflatable structures) operate mainly in summer season. In autumn and spring, the setting remains pleasant for family walks. In winter, access depends on snow conditions; the lake may freeze and activities are reduced. The site is a paraglider landing point throughout the year. Avoid peak weekends in July–August if seeking tranquillity (heavy crowds).

XQV0Z is accessible year-round. In summer (July–August), it is very busy on fine days — visit early morning for clear water and calm, or after 5 p.m. when crowds subside. Summer lifeguard supervision runs from the start of school holidays to August 31, from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m. Off-season (September to June), the atmosphere is quieter: perfect for picnicking, walking, exercising, or enjoying the lake view. The beach remains shaded by trees even in full summer.

XQV0Z is accessible year-round. Spring (April–May) and summer (June–September) are the best seasons to admire the emerald colour of the water and forest flowering. In summer, the site is very busy; prefer weekday mornings for more tranquillity. Free shuttles run from 14 June to 14 September 2025. In autumn (October), foliage turns to brilliant colours and visitor numbers drop. In winter (December–March), the lake is often frozen and snow-covered; the trail remains accessible by snowshoe from Plaine-Joux (ski station open). The light on XQV1Z is particularly beautiful in late afternoon year-round.

XQV0Z is accessible year-round. Swimming is pleasant from May to September, with cool but clear water. In summer (July–August), the site is more frequented but remains significantly quieter than managed beaches. In autumn and spring, walks along the shoreline offer peaceful atmosphere with soft light on the lake. In winter, the site remains open and accessible for walkers seeking absolute tranquility on Lake XQV1Z's shore.

XQV0Z is at 192 rue Charles Feige, right in the centre of XQV1Z village (74120), immediately next to the public car parks.
XQV0Z is based near XQV1Z, ~35 km (40 min) from Geneva via A40, ~1h from XQV2Z or ~30 min from Évian. Access to the Dranse from Thonon via the D902 to Bioge/Aulps valley. Free parking on site.

XQV0Z is beautiful from April to October, with optimal emerald colour in summer. Spring and autumn offer fewer visitors. In winter, the frozen lake and snow-covered forest create a magical landscape accessible by snowshoe.

XQV0Z is best hiked from April to October. In summer (July–August), the trail is very busy, especially at weekends and late afternoon — favour an early start or weekday visit to avoid crowds. Spring (April–May) offers lush forest and mild temperatures, while autumn (September–October) reveals the russet colours of the beech woods and exceptional light over the lake. In winter, the trail remains passable in dry conditions but becomes slippery or dangerous in snow, ice, or rain — polished limestone rocks are extremely slippery in wet weather. Avoid absolutely after precipitation or during freeze-thaw cycles. Best light hours for photography: sunrise for mist over the lake, late afternoon (17:00–19:00) for golden light on the Bauges and Beaufortain.

XQV0Z is booked online at lasermaxx74.fr or by phone — strongly recommended on weekends, during school holidays and for any group. Prices are around €9-12 per game at full rate, with discounts for multi-game packs and groups. Free on-site parking, waiting area, lockers and drinks available. Ideal in rainy weather, summer heat or winter, the activity combines well with a family outing in XQV1Z or a return to XQV2Z at the end of the day.
XQV0Z is easily reached from Geneva (~35 km, 40 min via A40 then lakeside road), XQV1Z (~75 km, 1h) or XQV2Z (~10 km). On-site parking.
XQV0Z is highly prized by XQV1Z trail runners. Loop route from XQV2Z-le-Vieux with significant elevation gain (850–900 m). Numerous paths indicating heavy foot traffic.

XQV0Z is impressive in spring (April–May) during snowmelt, with powerful flow. Summer (June–September) is ideal for canyoning and swimming in the pools. Winter offers a unique spectacle when the waterfall freezes (December–March). Autumn has fewer visitors.
XQV0Z is in XQV1Z (74000), XQV2Z. Exact address to confirm on atomeclimbing.com; access by car, bike or public transport (Sibra) from the centre.

XQV0Z is located 1.5 km east of XQV1Z. Accessible on foot or by bike via the green lane (20 minutes' walk). By car, follow the D25 towards XQV2Z. A solar boat operates from XQV3Z harbour in summer.
XQV0Z is located in XQV1Z (74150) - Base de Loisirs des Pérouses. Phone: 04 50 64 62 90. Car access recommended, free parking on site. Halfway between XQV2Z, Aix-les-Bains and Chambéry.

XQV0Z is located in the heart of XQV1Z (74000), within walking distance from the train station and town centre. Public car parks (Bonlieu, Hôtel de Ville, Courier) nearby. Exact address to confirm on latelierdesenigmes.fr.

XQV0Z is mainly open from February to October (weather dependent). High season July-August: daily opening. Low season: mainly weekends and school holidays. Check opening hours on kartingdumontblanc.com before visiting, especially in case of uncertain weather.

XQV0Z is not suitable for young children. The terrain is very rocky with exposed passages over drops (cables and barriers secure the aerial sections), rocks to step over, and steep slopes. Not suitable for strollers. Recommended from age 8–10 with some hiking experience.

XQV0Z is not wheelchair accessible for the entire route (mountainous site at 1,500 m altitude). Some facilities are accessible (restaurant, restrooms, reception area). The easy route is practicable by persons with reduced mobility when accompanied. Drop-off available before the entrance.
XQV0Z is on Chablais mountain-bike routes; signposted trails from Thonon and XQV1Z.

XQV0Z is open May through September, when animals are most active and trails most accessible. Best time to observe animals: early morning (from opening) or late afternoon, when they emerge from shade where they shelter during hot hours. In July–August, free activities in the afternoon and shuttle from XQV1Z station. High crowds in August: book in advance and arrive early for close parking. Spring (May–June): ideal for seeing newborn animals of the year frolicking. Autumn (September): peaceful, autumn colors, golden light on XQV2Z. The park will be exceptionally closed throughout 2027 for pasture rehabilitation and ecosystem preservation.

XQV0Z is open from February to October (weather dependent). Winter closure November-January. High season July-August: daily opening. Low season: mainly weekends and school holidays. Check before coming.

XQV0Z is open from late March to early November, with peak season from mid-June to late August (daily) and reduced hours in April–May and September–October (Wednesdays, weekends and school holidays). Early mornings from 10am opening are quietest. Sunny afternoons in April–May and September offer moderate crowds and pleasant forest surroundings. In July–August, proximity to La Crique beach (same site) allows combining playground and swimming in the same half-day. Bring sun cream for workshops at forest edges, and dress appropriately for weather (no swimwear in the playground). Special events (Easter, Halloween, autumn festival) require advance booking.

XQV0Z is open in summer (June 15 – Sept 15) daily from 10am to 7pm, and in winter (Jan–Mar) Monday to Friday from 2pm to 6pm. Spring and autumn: afternoon opening except Saturday. Verified on the official website on May 14, 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

XQV0Z is open two seasons per year: winter (December to April) for skiers and holidaymakers, and summer (June to August) for hikers and mountain bikers. No reservation is required (except for groups). Children under 12 must be accompanied by an adult (18 years and over). The centre also offers complementary activities: aquafitness classes (Mon-Fri 10:30 am, 45 min + 15 min hot tub), private swimming lessons, Avoriaz Quest underwater escape game, and scuba diving initiation.

XQV0Z is open two seasons: in winter (December to mid-April) for skiers and holidaymakers, and in summer (late June to late August) for hikers and mountain bikers. In winter, the centre is very busy during school holidays and weekends (check the Affluences app to avoid crowds). In summer, the atmosphere is quieter and Avoriaz resort is often empty, making for a more intimate experience. The outdoor hot tub is particularly popular in winter after a day of skiing, with views of snow-capped peaks. Aquafitness sessions at 10:30 am allow you to enjoy the pools in the morning before afternoon crowds. In season, last entry at 6:45 pm, pool evacuation at 7:15 pm.

XQV0Z is open year-round according to temporary exhibition programming (usually 2 to 3 major exhibitions per year). Peak periods occur during exhibition openings and weekends in July–August. Visiting on weekdays outside school holidays ensures a more peaceful experience. Guided family visits on Wednesday afternoons and Sunday mornings are very popular. In winter, enjoy the heating and natural light from the restored skylights. In summer, extend your visit with a stroll along the lake.

XQV0Z is open year-round and offers very different atmospheres depending on the season. Summer (June–September), the shaded trails beneath century-old trees provide welcome coolness; this is the ideal period for family walks, morning jogging and the fitness trail. The park is very busy in July–August. Autumn (October–November) is quieter, with golden colours along the Dranse. Winter (December–April), trails remain passable (cleared of snow), and adjacent facilities (outdoor ice rink, Water Park) are very active. Spring (April–May) sees the return of greenery and visitors after the ski season.

XQV0Z is open year-round with varying hours. In winter (Dec–Mar): Wed/Sat/Sun 1 pm–5 pm, school holidays daily 11 am–5 pm. In season (Apr–Aug): Mon–Fri 1 pm–5:30 pm, weekends 10 am–7:30 pm. Verified on official website May 14, 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
XQV0Z is open year-round, 7 days/week (except 25 Dec and 1 Jan). Extended hours in summer (9am-7pm). Booking recommended for groups and high season (July-August). Balaclava mandatory.

XQV0Z is open year-round, with a two-week annual closure during year-end holidays. The Wellness area and thermal cures operate Monday to Saturday, with extended weekday hours (until 7pm) allowing visits after work. The Aqua and Fitness areas remain accessible until 9:15pm on weekdays. January through March is the season for conventional cures (cure participants welcome from January 12). Free cures and wellness treatments are available year-round by reservation. Summer offers the advantage of enjoying the Thermes' private garden and outdoor relaxation areas with lake views. Book several days in advance for wellness treatments and aquabike/aquarun classes (limited to 15 participants).

XQV0Z is open year-round. Free access 1 January to 30 June and 1 September to 31 December. Paid access with lifeguard supervision 1 July to 31 August from 9:30 to 17:30. Closed 7:00 to 9:30 for cleaning in July–August. Verified on official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

XQV0Z is reached from the Petit Port car park in XQV1Z-le-Vieux (free) via the Bois Jettaz trail and the GR Tour du Lac towards Pré Vernet, then Col des Sauts and the ridge trail. Allow 2 h 30–3 h round trip. Vehicle access to Col des Contrebandiers has been prohibited since July 2024.
XQV0Z is served by LIHSA bus from Thonon (stop 'La Pinède'). In summer, CGN boats allow access from XQV1Z or directly to XQV2Z landing depending on season.
XQV0Z is served by LIHSA bus line T1 (Saint-Disdille stop). In summer, a tourist train connects Rives port to the beach. The XQV1Z cycle path (V61) also passes nearby.
XQV0Z is set in the heart of Parc des Dronières, route des Dronières, 74350 XQV1Z, halfway between XQV2Z and XQV3Z (~20 km from each).
XQV0Z is the culminating point of the Diagonale du Mont-Joly, a vertical kilometer race organized in summer and winter versions. 4 km, 1,000 m elevation gain.
XQV0Z is the only natural fine sand beach on the French shore of Lake Geneva. Its sand comes from glacial deposits reworked by winds to form exceptional lake dunes, unique in Europe.
XQV0Z is very bike-friendly. Lake greenway accessible nearby.
XQV0Z is very bike-friendly. Lake greenway nearby, bike access from the centre in ~20 min.
XQV0Z is very bike-friendly. Lake greenway nearby, bike parking close to the venue.
XQV0Z is very busy in summer — prefer spring (April–May) or autumn (September–October) to enjoy peace and changing forest colours.
XQV0Z is very cycle-friendly (lake greenway). Bike parking possible at the meeting point, but transfer to the canyon by car.

XQV0Z is worth visiting year-round. Summer (July–August) is ideal for swimming and water activities. Spring and autumn offer a more tranquil setting for walks and picnics. In winter, the snow-covered lake circuit offers a contemplative walk facing XQV1Z.
XQV0Z leads several iconic XQV1Z canyons: Angon, Montmin (around Lake XQV2Z), Grand Nant, Bionnassay, Aravis and XQV3Z valley canyons, depending on level and conditions. Selected for each group.
XQV0Z long-distance hiking trail XQV1Z at XQV2Z, XQV3Z — 207 km total · ~80 km in 74, moderate to difficult

XQV0Z majoritairement accessible PMR : ~80% en voies vertes site propre avec enrobé lisse. Dénivelé modéré (~150 m sur 40 km). Quelques sections routes partagées vélos roulants. Vérifié le 24 mai 2026.

XQV0Z mountain guides office: guided outings — hiking, mountaineering, climbing, canyoning, via ferrata, ski touring.
XQV0Z municipal beach
XQV0Z municipal beach on the shores of Lake XQV1Z with views of Semnoz, XQV2Z
XQV0Z museum in a 1705 house listed as a Monument Historique. Traditional alpine architecture, open July–August. Adult €5, free under 18.

XQV0Z ne prend pas de réservation. La billetterie se fait sur place uniquement aux horaires d'ouverture, paiement en CB ou espèces (chèques et chèques vacances refusés). Le parc n'accueille pas les groupes scolaires.
XQV0Z offers 4 "Super" courses for visitors from 1.35 m (accompanied) or 1.50 m (solo) — thrills guaranteed in the high-altitude forest.

XQV0Z offers 4 daily shows in the Eagle Stadium (1,000 seats with 400 covered). Lords of the Sky at 11:00 and 15:30: grand show with 80 raptors in free flight. Birds of the Lake at 14:00 and 17:00: fishing and diving demonstrations of aquatic raptors. All shows are included in the admission price.

XQV0Z offers a 750m outdoor track considered quite technical with good variation in driving rhythm. Located at the foot of the XQV1Z massif, it offers a unique landscape setting in France for a karting activity with exceptional views of the highest peak in Western Europe.
XQV0Z offers a free view of the Fête du Lac fireworks on the first Saturday of August, by seeking unobstructed viewpoints along the Sentier des Crêtes.
XQV0Z offers an
XQV0Z offers raft, inflatable kayak (canoraft) and hydrospeed descents on the Dranse de XQV1Z, one of the most renowned whitewater rivers in the Northern Alps.
XQV0Z offers rafting and whitewater activities on the Giffre and other XQV1Z rivers.
XQV0Z offers rafting and whitewater activities on the Giffre, Arve and Dranse rivers from its XQV1Z and XQV2Z bases, with qualified instructors and top-grade equipment.
XQV0Z offers several formats for all levels:

XQV0Z offers several packages: beginner outings for families (from 8-10 years depending on the canyon), sporty routes for fit adults, fun aquatic canyons for groups of friends or stag/hen parties, technical full-day outings for confirmed practitioners. Full gear is included in the price (5 mm neoprene wetsuit, helmet, harness, boots); you only need to bring swimwear, a towel and closed shoes that can get wet. Groups are deliberately kept small (4-8 people per instructor) for safety and quality.
XQV0Z offre des tyroliennes spectaculaires jusqu'à 400 m de longueur au-dessus du Parc Thermal de Saint-Gervais. Vue dégagée sur le massif du XQV1Z - cadre paysager unique en accrobranche.
XQV0Z on Lake XQV1Z with natural sand, lawns and views of Dents de Lanfon and Tournette
XQV0Z on Lake XQV1Z with shaded area beneath chestnut trees, XQV2Z, XQV3Z
XQV0Z on its rocky islet in the Thiou canal, emblematic monument of XQV1Z's historic centre

XQV0Z opening hours vary by season (February-October). See kartingdumontblanc.com/horaires for updated times. In case of uncertain weather, call before coming at 06 80 14 68 19.
XQV0Z opening hours vary by season: see kartingdumontblanc.com/horaires. In case of uncertain weather, contact before coming. Verified on June 5, 2026.
XQV0Z opens directly onto Lake XQV1Z, facing the CGN landing stage. Extend your visit with a walk along Évian's waterfront.

XQV0Z opens in June on weekends 11am to 6pm, in July-August daily 10am to 7pm, in spring and autumn Wednesdays, Saturdays and Sundays afternoon, and daily afternoons during school holidays. The park is closed in April and May 2026. Verified on official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

XQV0Z operates as a 4-star hotel year-round. It is not open for free tourist visits — access is reserved for hotel guests, participants in private events (weddings, seminars), and restaurant diners (by reservation). The setting is particularly popular in spring and summer for outdoor weddings. Winter offers an intimate atmosphere with fireplaces and historic halls. To discover the castle, it is necessary to book a stay or event.

XQV0Z operates only during summer season, from 1 July to 31 August. Best conditions occur from mid-July to mid-August, when the park is open continuously and stream flow is optimal for canyoning. In early and late season, some activities may only be accessible by reservation (outside continuous opening). The park offers a cool, shaded setting ideal during hot summer days. Canyoning is subject to weather conditions and water flow — in case of heavy rain or storms, certain activities may be suspended for safety reasons.
XQV0Z organizes personalized events: driving courses, corporate seminars, endurance challenges, birthdays. Track privatization possible. Contact kmb74@hotmail.fr for quotes and availability.

XQV0Z ouvre 7j/7 toute l'année. Lun 14h-19h, Mar 14h-20h, Mer 10h-12h30 et 14h-21h, Jeu 14h-22h15, Ven 14h-23h30, Sam 10h-12h30 et 14h-23h30, Dim 10h-12h30 et 14h-19h. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

XQV0Z ouvre de Pâques à la Toussaint. Juillet-août tous les jours 10h–19h, avril-juin et septembre-octobre week-ends + vacances scolaires zone A. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières et météo.

XQV0Z ouvre de mai à octobre. Juillet-août tous les jours 9h–18h30. Mai-juin et septembre-octobre week-ends et jours fériés 14h–18h. Fermé du 1er novembre au 30 avril. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

XQV0Z partiellement accessible PMR : rez-de-chaussée et entresol accessibles aux fauteuils roulants. Étages par escalier en pierre uniquement. Tarif réduit 4 € pour situations de handicap. Vérifié le 24 mai 2026.

XQV0Z peasant heritage ecomuseum since 1980, ecomuseum label 2003. 8 sites, 500 m² immersive display on rural life 1850–1950. Adult €7, free under 5.

XQV0Z prices: see kartingdumontblanc.com/tarifs. Payment accepted: cash, credit card, ANCV holiday vouchers (June 1 - August 25). Verified on June 5, 2026.
XQV0Z propose en complément une via ferrata sécurisée pour grimpeurs débutants à confirmés. Site multi-activités outdoor unique en Pays du XQV1Z - alternative ou complément aux activités glaciaires.

XQV0Z propose plus de 80 expériences VR au catalogue, régulièrement renouvelé : jeux d'action, escape games VR, simulateurs sportifs et de pilotage, expériences artistiques et culturelles. Sur stations individuelles de 9 m².

XQV0Z propose un snack salé sur place entre 11h et 13h (prise de commande jusqu'à 12h45). Les aliments et boissons extérieurs sont interdits, sauf aliments spécifiques bébé, biberons (micro-ondes à disposition), eau et fruits.

XQV0Z provides all technical equipment: wetsuit, neoprene socks, helmet, lifejacket, paddle (rafting/canoraft) or fins (hydrospeed). Bring: swimwear, towel, worn-out trainers or river shoes, water and snack.

XQV0Z présente 35 sculptures grandeur nature de dinosaures et créatures préhistoriques : T-Rex, vélociraptors, stégosaures, tricératops, dimétrodons, spinosaures, ptérosaures, du bébé dino aux adultes imposants sur 1,5 hectare de jungle.

XQV0Z rates are not publicly displayed. To learn the prices for courses, ziplines, canyoning and combined packages, contact the park directly at 06 83 19 21 18. Verified 14 May 2026.
XQV0Z regional buses lines Y91 or Y92 to XQV1Z, then free shuttle line M to the end of XQV2Z lake or Les Lindarets (check Vallée d'Aulps Tourisme for schedules).
XQV0Z resort car parks nearby.
XQV0Z runs canyoning descents in the Aravis range and around XQV1Z, led by state-qualified instructors. All levels, from family discovery to sporty adventure.

XQV0Z se vit de mars à octobre, fenêtre où le parc est ouvert le mercredi, samedi et dimanche, plus le mardi pendant les vacances scolaires zone A (Pâques, Toussaint, février, Noël). L'été (vacances scolaires d'été) ouvre du mercredi au dimanche jusqu'à 18 h. La jungle est dense et ombragée, idéale par temps chaud ; en revanche, les allées de terre deviennent boueuses par temps humide — prévoir bottes et vêtements adaptés. La dernière entrée est 1 h avant la fermeture. Le 20e anniversaire en 2026 peut donner lieu à des animations spéciales — à vérifier sur le site officiel.

XQV0Z se vit toute l'année et la fréquentation suit un rythme prévisible. Les mercredis après-midi, samedis, dimanches et vacances scolaires zone A sont les pics — arriver avant 11 h ou après 17 h pour éviter l'affluence aquatique. Les matins de semaine hors vacances (12 h–15 h, bassin natation seul) sont les créneaux les plus calmes et les moins chers (adulte 10 €). L'espace aquatique extérieur ouvre par saison (ex. 23-25 mai 2026 en haute saison anticipée, puis fenêtre estivale juin-septembre) — c'est l'argument principal pour venir en été. Le Spa est l'option d'hiver par excellence, particulièrement le soir après le travail.
XQV0Z sits between XQV1Z and XQV2Z: hikes, lakes, resorts and character villages. Easy to combine with a mountain day or a foodie stop.

XQV0Z sits in the high-altitude forest (1,600 m) of the Portes du Soleil resort — a shaded natural setting that is ideal in summer.

XQV0Z was built in 1060 by Count Gérold of Geneva. Inhabited by the de Sales family since 1559, it was the residence of Saint Francis of Sales (1567–1622) and the country retreat of Count Camille di Cavour, architect of Italian unity.

Year-round, daily

Year-round, daily (weather dependent)
Year-round, daily. Free access 24/7.

Year-round, daily. Supervised swimming July–August from 12pm to 6pm. WARNING: site undergoing refurbishment September 2025 to June 2026 — access to swimming and fishing lakes prohibited during this period; northern lake (windsurfing) and Arve embankment remain accessible.
Year-round, daily. Supervised swimming July–August only.

Year-round, depending on your interests. Winter (December-March) for alpine and Nordic skiing. Summer (June-September) for hiking, cycling and walks on the Beauregard plateau. Spring and autumn offer calm and magnificent colors.
Year-round, especially evening. After work, after an escape game, or a night with friends.
Year-round, especially in bad weather and winter. Busy on weekends and school holidays — come early.
Year-round, extended hours weekends and school holidays. See official site.
Year-round, free access day and night. Lifeguard supervision July–August from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m.
Year-round, free access. Supervised swimming July 1 to August 31, 10:30 am–7:00 pm. Verified on Thonon city website May 14, 2026.
Year-round, free access. Supervised swimming July 6 to September 1, 12pm–6pm.
Year-round, perfect in bad weather. Busy on Wednesdays and Saturdays; come early or book.
Year-round, slots on booking. See official site.
Year-round, usually afternoons and evenings. Extended weekend hours. See site.
Year-round, weather and snow conditions permitting. Recommended period: spring – summer – autumn.
Year-round, weather-dependent. Peak in summer, July-August and weekends.
Year-round, with peaks in winter (ski holidays) and summer (cool-down after hiking). Prefer morning or end of day to avoid crowds.
Year-round. Covered courts usable in winter; padel popular in all seasons. Peak booking: evenings during the week, weekends, school holidays.
Year-round. Early slots to avoid crowds. Summer all-day opening is great.
Year-round. Fond de la Combe sector temporarily closed (rockfall Sept. 2025). Cross-country domain winter subject to snow conditions.
Year-round. Great on rainy days or in the evening. Book 1-2 weeks ahead on weekends and during school holidays.
Year-round. Ideal on rainy days, afternoons (Wednesday, Saturday, Sunday) and during school holidays. Very busy in winter and on rainy weekends.
Year-round. Impressive flow in spring during snowmelt.
