Year-round. In winter (December–April): road closed after Salvagny, foot or snowshoe access only. In summer: direct car access.
Year-round. Less busy on weekdays outside school holidays (Tue-Thu). The outdoor pool is particularly appreciated in winter for the steam/cold air contrast effect.
Year-round. Peaks: Friday/Saturday evenings, Wednesday afternoons, school holidays, rainy days. Families more in the daytime, teens/adults in the evening.
Year-round. Peaks: Wednesday afternoons, Saturdays, Sundays, school holidays (All Saints, Christmas, February, spring), rainy/snowy days.
Year-round. Spring and autumn for bird migrations; summer for dragonflies and orchids; winter for stillness and wintering ducks.
Year-round. Spring for irises and birdsong; summer for the cool reeds; autumn for colours and migrations.
Year-round. Supervised swimming July and August only.

Year-round. The Spa is open continuously (maintenance closures announced on the website). Thermal treatments run from February to November depending on orientation. Magical atmosphere in winter in outdoor pools under the snow.
Year-round. Trails and green spaces accessible continuously. Paid facilities (swimming pool, ice rink, tennis) have their own seasonal opening times.

Year-round. Winter (December-March): unique atmosphere with the steamy outdoor pool under snow. Summer (June-September): sunny terraces and outdoor jacuzzi. Busiest: weekends, school holidays, bad-weather mountain days.
Year-round. Winter and migrations (March-May, September-November) for birds; summer for the cool alluvial forest.

Year-round: different disciplines by season. Summer (June-September): hiking, mountaineering, climbing, canyoning, via ferrata. Winter (December-April): ski touring, snowshoeing, ice climbing, winter mountaineering. Shoulder seasons: low-elevation climbing, hiking.
Year-round: winter for skiing (December-March), summer for hiking and cycling (June-September). Spring and autumn for peace and colors.
Yes for the footbridges: adult €5.50 to €6, child 7-15 €3, free under 7. The Chemin des Châteaux around the XQV0Z is however free to access.
Yes in most conditions. The Uffin Walking Loop is very easy, virtually flat and follows wide paths: it works well with an all-terrain stroller outside muddy periods.
Yes in summer via a forest road from XQV0Z or Mégevette. In winter the road is usually closed and access is on skis or snowshoes.
Yes on most of the route, on a smooth surface. All-terrain stroller recommended.
Yes — XQV0Z hosts a gourmet bistro and bar open with or without a gym ticket. Daily specials, coffee, local beers and sharing plates.
Yes — a dedicated area is set up for children aged 4 to 7 under parental supervision. From age 8, children can climb on the autobelay walls (minimum size required for harnesses).
Yes — partial or full hire for birthdays, seminars, hen/stag and corporate parties. Quote on request.
Yes — the gondola carries passengers both up and down. This is useful after a hike to avoid the steep descent on foot. Downhill tickets are available at the ticket office.
Yes — three options: the Chevrotin Cabin (unusual stay), the XQV0Z Cottage and the Beaufort Cottage. Booking and rates on lafermedupetitmont.fr.
Yes, 13 July to 30 August 2026 Mon-Fri — a rare summer ice rink in the Alps.
Yes, 1h-1h30 discovery sessions and weekly adult classes. Ideal to learn the basics of movement reading and falling.
Yes, 20 December 2025 to 30 April 2026 — through the ski season and shoulder season.
Yes, 7 distinct rooms; consecutive slots possible.
Yes, Ablok has a bar with drinks and snacks to relax before or after climbing.
Yes, Art of Pinball is a popular venue for birthdays (kids or adults) and original stag/hen parties. Privatisation generally possible by booking. Contact the venue for a quote.
Yes, Atome offers dedicated packages: supervised intro for stag/hen, kids' birthdays with instructor, friend challenges. Book ahead, programme on atomeclimbing.com.
Yes, Atome offers group or private intro lessons: knot, belay, falling, first routes. Booking essential, schedule on the site.
Yes, Chez El' offers birthday packages for kids (themed activity, cake possible) and original stag/hen workshops (custom piece creation). Contact the studio for a quote.
Yes, Château Bleu Aquatic Centre is independently accessible by wheelchair. Adapted equipment for pool and changing room access. Information at 04 50 87 52 30.

Yes, Curalla lends itself well to combination with the Bérard via ferrata in XQV0Z or the Saint-Gervais Thermal Park via in the afternoon, each as a half-day. Several guide offices offer this double programme.
Yes, EREEL offers multi-player sessions for up to 4-6 players (depending on the game). Ideal for birthdays, stag/hen, friend outings or business seminars. Communication via built-in mic.
Yes, Ecolorado welcomes groups (works councils, companies, clubs) up to 200 people on the Arve, 70 on the Giffre. Team-building packages, tailor-made days or weekends.
Yes, Issue Secrète offers tailored packages: privatisation of several rooms, inter-team tournament for seminars, extras (drinks, catering on request). Contact the venue for a quote.
Yes, Just-Jump non-slip socks are mandatory for jumping. They are sold on site and reusable from one visit to the next.
Yes, L'Atelier des Énigmes offers tailored packages: privatisation of several rooms, inter-team tournament for seminars, extras. Contact the venue directly for a quote.
Yes, Le Buet station car park is the official parking: 2 h free, then €3 for 5 h or €5/day. Very limited in high season — prefer the XQV0Z Express train (Le Buet stop).
Yes, Les Planards car park: 200 spaces (around 800 in summer). Rate €12/day. Free Biollay car park 5 min walk away. Shuttle bus line 14 (winter) or walk from town centre (5 min).
Yes, MTB transport is included in the specific MTB ticket for the Pléney Gondola. It is the main entry point to XQV0Z's world-class bikepark.

Yes, Onkart offers dedicated packages: stag/hen grand prix (practice, qualifying, final, podium, drinks), corporate grand prix for seminars (with meeting room and catering on request). Contact them for a quote.
Yes, Oxxy Beach, operating on XQV0Z during summer season, offers rentals of pedalos, rowboats and other watercraft. A snack bar is also available.
Yes, Savoie meat/cheese/sausage boards.
Yes, Thursday-Monday up to 100. Great for birthdays, bachelor/ette parties, seminars.
Yes, Urban Kart-In is indoor and air-conditioned, so the activity is available all year. An excellent alternative in rain, summer heat or winter.

Yes, Urban Kart-In offers birthday packages (2 sessions + private room for cake/drinks, souvenir podium) and tailored corporate grand prix (meeting room, catering, official ranking). Contact the venue for a quote.
Yes, Urban Kart-In uses 100% electric karts: no noise, no emissions, instant start-up and constant torque. A modern and eco-friendly experience without fuel smell.
Yes, Versant Nord shop (48 av. d'XQV0Z, XQV1Z, tel. 04 50 32 10 02) offers complete via ferrata equipment rental: harness, lanyards with absorber, helmet.
Yes, Vitam'Café in the aquatic area offers hot dishes, salads, snacks and drinks. Direct access from the spa without needing to get dressed. Vending machines and free herbal tea bar in the rest area.
Yes, XQV0Z Grand Casino houses the Le Trèfle restaurant, with a varied menu to extend your evening. Booking recommended at weekends.
Yes, XQV0Z SNCF station is 25 min on foot. The XQV1Z Express line serves XQV2Z.
Yes, XQV0Z accepts dogs on leash, especially on the alpine pastures where cows and goats graze between June and September. Leash is mandatory near grazing herds.
Yes, XQV0Z and the adjacent Châtaigneraie park have shaded picnic tables under chestnuts and free connected barbecues. It is very popular for family picnics.

Yes, XQV0Z has a fully dedicated children's area for ages 0-12: heated paddling pool at 32 °C, water tree with geysers and suspended water games, slides, water guns, tipping bucket. Colourful, fun and secure play area. Children under 12 must be accompanied by an adult (18 years and over).
Yes, XQV0Z has a natural pool at its base, carved by the waterfall. The water is cold (mountain water), but it is a popular spot for refreshing swims in summer.

Yes, XQV0Z is Tourism and Disability certified. A water access ramp and two amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo and Hippocampe) are provided free in July–August. Contact the lifeguards on duty for details.

Yes, XQV0Z is accessible to people using wheelchairs with assistance. The site has slopes greater than 5% but they are reasonable. Accommodations for hearing, mental and visual disabilities are available.

Yes, XQV0Z is family-friendly from Vérel (10–15 min, minimal elevation), but the final section requires holding children by the hand at all times. The trail is equipped with barriers and chains. Slippery ground; good boots essential. Angon start steeper, less suitable for young children.
Yes, XQV0Z is supervised July 1 to August 31, daily from 10:30 am to 7:00 pm, with a rescue post and lifeguards on duty. Outside this period, swimming is not supervised.

Yes, XQV0Z is wheelchair accessible with assistance. The establishment has an elevator (80×130 cm), doors ≥77 cm, adapted WC with grab bars and staff trained in disability accommodation. Drop-off available at the entrance.

Yes, XQV0Z offers a children's course with 30 stations at 2 m height, accessible from age 5 with minimum height 1.17 m. Duration approximately 40 minutes with mandatory adult supervision on the ground.
Yes, XQV0Z offers dedicated birthday packages: 2 games + private reception room for cake and drinks (subject to availability). Personalised invitations and souvenir photo possible. Book in advance.
Yes, XQV0Z offers dedicated stag/hen packages with possible privatisation from 6-8 people. Book several weeks in advance in peak season.
Yes, XQV0Z organizes custom birthdays: endurance races, podium, privatization possible. Contact kmb74@hotmail.fr or 06 80 14 68 19 for quotes and child/adult birthday packages.
Yes, XQV0Z welcomes groups (schools, companies, birthdays, bachelor/bachelorette parties) with reduced rates from 8 people. Children's birthday packages with cake and activities available by booking.

Yes, XQV0Z's outdoor hot tub is heated to 33 °C and accessible in winter and summer. It offers breathtaking views of the Portes du Soleil peaks. A unique mountain experience, particularly appreciated after a day of skiing.

Yes, XQV0Z-sur-XQV1Z Beach adjoins the water sports facility and pleasure marina. Sailing school, paddle, pedalo, windsurfing and boat rentals with or without licence, wakeboard. Diving school on-site.

Yes, XQV0Z-sur-XQV1Z Beach is wheelchair accessible independently (lawn and pathways). An amphibious bathing chair (tiralo) is available free from lifeguards July–August to facilitate water access. A companion is recommended due to gravel areas.
Yes, a bar/snack is often available to wait, take a break or share a drink between games. Relaxed and friendly atmosphere.
Yes, a bar/snack offers soft and alcoholic drinks plus snacks during the session, in a retro neon atmosphere.
Yes, a boutique offers the complete range of Saint-Gervais XQV0Z dermo-thermal cosmetics based on thermal water, as well as gift sets. Purchase possible on-site or online.
Yes, a clubhouse with food and drink welcomes players and guests.
Yes, a few alpine inns in summer.
Yes, a free car park is next to the pool. The pool is also served by several Sibra bus lines.
Yes, a free shaded car park is at the end of Chemin de Curalla in XQV0Z, on the left. Capacity limited to ~15 cars — arrive early in July-August.
Yes, a free shuttle links XQV0Z (village and Chinaillon) to the XQV1Z from 11 July to 21 August 2026. Regular departures all day. Convenient for hikers and cyclists doing one-way trips.
Yes, a game master follows each team via camera/intercom and can give clues on request. The aim is to have fun — don't stay stuck too long!
Yes, a hiking path reaches the Super-XQV0Z Gondola plateau from XQV1Z village in about 50 minutes. This is the only option outside the July–August gondola season.
Yes, a juice bar with smoothies and snacks.
Yes, a kids area is set aside. Under-5s enter free (skate rental extra).
Yes, a large free car park at the resort of XQV0Z, split between P1 Nordic and P2 Alpine. In summer, the road continues up to the Crêt de Châtillon; in winter, parking at the resort is mandatory.
Yes, a large free car park is available on site, next to the entrance of the aquatic centre.
Yes, a panoramic restaurant operates at the Brévent Cable Car summit (2,525 m). At Planpraz (2,000 m) a mountain hut restaurant is open in summer and winter.
Yes, a panoramic restaurant with a terrace facing Lake XQV0Z is at the col. Ideal for lunch after a hike or flight.
Yes, a path leads down from the XQV0Z site to the remains of the thermal baths at the bottom of the gorges. The buildings are disused and in poor condition; descend with care.
Yes, a play area for ages 3-12 (slides, swings, climbing frame, spring-mounted animals) is on site, under parental supervision.
Yes, a snack area offers drinks, pastries and snacks. Parent corner with wifi to wait during children's sessions.
Yes, a swim cap is mandatory in the sports pool. It is strongly recommended in the other pools. Caps are sold at reception if needed.
Yes, a tea room offers a tasty break during or after the visit. A shop also sells crafts and plants.
Yes, a toddler area with low depth.
Yes, a tube slide with landing pool — popular with kids and teens.
Yes, a valid Portes du Soleil Multi Pass gives free unlimited pedestrian access to the Super-XQV0Z Gondola and all other operational XQV1Z lifts in summer.
Yes, access is free year-round. Note that paths can be slippery in winter and muddy in spring after rain.
Yes, access to XQV0Z is free and free of charge year-round. Only the Mont d'Arbois cable car (to shorten the route from XQV1Z) is paid.
Yes, access to XQV0Z-sur-XQV1Z Beach is free and open year-round. Parking near the marina is also free.
Yes, access to the park is free. It's a public park managed by the city of XQV0Z and the French National Forest Office (ONF). Open year-round.
Yes, all XQV0Z instructors hold a state qualification (DEJEPS or BEES Canyon), the legal requirement in France for paid canyoning instruction.
Yes, all gear is provided: bows suited to level and size, arrows, arm guard and finger tab.
Yes, always. Visits to the Mechanical Music Museum of XQV0Z are always guided and musical: the guides play the instruments live. Allow 1 h 30 per building.
Yes, an adults-only wellness area (18+) with sauna, hammam and hydromassage showers. Access: €10 extra. Capacity limited to 20 people at a time. Direct access to the pools from the wellness area.
Yes, an annual programme offers themed guided tours (cherry blossom, rose garden, medicinal plants) by registration on the official site. Usually 1 to 1.5 hours.
Yes, an aquatic area for 1-5 year olds with water play.
Yes, an integrated bar/snack with social area — ideal to extend the session or take a family break.
Yes, an onboard video is usually offered as an option to keep a flight memento.
Yes, an outdoor mini-golf is open in summer at a modest fee.
Yes, and it's highly recommended: the DUO ticket at €9 covers both the XQV0Z AND the Abbaye Notre-Dame d'XQV1Z (14th-century painted cloister, a major listed monument).

Yes, and it's highly recommended: the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais adjoins the Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce church, a Baroque jewel listed as a Historic Monument (1726-1733). A complete cultural outing.
Yes, aquagym and aquabike classes are sometimes offered depending on the seasonal programme. Sign up at the counter or online. Programme on chatel-formedo.com.
Yes, barbecues are allowed at XQV0Z in open spaces (not under trees or near the adventure trail). Ground barbecues are strictly forbidden. Do not throw hot ashes on the ground.
Yes, being able to swim 25 metres in open water is mandatory for all whitewater activities. A certificate may be required for minors. Safety briefing before each departure.
Yes, birthday package from age 6 with an instructor, gym access and possible private snack area. Book in advance.
Yes, birthday packages with private room. Book several weeks ahead.
Yes, book online at break-out-game.fr/annecy/reserver/ or call +33 4 50 05 49 63. Weekends and school holidays fill up fast.
Yes, book online at mysteresdulac.com/reservation.
Yes, booking essential, well in advance (weather-dependent flights). Cancellation/rescheduling possible in bad weather.
Yes, booking is essential, ideally several weeks ahead in high season (Christmas and February holidays).
Yes, booking is mandatory online or by phone. Reserving several weeks ahead is strongly recommended in high season (Christmas, February, weekends, public holidays).
Yes, booking is recommended, especially on weekends, school holidays and for groups/birthdays/stag/hen. Book online on ereel.fr or by phone.
Yes, booking is recommended. Guided tours of the Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z take place in groups limited to 8 (two simultaneous groups possible).
Yes, booking is required. The centre works in time slots with a minimum number of players (often 6-8). Booking by phone or via the official website. Confirmation advised 48h in advance.
Yes, booking is strongly recommended, especially Friday/Saturday evenings, Sundays and school holidays. Book online or by phone.
Yes, booking is strongly recommended, especially in high season (school holidays, weekends). You can book online at thermes-saint-gervais.com or by phone at +33 4 50 47 54 57.
Yes, booking is very strongly recommended several days ahead in July-August and for groups (stag/hen). On weekdays in May-June or September, last-minute slots are sometimes available.
Yes, building accessible with adapted reception, changing rooms and pools. Pool hoist available.
Yes, building and indoor pools accessible.
Yes, building and pools accessible; pool hoist available.
Yes, but MTB access is suspended from 24 August 17:30 until end of season. Pedestrian trips remain available. Spectators can ride the gondola to watch the racing.
Yes, but with restrictions. Dogs are allowed on leash on the upper trails (lake circuit). However, they are prohibited on the beach, in creeks and on lakeside paths, even if leashed.
Yes, camps are offered during school holidays for children and teens, across several sessions to progress.
Yes, can be cancelled or rescheduled in heavy rain or strong wind. The instructor judges conditions on the day. Rescheduling or refund can be offered.
Yes, clay, tools and aprons are all provided. Firing (and glazing if chosen) is included. Nothing to bring.
Yes, climbing shoe and harness rental at reception. Shop or personal kit for regulars. Chalk and other consumables to buy on site.
Yes, companions can watch from an observation area, free of charge. Ideal to support a loved one or take photos.

Yes, contact with the dogs is part of the experience. The musher introduces them and explains the right gestures. Approach calmly, no shouting or sudden moves. Puppies (if present) are a hit with kids.
Yes, course access is free. Only disc rental from Indiana Ventures is paid (around €5-10) if you don't own kit.
Yes, courts (tennis and padel) are available for hourly rental to visitors, subject to availability. Online booking recommended.
Yes, daily 8.30 am - 6.30 pm, closed Tuesdays. Course conditions depend on the weather.
Yes, dedicated birthday package with pass access, private room subject to availability, cake or drinks on booking. Plan ahead in high season.
Yes, dedicated birthday packages are offered: 1 h jumping + private room for cake/drinks subject to availability, personalised invitations. Book several weeks ahead.
Yes, disc-golf-specific discs (different from regular frisbees). Indiana Ventures rents them for newcomers.
Yes, discovery sessions in French billiards are offered by club references, by reservation. Ideal to discover the discipline or progress.

Yes, dogs are accepted at XQV0Z but leashed only on high paths along the lake. They are prohibited on the recreation base itself (treetop courses, beach, play areas). Arrange secure tying if you wish to do the courses.
Yes, dogs are accepted at XQV0Z provided they are kept on lead at all times. They are not admitted to the water sports centre.
Yes, dogs are accepted on leash on the trail leading to the XQV0Z. The natural site is open access and tolerant of domestic animals.

Yes, dogs are admitted at XQV0Z but must be kept on a leash at all times. This rule is mandatory to protect the site's wildlife (nesting birds, waders, herons), which is particularly sensitive to disturbance.
Yes, dogs are allowed at XQV0Z provided they are kept on lead. The park is a shared municipal natural space with other users.
Yes, dogs are allowed at the Parc de l'Imperial provided they are kept on a lead, as in all XQV0Z's municipal parks. Please pick up after them.
Yes, dogs are allowed at the XQV0Z according to the Geopark factsheet. Keeping them on a leash is recommended out of respect for the wildlife of the site, classified ZNIEFF since 1991.
Yes, dogs are allowed at the XQV0Z on a leash. Dog swimming is prohibited, as for humans, to protect the lake's fauna.
Yes, dogs are allowed at the XQV0Z provided they are kept on a lead, as in XQV1Z's other public parks.
Yes, dogs are allowed in the Jardins de l'Europe provided they are on a lead, in accordance with the rules of the City of XQV0Z's public parks and gardens.
Yes, dogs are allowed on XQV0Z trails but must be kept on a leash, especially in alpine meadows where cattle, goats and sheep graze from June to September. XQV1Z is not located in a nature reserve.
Yes, dogs are allowed on leash at XQV0Z-sur-XQV1Z Beach. A dedicated swimming area for dogs is provided on the far right of the beach (facing the lake). Dogs are prohibited in the main swimming zone.
Yes, dogs are allowed on leash at XQV0Z. Specific regulations apply (municipal by-law available on the XQV1Z town hall website).
Yes, dogs are allowed on leash on the trail to XQV0Z. Note: goats roam freely in Les Lindarets village at the trailhead.
Yes, dogs are allowed on leash on trails leading to XQV0Z. The Grand Montoir passage can be tricky for dogs (cables and narrow passages); prefer the Petit Montoir.
Yes, dogs are permitted at XQV0Z and on the plateau, but must be kept on leash (respect for alpine herds and wildlife).

Yes, due to its low grade and fun character, the via can be very busy in July-August (up to 200 passages/day). Choose an early morning (from 8am) or late afternoon start to avoid queuing at the initial wall.
Yes, easy and family-friendly. A fun, educational visit to discover alpine nature.
Yes, especially in high season (July-August, February, weekends and public holidays). For high-mountain ascents, plan several weeks or months ahead for major objectives (XQV0Z etc.).
Yes, except in heavy frost which may make the boardwalk slippery — take care.

Yes, exemplary facilities: Unikart water-entry chair with support strap, 2 Sofao wheelchairs available free of charge, lift to dedicated changing room with shower chair and adapted toilets, 1 disabled parking space.
Yes, family-friendly early evening; kid-friendly game suggestions.
Yes, fishing is permitted in XQV0Z, which is home to Arctic char and lake trout. A fishing license is required. Contact the Chamonix Tourist Office for fishing procedures.

Yes, for children aged 8–10 and up who are accustomed to mountain hiking. The Flégère route is marked and secure but includes technical passages (metal ladders) and 570 m elevation gain. Bring hiking boots, water, and snacks.
Yes, for intro sessions all gear is provided: suitable bow, arrows, arm guard and finger tab.
Yes, for the upper section (equipped footbridge). The final descent to XQV0Z base is not recommended for children under 7: rungs on smooth wet rocks, no safety railings. Hiking boots mandatory.
Yes, free Vitam complex car park with several hundred spaces — ideal for families and groups.
Yes, free and spacious parking at XQV0Z base. At XQV1Z, parking available on site along route de Sixt.
Yes, free on site.
Yes, free on-site parking and cafeteria/terrace area to refuel between sessions, with park views.
Yes, free parking at the Col des Montets, right next to the chalet and trail.
Yes, free parking in front of the park. Easy access from A40 XQV0Z exit.
Yes, free parking in the XQV0Z business zone.
Yes, free parking is available directly on site. Car is the easiest way; bus links from XQV0Z are possible but check with the Sibra/LIHSA networks.
Yes, free parking is available in the immediate vicinity of the Thermal Baths. Access is also very easy on foot from Le Fayet SNCF station (5 min) or via the free elevator from Saint-Gervais center.
Yes, free parking on site. The farm is at the end of a small lane with little street parking.
Yes, free parking outside the ecomuseum. Access is via route de Montremont from XQV0Z (2 km from the centre).

Yes, free private parking is available at XQV0Z, subject to availability. Parking is reserved for light vehicles (no motorhomes, caravans or vans). Fitness members receive an access badge (€10 deposit).
Yes, from 13 June 2026. The Brévent Cable Car opens for hikers with access to the Grand Balcon Sud, the Brévent–Flégère traverse and Aiguilles Rouges nature reserve routes.
Yes, from 8 years for family rafting on the Arve, 12 years for the sporty Giffre. Stand-up paddle on lake is accessible from 6-7 years. Contact Ecolorado to adapt the trip to your children's age.

Yes, from age 12, minimum 1.40 m and 40 kg recommended. Children under 40 kg must be roped with passage through safety anchors by an experienced adult. Always check the child's willingness regarding heights.

Yes, from age 13 accompanied by an adult for general access. Family slots (3-12 years) exist on Sunday mornings outside holidays and certain days during school holidays. Baby sessions (6 months–2 years) with coach are offered on weekend mornings.
Yes, from age 5 with supervision. The XQV0Z trail has rocky sections and some exposed passages secured by cables. Not suitable for young children or strollers.
Yes, from age ~6-7. The tone is accessible, and watching dragonflies, frogs and water birds keeps children engaged. Great nature awareness experience.
Yes, full or partial privatisation packages exist for stag/hen parties, birthdays and businesses. Request a quote directly from the centre.
Yes, full rental: harness, shoes, chalk bag. Ideal to try without buying gear, or for visitors.
Yes, fully accessible to reduced-mobility visitors.
Yes, group and private lessons with a PGA pro plus beginner clinics. Book on 04 50 60 31 78.
Yes, group, stag/hen, birthday and corporate packages available. Privatisation possible on quote.
Yes, guided tours are provided by volunteers from the museum association, sharing their passion for local geology and prehistory. School tours by booking.
Yes, hands-on exhibits for all ages. Workshops during holidays. Free under 6.

Yes, ibex and chamois frequent the surrounding pastures, especially early in the morning. The col is also a viewing spot for the bearded vulture. Guided watching days are organised in summer by the Nature Park.
Yes, in season. Entry to the Pays de XQV0Z Heritage Museum includes a visit to the Manoir de la Tour (open in summer), an emblematic medieval residence in the historic centre of XQV1Z.
Yes, in summer the Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm offers bread-oven events — a demonstration of traditional baking and, on some days, a tasting of freshly baked bread.
Yes, in summer; pedalos, SUP and small boats from local providers.

Yes, it features three notably aerial passages: a Nepalese bridge (three-cable suspension), a horizontal metal beam and a metal walkway. The continuous lifeline ensures safety, but exposure management remains essential.
Yes, it's exclusively winter and depends on snow. It can be cancelled with insufficient snow, rain or extreme weather. Rescheduling is usually offered.

Yes, it's ideal! The XQV0Z is located in the heart of the medieval village of XQV1Z, ranked among France's Most Beautiful Villages. Allow 1 to 2 hours for the garden, then stroll through the medieval alleyways, visit the ramparts, and enjoy the shores of Lake Geneva. A full day is perfect to discover everything.
Yes, it's one of the region's flagship paragliding sites. Salève Airlines and other schools offer tandem flights from the summit — booking recommended.
Yes, kids' birthday package with a qualified instructor, gym access and possible privatisation of an area for cake. Book several weeks ahead.
Yes, lifeguards (MNS) on duty during opening hours
Yes, light food and drinks.

Yes, like all professional operators in France, XQV0Z carries professional liability insurance. Guides are state-qualified. For personal cover (cancellation, accident), your home/health insurance is usually enough.
Yes, most karting venues offer kids/teens birthday packages: 1-2 karting sessions + private room + cake + drinks + driver gift. Book 2-3 weeks ahead.
Yes, mountain restaurants and bars operate at the Pléney Gondola summit during the summer season.
Yes, multiple activities can be booked in the same session. It's a popular combo for hen/stag parties and birthdays.
Yes, next to L'Atelier des Énigmes — perfect combo.
Yes, observation areas and interpretation panels are dotted along the trails.
Yes, on a clear day. The XQV0Z offers an open view of the XQV1Z massif, which makes it one of the most popular road viewpoints in the area.
Yes, on a leash, especially around herds and livestock-guarding dogs (patous).
Yes, on a leash.
Yes, on a leash; dog swimming is not allowed at the supervised beach.
Yes, on arrival of the Débaroule at the Bossonnet you can continue with the Luge des Bois — another flagship summer attraction in XQV0Z, also included in the Pass Découverte.

Yes, on clear days, Mont Blanc is visible from the summit of Mont Baron. The orientation tables at the summit make it easy to identify the different Alpine peaks, including the Mont Blanc massif to the east.
Yes, on leash. Be careful with mountain flocks and guard dogs.
Yes, on site.
Yes, on the XQV0Z Art Nature Trail, a stop by the Bougerie pond offers 3 picnic tables — a shaded spot ideal for a family break.
Yes, on unfenced pasture areas. Respect the herds and pack out your waste.
Yes, online booking is essential, especially Friday evenings, weekends, school holidays and for evening sessions. Popular slots fill up fast.
Yes, online booking is required for all visits, workshops and birthdays. Slots are limited to keep the farm calm for the animals.
Yes, online booking is strongly advised at weekends, during school holidays and for groups or birthdays. Slots fill quickly.
Yes, online booking strongly recommended, especially Friday/Saturday evenings and for groups.
Yes, online booking strongly recommended, especially in summer and on weekends. Popular slots fill up fast.
Yes, outdoor urban games in XQV0Z are also offered.
Yes, packages available — contact the venue.
Yes, paid in season: about €7.50 adult and €4 child 5-15 (indicative prices to reconfirm).
Yes, parking at XQV0Z is free and located directly on site at the park entrance in the Rocailles forest.
Yes, parking is available near XQV0Z (dedicated car park, public car park or nearby streets depending on location). Check the conditions (free/paid) on site or on the official site.
Yes, parking is available right next to the Port des Clerges base, on chemin de Montjoux. Avoid event and regatta time slots to find a spot easily.
Yes, partially. The Frénalay loop (2.5 km, 45 min) at XQV0Z is accessible with an all-terrain stroller on a flat, wide trail. Trails to Prazon are manageable with a sturdy stroller.

Yes, partially. XQV0Z has trails partly accessible to people with reduced mobility. Vehicle access is possible to the La Châtaignière domain. Some lakeside trails are natural and not passable by vehicle.

Yes, particularly the route from Ajon. The path winds through forests and meadows, with the opportunity to observe XQV0Z cattle on the alpine pasture. The historic Way of the Cross adds cultural interest to the route.
Yes, particularly. The Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z at XQV1Z is a great hit with children: real pumps, helmets, red engines. An ideal family outing.
Yes, perfectly. The Mechanical Music Museum of XQV0Z is one of XQV1Z's most popular family outings: live music, automata, funfair. Family pass at €38.
Yes, perfectly. The Nature Museum at XQV0Z offers nature workshops during school holidays, a fascinating glass-fronted hive and naturalised animals at child height. Free under 2.
Yes, perfectly. The PAYSALP Peasant Museum at XQV0Z features an immersive scenography that children love, an interactive 1924 classroom and Absurd Game / Explor Games activities. Free under 5.
Yes, perfectly. The Wildlife Museum at XQV0Z is suited to children aged 4-12: interactive sound terminals, tracks to identify, animals at child height. Child rate €3, free under 4.
Yes, picnic tables and drinking water are available at the trail start, near the stadium car park.
Yes, picnicking on a towel is allowed. Snack bar on site and Restaurant Alexperience with a terrace facing the lake.
Yes, private court hire is possible for birthdays (kids/adults), stag/hen, outings or corporate seminars. Quote on request from the club.
Yes, professional mushers prioritise dog welfare: progressive training, regular rest, suitable diet, care. Huskies are bred for this activity and enjoy running.
Yes, provided the dog is on a lead, especially near the alpine pastures and grazing herds that line part of the Tour of XQV0Z Village.
Yes, racket rental (padel, tennis, squash) is usually available at the club for around €3-5 per session. Balls are sold individually or by tube. Bring sportswear and clean indoor shoes.

Yes, restaurant À la Belle Étoile and snack bar 'la case à Caza' are located at Beunaz Beach. You can have lunch, a snack or refreshments between climbing and swimming. Picnic areas are also available.
Yes, several MTB rental shops operate in the village and at the gondola base. Protective gear (helmet, back protector) is required on black runs and recommended everywhere.
Yes, several free parking lots (Marquisats, Colmyr, Tillier) serve XQV0Z, but they fill up quickly in summer. Alternative: Hôtel de Ville underground parking (paid), 5–10 minutes walk.
Yes, several hiking trails descend to XQV0Z (approximately 1h30-2h walk). In winter, a ski run connects the pass to the XQV1Z area (130 km of slopes total). Shuttle bus also available in season.

Yes, several hotels and restaurants at the summit: Les Rosières (family Savoyard cuisine), La Table de Marie-Ange (gourmet, 2 Gault & Millau toques), Les Sapins. All offer panoramic terraces. Some open year-round.
Yes, several intermediate exits allow you to leave the route mid-way if needed (fatigue, weather, vertigo).
Yes, several restaurants are located directly at Plaine-Joux, including La Bergerie and Lou Pachran, offering Savoyard cuisine with XQV0Z views.
Yes, several schools (Aerodream, Flyeo and others) offer tandem flights from around €100 depending on the package and flight duration. Booking advised in peak season.

Yes, several trails leave from the col: Tête des Annes (2 h round trip, 560 m climb), Refuge de Doran (3 h round trip, 780 m climb), Lac de Peyre (3 h 30 round trip). Yellow waymarking, intermediate to confirmed level. IGN map 3430ET recommended.
Yes, six autobelays are available on the indoor rope walls (8 and 10 metres). Great for solo climbing once you know how to use them.
Yes, snack and panoramic terrace.
Yes, snack bar open Wednesdays and Sundays 10am-6pm with sunny terrace overlooking the mountains. Picnic area on level 2 (lift accessible).
Yes, stag, birthday and corporate seminar packages available. Races between friends, rankings, podiums.
Yes, strongly recommended online for weekends, school holidays, birthdays and groups. Walk-in possible mid-week outside holidays but not guaranteed.
Yes, strongly recommended online, especially July-August, school holidays, weekends and for groups or birthdays.
Yes, supervised swimming in July-August at the Grangette beach; outside season the beach is unsupervised.
Yes, swim caps are compulsory in all pools. They can be bought at reception. A soapy shower before swimming is also compulsory.
Yes, swim school, aquagym and aquabike. Ask at reception.
Yes, swimming ability is usually required for all whitewater activities. Children must be comfortable in water. Check minimum age conditions (often 8-12 years depending on activity) with Ecolorado.
Yes, swimming is essential. Canyoning combines whitewater swimming, jumps and slides into pools. A declaration of ability to swim 25 m is generally required.
Yes, swimming is mandatory for safe canyoning. The required level is the ability to move in whitewater without panicking. No specific stroke is required, but good general fitness is needed.
Yes, swimming is mandatory for safe rafting or hydrospeed. The required level is the ability to float and move in whitewater without panicking. Good general fitness is also needed.

Yes, swimming school for children and adults, mini courses during holidays, aquagym (Mon-Fri mornings), aquabike (Thu-Sun), aquafit (Tue noon, Thu evening). Booking recommended: espace-aquatique@laclusaz.fr or +33 4 50 02 43 01.
Yes, the 19th-century hydraulic sawmill still operates: the water wheel drives belts and gears, and a log is sawn during the demonstration.

Yes, the Alpine Coaster runs in light rain and cold weather (partly covered track). Exceptional closure in case of high wind, thunderstorm or ice. Check live info on planards.fr or call ahead in extreme weather.

Yes, the Angon canyon is a premier canyoning destination near XQV0Z. Eight waterfalls total, with main XQV1Z (60 m rappel) as the finale. Accessible with professional guided instruction. Practiced in summer; Ice Canyoning possible in winter with appropriate gear.
Yes, the Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z trail game is designed for families with accompanied children from age 6-7. The 3 km route has no technical difficulty, and is fun and educational.
Yes, the Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z trail game is designed for families with accompanied children from age 6-7. The route is flat or gently rolling, with no physical difficulty, fun and educational.

Yes, the Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z trail game is part of a collection of four routes in the Chamonix Valley: XQV1Z, XQV2Z, Les Bois de Chamonix and Argentière. You can chain the routes using the XQV3Z Express.
Yes, the Boucles du Coteau are ideal for families: the green loop is easy and short, and the woodland terrain is shaded in summer. Dogs are allowed on a lead.

Yes, the Buvette de la Cascade de Bérard, at the end of the route, offers drinks, snacks, croque-monsieur and homemade tarts on a terrace facing the waterfall (open in season, generally 10am-6pm weather permitting, mainly cash payment).
Yes, the Casino of XQV0Z has three restaurants: Le Safran (gastronomic), Le Paradise (bar-brasserie) and Le Maloja (restaurant). Booking recommended, especially for Le Safran at weekends.
Yes, the D909 over the XQV0Z stays open for most of the winter. It may be closed exceptionally during heavy snowfall (about 5 days a year on average). Check road conditions before travelling.
Yes, the Dronières Lake has two free car parks: the tennis car park (start of the lake loop) and the pool car park. Coach parking is also available.
Yes, the Débaroule at XQV0Z is designed for families with children from age 3-4 (accompanied). Easy descending trail, child-friendly playful modules and wooden balls provided.
Yes, the Lac du Môle loop has been adapted to be accessible to people with reduced mobility and to prams — a rare path in XQV0Z suitable for all ages. Checked on 10 June 2026.
Yes, the Lake XQV0Z Ecomuseum is very family-oriented: playful trails for ages 2-3 and 3-6, puzzle-games for ages 6-10 and 10+, outdoor puzzle trail. Attractive family ticket at €22.
Yes, the Plaine-Joux paragliding takeoff area is accessible to supervised beginner pilots, with stable aerological conditions. The site also welcomes experienced pilots for cross-country flying.
Yes, the Refuge de l'Abricotine and Refuge des Brochaux are located immediately near XQV0Z and offer food and lodging, open in summer and winter.
Yes, the Restaurant La Colombière sits at the top of the col. Open June to September, it serves Savoyard specialities with a panoramic terrace. A coffee stop or a full meal are both possible.
Yes, the Ruins of the castle of XQV0Z can be visited with the family. The ground can be uneven around the remains: bring closed shoes and the usual care for young children near the walls.
Yes, the Secrets de fées trails are passable in winter with snowshoes, depending on weather conditions and snow cover on the Plateau des Moises. Check with the Foyer des Moises before setting off.
Yes, the Super-XQV0Z Gondola gives access to the 2-hectare alpine lake in July and August only. It is also reachable on foot from XQV1Z village in about 50 minutes.
Yes, the Tavern La Croix Blanche is located on the XQV0Z estate. In summer, the Priory Menu is offered for lunch. A gift shop completes the offering.
Yes, the Tour of Lac du Môle is a very easy family loop, ideal even for very young children: pram-friendly, flat path, lake to observe (birds, dragonflies).
Yes, the Uffin Loop welcomes dogs on lead. Please respect the crops and any pastures crossed along the way.
Yes, the Vitam complex includes a water park, spa, climbing gym, restaurants and a shop on the same site. Trampoline + spa or + water park combos are very easy and perfect for a full day.

Yes, the XQV0Z 3D application (augmented reality) is included in the admission price for XQV1Z. Visit with virtual reality headset or digital tablet to discover the abbey reconstructed in the 16th–18th centuries.
Yes, the XQV0Z Art Nature Trail is designed as a short, flat family walk through shaded woodland, with a pond, picnic tables and the chance to create your own land-art works. Ideal with children.

Yes, the XQV0Z Chalet-Refuge at 2,002 m offers traditional Savoyard cuisine from mid-June to late September. Panoramic terraces, Savoyard specialties, daily specials. Open daily in summer season. Reservation recommended: 04 50 93 10 10.
Yes, the XQV0Z has free parking on site, accessible via the castle avenue. Parking is limited in high season — early arrival recommended on July–August weekends.
Yes, the XQV0Z has two separate play areas — one for toddlers and one for older children — plus a biotope pond that children enjoy watching.
Yes, the XQV0Z is historically crossed by the Tour de France, most recently in 2020 according to available sources. It is also part of the Time XQV1Z XQV2Z cyclosportive.

Yes, the XQV0Z-XQV1Z-XQV2Z SNCF station is 400 m away (5 min walk). XQV3Z-Saint-Gervais regional rail link. SAT Autocars XQV4Z buses serve the Gare and Charly Mottet stops. Free parking on site if you come by car.
Yes, the activity is weather-dependent. In case of strong wind, rain or storms, the flight is rescheduled or refunded under the school's terms. Confirmation 24-48h before the flight.

Yes, the alpine-pasture trail is suitable for families with children from age 7–8 who are confident walkers. 6.5 km and 330 m of climb, with no technical difficulty. Water and walking shoes recommended.

Yes, the area is wheelchair accessible with lift, adapted changing rooms, accessible toilets and assistance available. The balneotherapy pool has easier access. Free companion entry upon presentation of disability card.
Yes, the auditoriums are equipped for people with reduced mobility (PRM spaces, step-free or lift access, adapted toilets). To confirm for a specific screening, contact the cinema.
Yes, the auditoriums are fitted for visitors with reduced mobility (dedicated spaces, level access or lifts, accessible toilets). To confirm for a specific showing, contact the cinema.
Yes, the birthday package includes 1 hour of jumping + a private room for cake and drinks. Personalised invitations and a souvenir photo. Book several weeks ahead.

Yes, the centre has a dedicated paddling pool for toddlers and offers baby-swimmer sessions from 6 months. Free for under-5s. In summer, the outdoor area offers a fun splashpad suitable for young children. Changing rooms with baby-changing tables.
Yes, the centre has a wellness area with sauna, hammam and sensory showers, available with an extra fee. Reserved for teens and adults from age 16.
Yes, the centre has adapted changing rooms and a pool hoist for people with reduced mobility. Contact reception in advance to prepare your visit.

Yes, the cinema regularly programmes family-friendly films (Wednesday, Saturday, school holidays) and specific operations (Ciné-goûter, École et cinéma, holiday activities). The family programme is published weekly.

Yes, the cinema regularly programmes films for young audiences (Wednesday, Saturday, school holidays) as well as specific operations (Ciné-goûter, École et cinéma, holiday events). The young-audience programme is published each week.
Yes, the circuit is self-guided and intuitive. An explanatory booklet for the Roman-Irish circuit is available at reception. Staff are available to guide you through the different areas.
Yes, the club has a friendly family vibe and offers junior packages. Discovery from age 6-7.
Yes, the club is FFT-affiliated and organises tournaments and inter-club competitions throughout the year, plus friendly padel tournaments. Calendar on the site.
Yes, the club offers group intro lessons (~€15-20/person) and private lessons with a coach to progress faster. Children/teen camps during school holidays.
Yes, the club offers racquet and ball rental (~€5). For beginners, this is ideal to try without investing in your own equipment.
Yes, the club runs group introduction lessons for adults and children, plus private lessons. Ideal to discover padel or progress at tennis/squash. School-holiday camps available.
Yes, the establishment renovated in 2018 has adapted facilities. It is recommended to contact the Thermal Baths in advance for any specific assistance request.
Yes, the facility is wheelchair-accessible: equipped changing rooms and pool access. A pool hoist is available on request.
Yes, the flat, short trail is perfect for children. The playful panels capture their attention and the ponds harbor frogs and dragonflies that are easy to spot.
Yes, the format is designed to be family-friendly. Exact terms (minimum age, group size, duration) are on the official site.
Yes, the gorge can be visited even in the rain. Sturdy shoes are strongly advised.
Yes, the hotel-restaurant Les Rochers Blancs offers 16 rooms at the summit of XQV0Z, open in season. There is also the Éco-Bivouac with panoramic domes for a unique mountain night.
Yes, the large lawns and picnic tables work well for birthdays; come early in summer.
Yes, the laser game offers dedicated birthday packages: 2 games + private room (subject to availability) for cake and drinks, personalised invitations and souvenir photo. Book several weeks ahead.
Yes, the outdoor jacuzzi is heated and open year-round. It is particularly spectacular in winter, in the snow, with views of the Bossons glacier.
Yes, the park is accessible year-round. In winter, access depends on snow and the state of the Semnoz road. Check conditions before going up, bring suitable shoes.
Yes, the park offers dedicated birthday packages: 1h or 2h session + private room for cake and drinks. Personalised invitations and souvenir photo included. Book several weeks ahead.
Yes, the park regularly welcomes stag/hen parties with dedicated slots or full privatisation. Dodgeball, ninja and trampolines are perfect for memorable moments with friends.
Yes, the park uses Smart-Belay: lanyards that prevent unclipping both carabiners at once, ensuring continuous safety. Mandatory briefing before each session.
Yes, the pass road is open and cleared year-round. Free parking at the summit, but beware: it can be saturated during high season (winter weekends and nice summer days).
Yes, the pilot holds an FFPLUM (French Microlight Federation) licence, experienced and responsible for flight safety.
Yes, the plateau is free and open. Only the Nordic pass in winter is payable (XQV0Z Nordic).
Yes, the pool has a retractable roof: the glass canopy slides back in good weather, turning the centre into a semi-outdoor pool in summer.
Yes, the resident club VVA Natation runs the swimming school, baby swim, improvement courses and training. Aqua aerobics is offered as group classes.
Yes, the setting suits safe family introduction to fishing.
Yes, the short loops and gentle terrain are ideal for families. Kids will see cows, marmots and wildflowers.
Yes, the site can be privatised for weddings, seminars or receptions. Unique setting in XQV0Z. Book several months ahead.
Yes, the site is in a nature reserve: stay on trails, no picking, dogs on leash, no fire, leave no trace.
Yes, the spa has a restaurant and offers an Italian aperitivo included in the path. Lunch or dinner available on site on selected days.
Yes, the swim school offers group or private lessons for children and adults (beginners, improvers). Baby swim and water garden for the youngest. Online or on-site enrolment.
Yes, the swimming zone at XQV0Z-sur-XQV1Z Beach is supervised by lifeguards in July and August only. Outside summer season, swimming is unsupervised.

Yes, the top of the col offers a safe flat area with parking, picnic tables and an easy trail to the Tête des Annes (30 min one way). Note: narrow winding road on the way up — bring travel-sickness aids if needed.
Yes, the trail is accessible in dry weather. Avoid wet periods as paths can become muddy and difficult to navigate.
Yes, the via ferrata is very busy in July-August, especially afternoons. Arrive before 9 am to enjoy it peacefully. Many supervised and school groups during the day.
Yes, the walk is well suited to families. Bring binoculars and keep quiet.

Yes, the workshop offers gift cards to give an introduction or a half- or full-day course. The card is valid for around 1 year. Ideal for a birthday, Mother's or Father's Day, Christmas or a wedding anniversary.
Yes, themed cabins with bespoke entertainment.
Yes, this via ferrata is very popular in July–August. The car park can be full: arrive early in the morning or consider parking at the Col de la Colombière car park (10 min extra walk).
Yes, three escape routes allow you to exit after each section. You can do only section 1 (D, 1 h) or the first two sections (D + D+, 2 h 30).
Yes, three escape routes allow you to exit after each section: after Le Cabri, after Le Chamois (Le Vionnet exit) and after Le Bouquetin. You can thus adapt the route to your fitness.
Yes, to climb the rope routes independently (lead or top-rope with a partner) a climbing passport or in-house validation is required. Otherwise, take a discovery session first.
Yes, two 150-meter zip-lines soar above the waterfall at 40 meters height at XQV0Z. Minimum weight required: 30 kg. Zip-lines cost extra beyond the cave tour admission (inquire on-site).
Yes, two free car parks flank the XQV0Z: one on the XQV1Z side (the larger) and one on the Allonzier-la-Caille side. The Allonzier car park is smaller but shadier in the afternoon.
Yes, two outdoor pools including an Olympic 50 m, open in summer.
Yes, usually paid by weight. Some areas may be no-kill only.
Yes, visiting the Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm in XQV0Z is only possible by booking with XQV1Z Tourist Office (+33 (0)4 50 34 40 28 or ecomusee.closparchet74@gmail.com).

Yes, walk-in access is possible, but online booking is strongly recommended during peak season (July-August, spring and autumn weekends) to guarantee your time slot. The park manages visitor flow by time slots for safety and comfort reasons.
Yes, weekly group classes year-round for kids (4-5, 6-8, 8-10, 10-12), teens and adults. Week-long camps during school holidays from €135.
Yes, with insufficient snow, the activity may be cancelled or rescheduled. The decision is made the day before. In that case, your booking is generally refunded or moved. Follow kennel updates.

Yes, with restrictions: maximum 1 wheelchair per train, best accessed from Saint-Gervais station (not Le Fayet Thermal Park). Col de Voza is recommended for wheelchair users; Bellevue is difficult. Advance booking by email is mandatory.
Yes, €4-5 (all sizes) and helmets €2.
Yes, €4.50, all sizes available. Bring good socks.
Yes. A café and snack bar (Transat restaurant) operate at the XQV0Z during the summer season, approximately mid-April to mid-September. Contact the tourist office for exact opening times.

Yes. A complementary trail 'In the footsteps of the Mont Sion granite cutters' extends the XQV0Z trail with a loop of about 10 km towards Charly, Presilly and the Mont Sion pass, linked to the history of the 19th-century Piedmontese and Lombard stonecutters.
Yes. A dedicated car park is located right next to the XQV0Z, reachable by car up to the foot of the site from XQV1Z via the La Chèvrerie road.
Yes. A drinks stand (Buvette des Criques) and snack bar open in summer at XQV0Z. Several picnic areas also allow you to bring your own food. Tables and benches are available around the lake.
Yes. A food truck, picnic tables and an electric barbecue are near Lac du Jaillet. A mountain restaurant is at La Ravine, 800 m from the Jaillet Gondola arrival station.
Yes. A kayak, paddleboard, and roller rental operator is located immediately next to Albigny Beach on the XQV0Z side. Also a mini-golf between the beach and Impérial Palace.
Yes. A natural pool at the base of the XQV0Z allows for swimming in summer. The water is cold. A small stream sheltered by the forest is also accessible for children.
Yes. Access to XQV0Z is free and cost-free year-round. The car parks at Petit Port and XQV1Z-le-Vieux stadium are also free.
Yes. Access to XQV0Z is free and costs nothing year-round. The Nyon cable car parking is also free. Only guided canyoning activities are paid.
Yes. Access to XQV0Z is free and open year-round. It is a hiking trail in free access with no admission fee or toll.
Yes. Access to XQV0Z is free and open year-round. Parking at Parc Biolley in Chedde is also free.
Yes. Access to XQV0Z is free and open year-round. Swimming is also free and unsupervised.
Yes. Access to XQV0Z is free and open year-round. The adjacent parking is also free.
Yes. Access to XQV0Z is free year-round, as is the Bossonnet parking located near the lake.
Yes. Access to the XQV0Z is free and at no charge year-round, including visits to the Romanesque chapel and the ruins of both fortresses.
Yes. Access to the XQV0Z is free and open year-round. The Grépon car park (central Chamonix) and the tunnel access road car park are also free.
Yes. Access to the XQV0Z is free and unrestricted year-round, as is parking on site.
Yes. Access to the XQV0Z is free year-round, as is the parking (Nant Sec parking in Salvagny or parking at the base of the waterfall in summer).

Yes. Admission to XQV0Z, the beach, swimming, and green spaces is free year-round. Certain on-site activities are paid: tree-top course, equestrian centre, inflatable structures, Big Air Bag, kayaking, paddleboarding.
Yes. Albigny Beach has an accessible water entry ramp and free flotation chairs in July–August.
Yes. Albigny Beach has three free sanitary blocks (one at each end, one on the other side of the road) and two blocks of four free outdoor showers.
Yes. Albigny Beach is supervised by lifeguards in July and August, daily from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m. Outside this period, swimming is unsupervised.

Yes. All courses at XQV0Z are equipped with continuous safety lines: the user clips in at the start and can only unclip at the finish. Little Kid courses are supervised by an accompanying adult. Professional height safety staff are on-site.

Yes. Alpine Coaster from age 3 accompanied by an adult, solo from 1.35 m. Amusement park: rides from age 1 (carousels, diggers), mini-karts and slides from age 3, trampolines from age 4, splash boat and loops from age 7. Free playground on site.
Yes. An alpine refreshment bar operates by the XQV0Z, open from Pentecost to late September according to the Tourist Office. Precise hours are not published on an official source.
Yes. An audio tour in French and English accompanies the Follow the Fly trail, downloadable free from the izi.TRAVEL app - the Chéran itself guides your steps through Alby's history.

Yes. An orientation table is installed on the old cable car platform at 1,254 m, ten minutes before XQV0Z's summit. It helps identify surrounding peaks: Semnoz, Tournette, Bauges, Aravis, Mont Blanc, and Jura.
Yes. Animals are allowed at the Parc des Dronières and on the Dronières Lake loop. Follow standard rules: leash recommended, clean up after your dog, and keep clear of the pool area in season.

Yes. Beyond the XQV0Z, the trail continues to Gouille des Fours (2,030 m), an altitude lake that is the waterfall's source. Allow 5 to 6 hours round trip from Lintre, difficult level, for experienced hikers.

Yes. Col des Pitons is a starting point for several hikes: to Grand Piton (45 min one-way), to Thuile alpine pasture, or along the GR Balcon du XQV0Z. Marked trails, moderate elevation gain but uneven karst terrain. Hiking shoes recommended.

Yes. Col des Pitons offers panoramic views of XQV0Z to the east, the Geneva basin and Lake XQV1Z to the north, and the Jura chain to the west. The most spectacular views are at Grand Piton (45 min hike from the pass), where an orientation table is installed.
Yes. Dogs are accepted at XQV0Z and on trails leading to the lake, provided they are kept on lead.
Yes. Dogs are accepted at XQV0Z, provided they are kept on leash on the trail.
Yes. Dogs are accepted on XQV0Z on leash, due to the presence of livestock on alpine pastures and protected wildlife (lynx, wolves, many nesting birds).
Yes. Dogs are accepted on lead at XQV0Z. Restrictions may apply to certain activities (swimming beach, adventure course) — check with each provider.
Yes. Dogs are allowed at XQV0Z and on surrounding trails, but must be kept on leash (due to livestock and wildlife).
Yes. Dogs are allowed at XQV0Z, but must be kept on a leash. The site is a Sensitive Natural Area with remarkable wildlife.
Yes. Dogs are allowed at the XQV0Z but must be kept on a leash out of respect for other visitors and local wildlife (site at the entrance of a natural reserve).
Yes. Dogs are allowed at the XQV0Z in XQV1Z provided they are kept on a lead, in line with public-park rules.
Yes. Dogs are allowed at the XQV0Z provided they are kept on a leash throughout the site (trails, ruins, chapel).
Yes. Dogs are allowed at the XQV0Z. Keeping them on a leash is recommended on the deck and walkways for safety.
Yes. Dogs are allowed on leash at XQV0Z and its trails. Since the site is natural and unsupervised, please respect its cleanliness.
Yes. Dogs are allowed on leash at XQV0Z up to the Prazon refreshment stand. Beyond that, they are prohibited (nature reserve regulations).
Yes. Dogs are allowed on leash on XQV0Z trails. Bring water (no water sources during the ascent) and supervise your dog on exposed rocky passages.
Yes. Dogs are allowed on leash on the trail leading to the XQV0Z.
Yes. Dogs are allowed on the trail to XQV0Z, provided they remain on leash. The site has been a designated Natural Site of France since 2024; environmental regulations apply.
Yes. Dogs are permitted on the XQV0Z trail but must be kept on a leash, due to livestock in the pastures crossed (cattle, sheep).
Yes. Dogs are permitted on the trail leading to the XQV0Z, provided they are kept on lead throughout the walk.
Yes. Entry to XQV0Z XQV1Z is strictly limited to adults (18+) not on the gaming-exclusion list, on presentation of valid ID.
Yes. Entry to XQV0Z is free and unrestricted year-round. On-site parking is also free (blue zone 4 hours).
Yes. Entry to XQV0Z is free year-round. Some activities on site are paid: open-air swimming pool, tennis, adventure course, paddle rental.
Yes. Entry to the XQV0Z is free and open year-round. Only the guided Via Corda organized by the XQV1Z Guides Bureau is a paid activity.
Yes. Every visit to the XQV0Z ends with a tasting of XQV1Z cheese paired with a glass of Savoie wine or local apple juice, included in the entry price.
Yes. Family zone with gentle-slope access, sandpit, free for under-5s. Changing tables, possibility to warm up meals, high chairs available. Lifeguard-supervised swimming in season.

Yes. Guided tours of the XQV0Z are offered by Évasion XQV1Z on reservation (Bertrand Prieur, heritage guide, tel. 06 56 83 94 27). Tours available for school groups, families and individuals with medieval treasure hunt for children.
Yes. Harness, via ferrata lanyard and helmet rental at Bouvet Sport (XQV0Z), Trincaz Sports (XQV1Z) or Arbr'acadabra adventure park. Booking advised in high season.
Yes. In winter (December–March), Mobil'Alp Glières shuttle from XQV0Z Bus Station on Wednesdays, Saturdays and Sundays. Fare: €1.50 single, €3 return.

Yes. In winter, the Confins plateau becomes a cross-country skiing area with 65 km of groomed trails (classic and skate). The cross-country skiing area operates from December to March depending on snow conditions.
Yes. La Table du Château offers gourmet French cuisine made from local and seasonal products. The restaurant is open by reservation for hotel guests and private events.

Yes. Lake 3 (515 m long) is one of the best windsurfing and wingfoil spots in XQV0Z. The southern section of the lake is reserved for windsurfers, with pontoons and launching ramps. The northern section is dedicated to category 2 fishing.
Yes. Lake Beunaz offers a supervised swimming zone in July-August with pool fed by lake water, diving boards, slide and inflatable structures. The water is naturally temperate around 16 °C.

Yes. Les Planards Restaurant (terrace facing Mont Blanc) serves Savoyard dishes, burgers and snacks year-round. Bar open at lunch and après-ski. Picnic areas also available. Reservation recommended for evening groups.
Yes. Mont Veyrier and XQV0Z are connected by a ridge trail of around 15 minutes. From XQV1Z, continue northwest — allow 30 minutes additional round trip to reach Mont Veyrier (1,291 m).

Yes. Natural pools downstream of XQV0Z allow refreshing swims in summer. Warning: the water remains very cold even in July–August, as it comes from snowmelt from Mont Nyon. Swimming in the main basin of the waterfall is discouraged (strong current).
Yes. One section of XQV0Z, marked with official signs, is reserved for naturist practice. The area is secluded and discreet, consistent with the site's preserved character.
Yes. Outside school holidays, the visit to the Sacred Art Museum of XQV0Z is by appointment via the Tourist Office. During holidays, public visits on Friday at 3 pm.
Yes. Partial or full private hire of all 8 simulators for stag/hen, birthdays, seminars and team-building. Detailed quotes via emotion-concept.fr.

Yes. Planfait municipal car park is located immediately adjacent to XQV0Z. This car park is free October to April, and paid 2 May to 30 September under municipal order (linked to summer tourist traffic at the adjacent paragliding site).

Yes. Planpraz (2,000 m), reached via the Brévent Cable Car, is a recognised paragliding launch site with direct Mont Blanc views. Tandem operators are active in season. Contact the Chamonix tourist office.

Yes. Refuge du XQV0Z, located immediately next to the lake at 2,352 m elevation, offers food and lodging June to September. Cash payment only. The current refuge was rebuilt after the 1986 avalanche that destroyed the original building.

Yes. Refurbishment works at the XQV0Z are underway from September 2025 to June 2026. During this period, paths around the fishing and swimming lakes are closed, as are the north and central car parks. The northern lake (windsurfing) and the Arve embankment remain accessible. For your safety, respect barriers in place.
Yes. Several alpine farms at the XQV0Z sell directly in season. Reblochon is the historic cheese of the Aravis range.
Yes. Several free car parks at Chinaillon provide access to the XQV0Z trail start: the base of slopes car park and the Mottaz/Arbelay car park.

Yes. Several independent providers offer pedal boat, small electric boat, stand-up paddle and windsurfing equipment hire at XQV0Z. Rentals are arranged directly on site, at the miniature port or on the beach.
Yes. Several paragliding schools operate from Plaine-Joux: Aerofiz, Élévation Parapente, De Cimes en Ciel. Tandem flights with qualified instructors, year-round by reservation.
Yes. Several picnic areas equipped with tables, benches, bins and sanitary facilities are available free of charge at XQV0Z. Shaded setting between the two lakes.

Yes. Several trails start from the XQV0Z, including the Croix de Fer and chalet du Curé loop on the La Giettaz side, and the climb to the Porte des Aravis. Check with the tourist office for conditions and waymarking.
Yes. Since the 18th century, XQV0Z has been a popular stop for travelers journeying from Geneva to Chamonix. They organized departures to arrive at sunrise and admire the rainbow in the mist.
Yes. Swimming at XQV0Z is supervised by lifeguards from 1 July to 30 August, daily from 11:15 AM to 5:45 PM. Outside this period, swimming is free but unsupervised.
Yes. Swimming at XQV0Z is supervised by lifeguards in July–August, daily from 1–7 pm. Shallow areas are cordoned off for young children.
Yes. Swimming at XQV0Z is supervised in July and August only, from 12 noon to 6 pm, on the sandy beach with two delimited zones (
Yes. Swimming at lake 2 (swimming lake, 450 m long) is supervised by lifeguards in July and August from 12pm to 6pm. Outside this period, swimming remains permitted but unsupervised.
Yes. Swimming is supervised in one of the two lakes at XQV0Z from July 6 to September 1, 12pm to 6pm. Outside the supervision period, swimming is possible but unsupervised.

Yes. The 11th-century Romanesque chapel at the XQV0Z is open for free visits year-round. You can admire Savoy's oldest fresco preserved in the barrel-vaulted apse. The chapel remains a place of worship and Salesian pilgrimage.
Yes. The Bike Park has green and blue trails for beginners, a skills area, and guided lessons. Black runs and natural lines are for experienced riders.
Yes. The Birds Trail and the Col de Bassachaux car park are free of charge throughout the summer season.
Yes. The Calvary Ermitage Museum offers guided tours of the Calvary site (€5.50) by a heritage mediator of the Pays du XQV0Z, with usually closed chapels opened.

Yes. The Calvary of XQV0Z, presented at the Calvary Ermitage Museum, has been listed on the Supplementary Inventory of Historic Monuments since 1988 and features on the European register of Sacred Mountains.

Yes. The Camille Dunant refuge, located on the summit of XQV0Z, offers overnight stays in dormitories (2 to 18 beds) from mid-May to mid-September. Reservation required via refugedparmelan.ffcam.fr. Bivouacking available nearby under conditions.
Yes. The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z features enlarged mechanical models, workshops during school holidays, and free entry for under-12s.
Yes. The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z is accessible to people with reduced mobility, in the renovated Espace Carpano & Pons.
Yes. The Cobbler's Museum at XQV0Z is a short, hands-on visit for families: tools, machines, old shoes. To combine with the medieval town.
Yes. The Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z has free on-site parking at the Domaine de Guidou. Easy parking in any season.
Yes. The Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z is 5 min by car from XQV1Z beach on Lake Geneva, perfect for a full-day family outing.
Yes. The Discovery Walking Trail of XQV0Z Village is an easy walk, ideal for a family outing: a short, easy route with observation and drawing activities that make the walk fun.

Yes. The Dronières Lake is classified as a closed water and managed by the association Les Eaux Closes. A specific permit is required (€13/day, €80/year in 2025). The French federal FNPF / AAPPMA card is not valid here. Permits are sold at La Fleuristerie and Gamm Vert in XQV0Z, or online via HelloAsso.
Yes. The Eterlou Trail is free and open throughout the summer season, like all interpretive trails in XQV0Z.

Yes. The Eterlou Trail is specifically designed for families with young children: short, fun, dotted with around ten panels on wildlife and plants. Keep an eye on children near the river that runs alongside the trail.
Yes. The Ferme de Chosal animal trail is accessible via the educational farm ticket office: €9.80 adult and €7 child, free under 2. Reduced rates for groups, schools and visitors with disabilities.
Yes. The Fiz Natural Reserve to the north prohibits overflight below 300 m/ground. Flight inadvisable in strong westerly winds. Flight priorities must be respected as the site is heavily used.
Yes. The Follow the Fly trail has free access year-round. The izi.TRAVEL audio tour is also free.
Yes. The Follow the Fly trail is very family-friendly: short route, riddles at each stop, and during the Cobblers' Museum opening hours, a children's hunt for Saint Crispin's hammer.
Yes. The Fox Trail can be snowshoed in winter, depending on snow conditions. The wide, gently sloped track is well suited to beginner snowshoers.
Yes. The Fox Trail is wide, gently sloped and lined with educational panels on wildlife, flora and geology: it suits families with children old enough to walk.
Yes. The Granges de Servette Museum at XQV0Z is 10 min from the medieval village of XQV1Z (Plus Beaux Villages de France), ideal for a culture-and-heritage day on the shores of Lake Geneva.
Yes. The Granges de Servette Museum at XQV0Z offers a varied tour (contemporary art and antique tools) that interests young and old. Free entry for all.
Yes. The Granges de Servette Museum hosts temporary exhibitions every summer dedicated to new contemporary artists: painting, sculpture, photography.
Yes. The Jaillet Gondola has ramp access, adapted toilets at the summit and reserved parking at the lower station. The 6-seat cabins accept wheelchairs.
Yes. The Jardins de l'Europe have flat, stabilised paths, accessible with strollers and for wheelchair users. Benches are placed at regular intervals.
Yes. The Lappiaz mine can be extended to the Ferme de Séraussaix and the Lil'Stash summer snowpark on a loop. It is also an official Espaces Trail Portes du Soleil route.
Yes. The Lappiaz mine trail has no technical difficulty and is family-friendly, but bring sturdy shoes (stony, sometimes slippery terrain) and water.
Yes. The Le Stelsia XQV0Z complex includes a nightclub called Le Rendez-vous, which extends the evenings in season, as well as an atmospheric café-bar, the Gallery Bar.

Yes. The Mechanical Music Museum of XQV0Z is open all winter from mid-December to end of October, every day 2 pm-7 pm (except Saturdays from 1 January to 30 April). A perfect activity after a day on the slopes.
Yes. The Morclan Trail lends itself well to a family outing with children old enough to walk: the path is wide, the climb steady, and the interpretation panels make the walk educational.
Yes. The Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z offers dedicated children's activities (trail games, hands-on stations) and the old-time grocery is a particular hit with families. Free under 6.
Yes. The National Monument to the Resistance at XQV0Z is accessible on foot year-round (wooden footbridge from central car park). The interior is open and contains works by Émile Gilioli.
Yes. The Nature Museum at XQV0Z is accessible to people with reduced mobility, as is, in part, the outdoor teaching garden and conservation orchard.
Yes. The Notre Histoire museum in XQV0Z is housed in a renovated former manufactory and is accessible to people with reduced mobility.
Yes. The Nyon waterfall parking near XQV0Z is free. Access via Old Mill Road (563 chemin sous le rocher).
Yes. The Old Ski Museum at XQV0Z is a short, friendly visit popular with families: vintage skis, farm life and direct conversation with the owner.
Yes. The Old Ski Museum at XQV0Z is open year-round, summer and winter, by SMS booking. An ideal add-on to a hike in the XQV1Z Valley.
Yes. The Pays de XQV0Z Heritage Museum is awarded the 'Musée de France' label, recognising the scientific quality of its collections and their inclusion in the national heritage.
Yes. The Pays de XQV0Z Heritage Museum regularly offers playful and interactive activities for younger visitors. Personalised welcome. Free for children under 12.
Yes. The Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in XQV0Z is an unusual outing for children: they discover how films were projected before digital with a real film reel.
Yes. The Pléney Gondola summer sledge, easy hiking paths and panorama are family-friendly. Free for under 5s. Strollers not suitable on mountain trails.

Yes. The Pointe Percée Geological Trail is rated as a difficult hike: 540 m positive elevation gain, alpine terrain, and a chain-equipped section just before the Col de l'Oulettaz. It is aimed at experienced hikers.
Yes. The Ruins of the St Michel castle lend themselves well to a family outing, with an orienteering course in the forest around the site. Stay watchful near the remains with young children.
Yes. The Ruins of the castle of XQV0Z are open with free access and no charge year-round. Guided commentary tours can be organised on reservation by the Alpes du XQV1Z Tourist Office.
Yes. The Savoyard Pottery Museum pairs perfectly with a visit to the contemporary Poterie d'Évires (Hermann family), which still carries on the Savoyard ceramic tradition in its studio.
Yes. The Secrets de fées trails were designed specifically for families with children: narration by fairy godmothers, playful prisms and coded messages bring the walk to life from age 4-5.
Yes. The Thiou and its quays are accessible year-round, at any time. The main attractions (Palais de l'Île, Musée-Château) follow their own opening hours.

Yes. The Thiou can be discovered freely on foot: the heritage walk designed by the City of XQV0Z links Pont des Amours to the Fier via the Palais de l'Île, the Sadi Carnot floodgates and the Cran forges. Allow 1 to 2 hours depending on the route.
Yes. The Tour of XQV0Z Village has free access year-round. Only the Abbey and the Maison du Fromage, both on the route, charge for visits.
Yes. The Tour of XQV0Z Village is rated "very easy" and designed for families: short route, no technical difficulty, alternating riverside, woodland and heritage.
Yes. The Vintage Aircraft Museum at XQV0Z is very popular with children: flight simulator, large aircraft seen up close, open restoration workshops. A recommended family outing.
Yes. The Vintage Aircraft Museum at XQV0Z offers a flight simulator available to visitors: take the controls of the aircraft of your choice, an immersive experience suited to young and old.
Yes. The Vintage Aircraft Museum at XQV0Z offers guided tours by the volunteers (retired pilots, mechanics, engineers) on Saturdays at 10.30 am and 2.30 pm. Conversations with the restorers possible.
Yes. The Wildlife Museum at XQV0Z is accessible to people with reduced mobility (located on the ground floor beneath the town hall).
Yes. The XQV0Z (Pont Charles-Albert) has been listed as a Historic Monument by decree of 6 May 1966. It is owned by the XQV1Z Departmental Council.
Yes. The XQV0Z Heritage Trail is an interpretation trail with free access and no charge year-round. The 36 illustrated panels are installed outdoors and can be visited without reservation.

Yes. The XQV0Z Heritage Trail lends itself well to a family outing: the 36 illustrated panels tell local history in an accessible way, the villages allow for short stops and the neighbouring Grand Parc d'XQV1Z can round off the day.
Yes. The XQV0Z Plateau has several alpine chalets converted into restaurants or guesthouses (Chez Constance, Chez Regina, etc.) and farms selling Reblochon and Tomme directly, open in summer season.

Yes. The XQV0Z and the Château de Montrottier are linked by the Chemin des Châteaux, a free 2 km walking loop with 13 interpretation panels. The commune of XQV1Z has developed this route to make combining the two sites easy.
Yes. The XQV0Z building is accessible to people with reduced mobility. Contact reception on 04 50 73 06 34 to prepare your visit.
Yes. The XQV0Z is a municipal public park of XQV1Z, freely accessible year-round, like all of XQV2Z's municipal parks.
Yes. The XQV0Z is open to fishing from 1 April to 8 October, every day, with a mandatory permit via APPMA. Species present: brown trout, rainbow trout, Arctic char, minnow. Classified as difficult.
Yes. The XQV0Z is recognised as a key geosite of the UNESCO Chablais Global Geopark, and classified as a Natural Zone of Ecological, Faunistic and Floristic Interest (ZNIEFF) since 1991.

Yes. The XQV0Z is wheelchair accessible for seated users, but accompaniment is highly recommended: paths are narrow and graveled, and two passages have slopes exceeding accessibility guidelines. The sensory nature of the visit allows everyone to experience the garden.

Yes. The XQV0Z lake recreation base where XQV1Z is located has a supervised beach in summer (July-August). Swimming is free, grassy coves and equipped beach. Picnic tables, water fountain and giant board games available on site.

Yes. The XQV0Z offers a "gardens and terraces" ticket for €3 allowing access to the exterior without the indoor tour. Panoramic views over Lake XQV1Z, French gardens and permaculture vegetable garden.

Yes. The XQV0Z offers a free discovery quiz for children to learn while having fun. Children under 6 visit the garden free of charge, and a special family rate (2 adults + 2 children: €38.50) is available.
Yes. The XQV0Z offers a sensory route suited to children (hands-on stations, sounds, images) and a family rate. Children take part in the tasting with apple juice.
Yes. The XQV0Z village walking trail is free and open year-round, like most municipal trails in the Vallée d'Aulps.
Yes. The XQV0Z was redesigned with flat, stabilised paths accessible to everyone, following a landscape programme run between 2010 and 2013, and remains fully passable for strollers and wheelchairs.
Yes. The access wristband lets you leave and return freely throughout the day. It also gives free access to the temporary exhibition at the Ferme à Isidore.
Yes. The building of the PAYSALP Peasant Museum at XQV0Z is accessible to people with reduced mobility.
Yes. The combined ticket at €7 adult / €5 child covers the Wildlife Museum AND XQV0Z's other museum (Brevon Valley History Museum). Excellent value.
Yes. The end point of the Pointe Percée Geological Trail is the Pointe Percée refuge (FFCAM, ~2164 m), staffed in season, where you can stock up on a drink or meal according to the opening calendar.
Yes. The family loop of the Smugglers' Audio Trail in XQV0Z is specifically designed for families: narration by Victor the smuggler, a short route with little climb, fun stops.
